words.def: 1. (LOC) At the space separating two objects or regions. 2. (LOC) In the period separating two points in time or on a scale.
1. Negates the meaning of the modified verb.

Productive Affix NOTE: Less commonly used than the similar suffix '-(i)ger'. Typically, this suffix is used to either convey a sense of 'bringing' or to better follow a poetic meter. 1. (Poetic) Forms adjectives or nouns describing things that carry or bring the original noun.

words.loc: Noun-to-Noun, Adjective-to-Noun, Country, Land, Place, Toponymic
words.def: Productive Affix 1. Used to form the name of a territory, country, or region. 2. Used to form a noun meaning an environment characteristic of the original word.
Productive Affix NOTE: More commonly used than the similar suffix '-(e)mnūs' 1. (Poetic) Forms adjectives or nouns describing things that bear or carry the original noun.
Productive 1. Used to form pejorative nouns denoting specific groups, communities, or ethnicities of people.
Unproductive Affix 1. (Archaic) Used to form the name of a territory, country, or region.
Productive Affix 1. 1. Forms a transitive verb out of a noun.
Unproductive Affix 1. Forms nouns meaning the original noun and another noun associated with it as a complete pair.
Unproductive Affix 1. Converts nouns into adjectives that resemble or have some of the characteristics of the original noun.
Productive Affix 1. Converts toponyms into nouns and/or adjectives relating to things and characteristics of said toponym, such as the people and the language spoken by these people.
Semi-Productive Affix 1. Forms an inanimate irregular N-stem noun meaning the location where the original verb or noun o
Unproductive Affix 1. Forms an inanimate noun meaning the entirety of the original noun.
Unproductive Affix 1. Forms nouns which are a part of (typically the smallest element of) the original noun. (Forms animate nouns declined as Final Vowel-Stems)
Productive Affix 1. Converts count nouns into mass nouns.
Productive Affix 1. Expressing a negative or disrespectful connotation towards the original noun or adjective.
Unproductive Affix 1. Forms adverbs out of verbs
Productive Affix 1. Forms adjectives describing nouns with a tendency or inclination to the action of the original verb.
Unproductive Affix 1. Converts nouns into adjectives that describe something as being made of the original noun.
Productive Affix 1. To become, turn, grow. (e.g, evening -> to become evening)
Unproductive Affix 1. Suffix denoting an activity, practice, or ritual, or gift.
Productive Affix 1. Forms adverbs out of adjectives.
Productive Affix 1. Forms a transitive verb out of a noun. (Typically with a causative meaning)
Productive Affix 1. Used to form nouns meaning the result of the original verb.
Productive Affix 1. Suffix that weakens/diminishes the idea of the original word.
Productive Affix 1. Forms a noun meaning someone or something that performs the action of the original verb.
Productive Affix 1. Converts nouns into nouns meaning the child of, descendant of, or creation of the original noun.
Productive Affix 1. Forms an abstract noun from the original verb.
Productive Affix 1. Forms a noun meaning a maker or performer of the original noun. 2. Forms a noun meaning a tool or instrument used to perform the original verb.
Productive Affix 1. Forms adjectives describing nouns characteristic of the original verb.

Productive NOTE: This derivational affix, due to the frequency of phonological changes associated with it, has been reanalyzed as '-rim' though the original form was '-sim'. Some older words may still adhere to the original form '-sim'. 1. Used to form nouns denoting specific groups, communities, or ethnicities of people.
Unproductive Affix 1. Forms a noun meaning a maker or builder of the original noun.
Productive Affix 1. Forms abstract nouns, sometimes denoting offices and groups.
Mostly Unproductive Affix 1. Forms nouns referring to the state, property, or quality of the original adjective.
Productive Affix 1. Converts nouns into adjectives that are of or pertaining to the original noun. (Declined as irregular N-Stems)
Productive Affix 1. Forms adjectives meaning able to be acted upon in the manner denoted by the original verb.

Abat, Abath, Abed, Abeth (an.) Abed, Abdas, Abdae, Abdath (inan.) 1. Lacking moisture or liquid. 2. Dried out by heat. 3. (Of plants) Dry and shriveled. 4. (Of a climate or land) Having little or no rain; Too dry or barren to support vegetation.
Abdāsh, Abdāshae, Abdāshath (inan.) 1. A prolonged period of abornmally low rainfall or of abnormally low access to water. 2. The state of being dry or arid.
Adar, Adrae, Adrath (inan.) 1. The red liquid that circulates within the arteries and veins of humans and other animals. 2. Blood that has been shed.

words.loc: Smell, Scent, Odor, Aroma, Fragrance, Stench, Stink, Reek
words.def: Adnil, Adnilae, Adnilath (inan.) 1. A quality in something that is perceived by the faculty of smell.
Adrauk, Adraukh, Adrauk, Adrauketh (an.) Adrauk, Adraukas, Adraukae, Adraukath (inan.) 1. Causing pain or suffering to others, or feeling no concern about it. 2. Showing no mercy or pity.
1. In a way that causes pain or suffering; in a cruel manner.
Adrebu, Adrebūs, Adrebu, Adrebūth (an.) 1. A droplet of blood.

Adriger, Adrigros, Adriger, Adrigeroth (an.) Adriger, Adrigras, Adrigrae, Adrigrath (inan.) 1. Stained, spotted, or otherwise discolored with blood. 2. Responsible for the deaths of others; Guilty of murder. 3. (By extension) Responsible for a specified wrongdoing.
Aebo, Aebae, Aebōth (inan.) 1. A colored substance which is spread over a surface and dries to leave a thin decorative or protective coating.

Ābo, Ābōt, Ābōkūs, Aebo, Aebo, Aebōned 1. To cover the surface of something with paint, as decoration or protection. 2. To mark a person or a part of the body with an indelible design by inserting pigment into punctures in the skin.
Aebōl, Aebōlae, Aebōlath (inan.) 1. A mark made by tattooing.
Āg, Āget, Āgūs, Aeg, Aego, Aegened 1. To mend a thing suffering from damage or a fault.

words.loc: Besiege, Invest, Lay Siege To, Put to Siege, Beleaguer, Blockade, Surround
words.def: Āgur, Āgurit, Āgurgūs, Aegur, Aeguro, Aegurined 1. To surround a place with armed forces in order to capture it or force its surrender.

words.loc: Wall, City Wall, Stone Wall, Rampart, Fence, Palisade, Stockade, Fortification
words.def: Aegus, Aegurae, Aegurath (inan.) NOTE: Aegus "wall, fence" is used in reference to defensive walls such as those of cities and forts. See termīkar "wall, partition" for a word meaning the walls of a house or room. 1. A continuous vertical brick or stone structure that encloses or divides an area of land, especially if surrounding a city or town. 2. A fence of wooden stakes or iron railings fixed in the ground, forming an enclosure or defense.

Agos, Agosh, Ager, Agreth (inan.) 1. The part of the earth's surface that is not covered by water. 2. An expanse of land or area of ground, especially in terms of ownership or use. 3. A country. 4. An identifiable area or division of land, typically with definable characteristics.
Aker, Akras, Akrae, Akrath (an.) 1. A small deer which lacks a visible tail and has a reddish summer coat that turns grayish in winter.
Akhneg, Akhneges, Akhneg, Akhnegeth (an.) 1. A badly behaviored and mischevious child. 2. A small, mischevious devil or sprite.
Akhnāsh, Akhnāshae, Akhnāshath (inan.) 1. The state of being a child or young.
Akhon, Akhōs, Akhen, Akhoneth (an.) 1. A young human below the age of puberty or below the legal age of majority, which is 16 in most kadres.

words.loc: Rise, Stand, Go Up, Get Up, Ascend, Soar, Fly Up
words.def: Ālder, Āldrikūs, Āldre, Alder, Aldro, Aldrined NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Intransitive) To get up from lying, sitting, or kneeling. 2. (Intransitive) To move from a lower position to a higher position. 3. (Reflexive) To have or maintain an upright position supported by one's feet.

words.loc: High, Tall, Deep, Low, Low-Pitched, Upper
words.def: Aldor, Aldres, Alder, Aldreth (an.) Alder, Aldras, Aldrae, Aldrath (inan.) 1. Of great vertical extent. 2. Of great or of above-average height, especially (with reference to an object) when related to width. 3. Extending or positioned far below the top or surface. 4. (Of sounds) Low in pitch. 5. (In the comparative) Situated above another part. 6. (In the comparative) Situated on higher ground or away from the sea.
Aldoran, Aldorās, Aldoren, Aldoraneth (an.) 1. The time of day when the Sun seems to reach its highest point in the sky.

Aldrāsh, Aldrāshae, Aldrāshath (inan.) 1. The distance from the bottom to the top of something. 2. The distance of something above the ground or some other chosen level. 3. (Figurative) A high point or location, especially when referring to a tall mountain. 4. The degree to which something is deep. 5. The distance of something below the surface or some other chosen level.
1. In or towards the sky or a high place. 2. At depth. 3. Far down physically into or though something.
Alum, Alūs, Alem, Alumeth (an.) Alum, Almas, Almae, Almath (inan.) 1. Denoting a person or thing that is different or distinct from one already mentioned or known about.
1. (Interrogative) In what manner or by what means. 2. (Interrogative) In what state or condition.

Amar, Anrae, Anrath (inan.) 1. The woody part of a tree arising from the trunk and usually dividing. 2. A weapon for shooting arrows, typically made of a curved piece of wood whose ends are joined by a taut string.
Amban, Ambanae, Ambanath (inan.) 1. An outdoor area containing one or more types of plants grown chiefly for ornamental purposes.
1. (GEN) Not having the use or benefit of. 2. (COM) In the absence of.
An, Anes, Ane, Aneth (an.) 1. A reproductive structure in flowering plants that is often brightly colored and has sepals and petals. 2. (Figurative) An element of decoration.

words.loc: Even, Also, And Also, As Well As, And Even
words.def: 1. Used to emphasize something shocking, unexpected, or extreme. 2. Used to emphasize a comparative. 3. (Coordinator; Postpositive) Used to coordinate an additional conjunct that is unexpected, surprising, or warrants emphasis.
Anash, Anash, Anesh, Anasheth (an.) Anesh, Anshas, Anshae, Anshath (inan.) 1. Shaped like or approximately like a circle or sphere.

words.loc: Bloom, Blossom, Flower, Sprout, Germinate, Foam, Froth, Fizz, Bubble
words.def: Āne, Ānēt, Ānēkūs, Ane, Ano, Anēned 1. (Of plants) To open one's or produce flowers. 2. (Of plants) To grow from a seed. 3. (Of wine) To form or contain a rising or overflowing mass of bubbles.

words.loc: Circle, Go Around, Surround, Encircle, Encompass, Enclose, Wall, Fence
words.def: Ānesh, Ānshit, Ānshikūs, Anesh, Ansho, Anshined 1. To move all the way around something. 2. To form a circle around or surround something.

Ānor, Ānret, Ānorgūs, Anor, Anro, Norened 1. To be without or deficient in. 2. (When used with an existential particle; Can only be used with negative existential predicate constructions) To not exist.
Anril, Anrila, Anrilāth (inan.) 1. A thin piece of wood that has fallen or been cut from a tree. 2. A slender, woody shoot growing from a branch or stem of a tree or shrub.
Anīn, Anīs, Anīn, Anīneth (an.) Anīn, Anīnas, Anīn, Anīnath (inan.) 1. Relating to, resembling, full of, or smelling of flowers.

words.loc: Pink, Rose, Salmon, Coral, Rosy, White, Fair
words.def: Anīn Rhūs, Anīs Rhūsh, Anīn Rhūr, Anīn Rhūseth (an.) Anīn Rhūr, Anīnas Rhūras, Anīnae Rhūrae, Anīnath Rhūrath (inan.) NOTE: Considered a shade of rhūs 'red'. 1. Of a color intermediate between red and white, as of carnations or salmon. 2. (Of people or skin) Pale, white, or pinkish in color.

words.loc: Crawl, Creep, Slink, Slither, Spread, Expand, Grow
words.def: Āpoph, Āpophet, Āpophkūs, Apoph, Apopho, Apophened 1. (Of a person) To move forward on the hands and knees or by dragging the body close to the ground. 2. (Of an animal) To move slowly with the abdomen close to the ground. 3. (Of snakes) To move smoothly with a twisting motion. 4. (Of a thing or situation) To move very slowly or imperceptibly at a steady pace. 5. (Of fire) To take up a larger space or area.
Productive Affix 1. Used to create a masculine form of the original word.
Arbezeph, Arbezeph, Arbezeph, Arbezpheth (an.) 1. A large bird of prey with a large hooked beak and long broad wings.
Arbērus, Arbērae, Arbērath (inan.) 1. A very large meal, often of a ceremonial or celebratory nature.

words.loc: Captain, Commander, Skipper, Lord, King, Chief, Master, Ruler, Leader
words.def: Arbēs, Arbēsh, Arbēr, Arbēseth (an.) 1. The person in command of a ship or other vessel. 2. (Literary; Poetic) Someone or something having power, authority, or influence; a person exercising dominion.
Ārin, Ārnet, Ārihūs, Arin, Arno, Arnened 1. (Intransitive) To emit an odor or scent of a specified kind. 2. To perceive or detect the odor or scent of something.

words.loc: In The Presence Of, Before, Known To, Known By
words.def: 1. (COM) In the presence of. 2. (COM) In the knowledge or awareness of.

words.loc: In Person, Personally, Face-to-Face, Publicly, Openly
words.def: 1. With one's own physical presence. 2. In an open and public manner.
Arkhon, Arkhōs, Arkhen, Arkhoneth (an.) 1. A male child.
Arkhonauk, Arkhonaukh, Arkhonauk, Arkhonauketh (an.) Arkhonauk, Arkhonaukas, Arhonaukae, Arkhonaukath (inan.) 1. Having or showing an emotional or intellectual development appropriate to a child.
1. In an unwise or stupid manner. 2. In a childish or immature manner.

words.loc: Lie Beside, Lie Next To, Be Adjacent To, Be Continuous With, Border, Adjoin, Neighbor
words.def: Ārthambrae, Ārthambraet, Ārthambraekūs, Arthambrae, Arthambro, Arthambraened 1. To be situated or placed next to. 2. To be next to and joined with.
Aruk, Arukae, Arukath (inan.) 1. Pots, dishes, and other articles made of earthenware or baked clay.

At, Ath, At, Ateth (an.) 1. A stiff, sticky fine-grained earth, typically yellow, red, or bluish-gray in color and often forming an impermeable layer in the soil. It can be molded when wet, and is dried and baked to make bricks, pottery, and ceramics. 2. The substance that makes up the land surface. 3. (Philosophy, Alchemy) One of the six elements by which the Patrin categorize the world's substances.

words.loc: Muddy, Muddied, Swampy, Boggy, Marshy, Mucky
words.def: Atauk, Ataukh, Atauk, Atauketh (an.) Atauk, Ataukas, Ataukae, Ataukath (inan.) 1. Covered in or full of mud. 2. (Of the ground) Too wet and muddy to be easily walked on
Athān, Athānae, Athānath (inan.) 1. A person who makes pottery.
Audril, Audrilae, Audrilath (inan.) 1. A gap permitting passage, access, or visibility through.
Aukauk, Aukaukh, Aukauk, Aukauketh (an.) Aukauk, Aukaukas, Aukaukae, Aukaukath (inan.) 1. Having a rounded and slightly elongated shape or outline like that of an egg.

words.loc: Egg, Ovum, Nit, Roe, Spawn, Seed, Grain, Beginning, Start, Origin, Genesis, Germ, Offspring, Progeny, Descendant
words.def: Auku, Aukūs, Auku, Aukūth (an.) 1. An oval or round object laid by a female animal, usually containing a developing embryo. 2. A flowering plant's unit of reproduction, capable of developing into another such plant. 3. The harvested seeds of various grass food crops. 4. (Figurative) The origin or earliest version of an idea or project. 5. (Poetic) Any of a person's descendants, including of further generations.
Aukūban, Aukūbnae, Aukūbnath (inan.) 1. A female reproductive organ, often paired, that produces egg cells in animals.
Aukūbar, Aukūbrae, Aukūbrath (inan.) 1. A group of eggs, prior to hatching, fertilized at the same time and laid in a single session.
Aukūger, Aukūgros, Aukūger, Aukūgroth (an.) Aukūger, Aukūgras, Aukūgrae, Aukūgrath (inan.) 1. (Of a person, animal, or plant) Able to conceive young or produce seed.
Aulos, Aulosh, Aulor, Auloseth (an.) 1. The star at the center of the solar system which Nīmlad is in. 2. The light or warmth received from the Sun.

Aulozek, Aulozekae, Aulozekath (inan.) 1. The wind that originates in the east and blows in a westward direction. 2. The direction toward the point of the horizon where the sun rises. 3. The eastern part of the world or of a specified country, region, or city.

Aulozekīn, Aulozekīs, Aulozekīn, Aulozekīneth (an.) Aulozekīn, Aulozekīnas, Aulozekīnae, Aulozekīnath (inan.) 1. Situated in the west or directed toward or facing the west. 2. Living in or originating from the west.

words.loc: Din, Clamor, Cacophony, Uproar, Racket, Shouting, Yelling, Roar, Warcry
words.def: Aur, Aurae, Aurath (inan.) 1. A loud noise, often confused, unpleasant, and/or prologned. 2. Loud and continued shouting or exclamation. 3. A call made to rally soldiers for battle.

Aurael, Auraelae, Auraelath (inan.) 1. A large bird with mostly glossy black plumage, a heavy bill, and a raucous voice. 2. A large diving bird with a long neck, long and hooked bill, short legs, and mainly dark plumage; typically breeds on coastal cliffs and is noted for its voracious appetite.

words.loc: Shout, Yell, Cry out, Clamor, Exclaim
words.def: Āre, Ārēt, Ārēkūs, Aure, Auro, Aurēned 1. To utter a loud call or cry.
Aurēl, Aurēlae, Aurēlath (inan.) 1. A loud call or cry, typically unpleasant-sounding.

words.loc: Open, About to be, Prospective Aspect
words.def: Āth, Āthkūs, Ādre, Auth, Audro, Audrened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To make something accessible or allow for passage, access, or visibility by moving from a shut position. 2. To make something accessible or allow for passage, access, or visibilty by clearing away an obstacle or obstacles. 3. (Auxiliary) Denotes that the action expressed by the lexical verb is imminent or will happen very soon.
Auth, Authae, Authath (inan.) 1. Vegetation consisting of typically short plants with long, narrow leaves. 2. Grass and the surface layer of earth held together by its roots.
Az, Az, Azoth (inan.) 1. (Numeral) One.

words.loc: Many, Much, A Lot, Enough, Sufficient, Long
words.def: Azor, Azores, Azor, Azoreth (an.) Azor, Azoras, Azorae, Azorath (inan.) NOTE: When azor "many, much" is combined with another adjective, it is usually joined to the adjective with īm "and". NOTE: See 'zhīr' for the comparative and superlative grades. 1. A large amount of. 2. As many or as much as required. 3. (Of time) Great in duration.

Azru, Azrae, Azrūth (inan.) NOTE: This noun takes the allative case. 1. The expanse of salt water that covers most of Nīmlad's surface and surrounds its landmasses. 2. A roughly definable area of the sea. 3. A large lake.
Azrīn, Azrīs, Azrīn, Azrīneth (an.) Azrīn, Azrīnas, Azrīnae, Azrīnath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to the sea.

Azrūgos, Azrūgosh, Azrūger, Azrūgreth (inan.) 1. A region of the sea directly controlled or claimed by a Kadar. (Can be used to refer to the waters of other prominent city-states but it very uncommon)
1. (Adverbial Allative) In the direction of or toward the sea.
Azīm, Azīmed, Azīm, Azīmeth (an.) 1. An organ by which animals see.

words.loc: Watchtower, Lookout, Guard Tower, Observation Post
words.def: Azīmbash, Azīmbashae, Azīmbashath (inan.) 1. A tall building from which to keep watch for danger or trouble.
1. On one occasion or for one time only.
Baes, Bēs, Baethen (an.) Baes, Baes, Baethen (inan.) NOTE: This adjective is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. Relatively small in number.
Balakh, Balkhes, Balekh, Balkheth (an.) 1. A general term for any sea-borne vessels larger than a canoe or skiff.
Balkhebar, Balkhebrae, Balkhebrath (inan.) 1. A group of naval vessels.
Balkhetān, Balkhetānae, Balkhetānath (inan.) 1. A person who designs and constructs ships.
Banek, Bahae, Bahath (inan.) 1. (Of a person) A dead body.
Bangeph, Bangepesh, Bangeper, Bangepheth (an.) 1. A large bird of prey with the head and neck more or less bare of feathers, feeding chiefly on carrion.

Bebar, Bebarkūs, Bebre, Bar, Baro, Barened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Of animals) To take food into the body. 2. (Vulgar; Of people) To eat in a crude manner.
Barbor, Barbres, Barber, Barboreth (an.) Barber, Barbras, Barbrae, Barbrath (inan.) 1. Wanting or devouring large quantities of food.

words.loc: Devourer, Eater, Consumer, Destroyer, Annihilator, Obliterator, Eradicator
words.def: Barkar, Barkres, Barker, Barkreth (an.) 1. A person or thing that eats greedily or voraciously. 2. A person or thing that completely destroys something.
Barnūs, Barnumesh, Barnūmer, Barnūseth (an.) 1. Food that is prepared and eaten, usually at a specific time, and usually in a comparatively large quantity.

words.loc: Edible, Consumable, Digestible, Esculent, Potable, Drinkable
words.def: Barīn, Barīs, Barīn, Barīneth (an.) Barīn, Barīnas, Barīnae, Barīnath (inan.) 1. Fit or suitable to be eaten. 2. Safe to drink.
Bash, Bashae, Bashath (inan.) 1. A tall, narrow building, either freestanding or forming part of a building such as a temple or castle.
Bebeg, Bebgat, Bebgūs, Bek, Beko, Bekned 1. To like (one thing or person) better than another or others.
Ben, Benae, Benath (inan.) 1. A very young male child, especially one newly or recently born.
Bilka, Bilkae, Bilkāth (inan.) 1. A living organism that typically grows in a permanent site and gathers nutrients via photosynthesis and its roots.
Bodnauk, Bodnaukh, Bodnauk, Bodnauketh (an.) Bodnauk, Bodnaukas, Bodnaukae, Bodnaukath (inan.) 1. Covered with hair, especially thick or long hair. 2. Covered with fur.

words.loc: Fur, Coat, Pelt, Pelage, Body Hair, Pubic Hair
words.def: Boron, Bodnae, Bodnath (inan.) 1. The short, fine, soft hair of an animal. 2. (Of a person) Hair that grows on the body, excluding the head and face.

words.loc: Emerge, Surface, Rise, Ascend, Exit, Come Out
words.def: Bebul, Beblet, Beble, Bul, Bulo, Bulened 1. (Intransitive) To come out of a body of water or the ground. 2. (Of heavenly bodies) To appear to move away from and above the horizon.

words.loc: Souls of the Dead, The Dead, Soul, Spirit
words.def: Bābar, Bābrae, Bābrath (inan.) 1. Those who have died: dead people. 2. The spiritual or immaterial part of a specific living being.

words.loc: At That Time, At The Time, Back Then
words.def: 1. (Idiomatic; lit. in his season) During a specific time in the past.

words.loc: Sing, Intone, Chant, Sound, Resound, Play, Chirp, Tweet, Twitter, Warble, Trill, Hoot
words.def: Bebēl, Bebēlet, Bebēlgūs, Bēl, Bēlo, Bēlened 1. To produce musical or harmonious sounds with one's voice. 2. To perform a vocal part in a musical composition. 3. (Of musical instruments) To produce a sound. 4. (Of small birds, owls, or insects) To make a chirping or hooting noise.

words.loc: Spell, Cantrip, Enchantment, Incantation, Charm, Curse, Ritual, Rite
words.def: Bēlis, Bēlish, Bēlir, Bēliseth (an.) 1. A magical effect or influence induced by magic. 2. A magical or religious ceremony or spell that follows a specific set of actions, words, and behaviors.
Bēlkar, Bēlkres, Bēlker, Bēlkreth (an.) 1. A person who sings, especially professionally. 2. A bird with a musical song.

Bībauk, Bībaukh, Bībauk, Bībauketh (an.) Bībauk, Bībaukas, Bībaukae, Bībaukath (inan.) 1. Relating to the Bibunic region, the peoples within it, or their language. 2. (Substantive Animate) The Bibunic language.

words.loc: Red Deer, Bibunic Antelope, Stag, Hart, Doe, Hind
words.def: Bībauk Lamon, Bībaukh Lamōs, Bībauk Lamon, Bībauketh Lamoneth (an.) 1. A hoofed grazing or browsing animal native to Rhusagos with branching bony antlers that are shed annually and typically borne only by the male.
Bību, Bībae, Bībūth (inan.) 1. An underground conduit for carrying off drainage water and waste matter.
Bībūnor, Bībūnorae, Bībūnorath (inan.) 1. A region along the southern coastline of Rhusagos, wherein live the Bibunic peoples. (Literally meaning 'Gutter Land')
Bīl, Bīlae, Bīlath (inan.) 1. The smallest unit of language that has a particular meaning and can be expressed by itself.
Bīlael, Bīlaeles, Bīlael, Bīlaeleth (an.) Bīlael, Bīlaelas, Bīlaelae, Bīlaelath (inan.) 1. Using or expressed in more words than are needed.

words.loc: Proverb, Saying, Adage, Maxim, Axiom, Proposition, Postulate
words.def: Bīlob, Bīlbae, Bīlbath (inan.) 1. A short saying in general use, stating a general truth or piece of advice. 2. (Of philosophy) A statement or proposition which is regarded as being established, accepted, or self-evidently true. 3. (Of logic, mathematics, etc. ) A fundamental assumption that is accepted as being true without proof and is used as a basis for theorems.

words.loc: Bright, Shining, Light, Clear, Understandable, Comprehensible, Intelligible, Lucid
words.def: Bōl, Bōles, Bōle, Bōleth (an.) Bōl, Bōlas, Bōlae, Bōlath (inan.) 1. Giving out or reflecting a lot of light. 2. Easy to perceive, understand, or interpret.

Bōlus, Bōlrae, Bōlrath (inan.) 1. An open or hidden space in a forest, especially if lush or beautiful. 2. A bright surface or patch of ice, snow, or water. 3. A bright patch of sky or a bright space between clouds.

words.loc: Stop, End, Halt, Finish, Cessative Aspect
words.def: Bebūz, Bebūzit, Bebūzgūs, Būz, Būzo, Būzined 1. To cease moving. 2. To not continue. 3. To come to a final point. 4. (Auxiliary) Denotes that the action expressed by the lexical verb is ending or has just ended.
Būzīl, Būzīlae, Būzīlath (inan.) 1. The final part of something, especially a period of time, an activity, or a story.
Dedbat, Dedbath, Dedbatkūs, Dabat, Dabdo, Dabdened 1. (Intransitive) To become dry. 3. (Transitive) To cause to become dry.
Dagbesh, Dagbeshae, Dagbeshath (inan.) 1. The act of destroying. 2. The results of a destructive event. 3. (Figurative) The act of putting to death or being put to death as a punishment.
Dagbor, Dagbres, Dagber, Dagboreth (an.) Dagber, Dagbras, Dagbrae, Dagbrath (inan.) 1. Causing or tending to cause great and irreparable harm or damage.

words.loc: Kill, Murder, Slay, Thwart, Check, Foil, Curb, Prevent, Hinder, Frustrate, Stop
words.def: Derak, Derakūs, Derke, Dak, Dako, Dakened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To cause to die. 2. (Figurative) To cause to fail or to otherwise prevent.
Dakar, Dakres, Daker, Dakreth (an.) 1. One that kills. 2. A person who commits or has committed murder.
Dakargo, Dakargae, Dakargōth (inan.) 1. A small flowerless green plant that lacks true roots, growing in damp habitats and reproducing by means of spores released from stalked capsules.

words.loc: Kill Openly, Kill Outright, Slay Openly
words.def: Derkob, Deragbet, Derkobgūs, Dakob, Dagbo, Dakobned 1. To kill someone in a bold and shameless way, especially when in public and immediately.

words.loc: Raise, Lift, Pick Up, Elevate, Build, Construct, Erect
words.def: Dralder, Draldrikūs, Draldre, Dalder, Daldro, Daldrined NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To cause something to rise. 2. To move something up to a higher position or level. 3. (Of buildings, towers, walls, or other upright structures) To build.
Dargis, Dargish, Dargir, Dargiseth 1. A slender, long-legged fly with aquatic larvae, the females of which bite humans and animals and suck blood.

words.loc: Skin, Epidermis, Hide, Pelt, Bark, Rind, Crust
words.def: Do, Dae, Dōth (inan.) 1. The thin layer of tissue forming then natural outer covering of the body of a person or animal. 2. The tough protective outer sheath of the trunk and branches of a tree or woody shrub. 3. The tough outer layer of fruits, cheeses, and bread.
Productive Affix 1. Forms an intransitive verb from a noun. 2. Forms a causative verb from a verb.

words.loc: Feed, Nourish, Nurse, Suckle, Breastfeed, Pasture
words.def: Dedbar, Dedbarkūs, Dedbre, Dobar, Dobro, Dobrened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To give food to. 2. To provide with the food or other substances necessary for growth, health, and good condition. 3. To feed a baby from the breast or teat. 4. To move animals into a pasture.

words.loc: Shine, Gleam, Glare, Glint, Flash, Flare, Blaze
words.def: Dedbol, Dedbolet, Dedbolkūs, Dobel, Doblo, Doblened 1. (Intransitive) To shine brightly in a brief, sudden, or intermittent way.

words.loc: Lightning, Lightning Bolt, Thunderbolt, Distant Lightning, Cold Lightning
words.def: Doblekar, Doblekres, Dobleker, Doblekreth (an.) NOTE: This word specifically connotes lightning that is far away and has no audible thunder. See mūres "hot lightning" for a word referring to nearby, audible lightning. 1. A flash of light produced by a short-duration discharge of electricity within a cloud or between a cloud and the earth, especially when not accompanied by thunder or observed from far-off.

words.loc: Harmful, Hurtful, Damaging, Injurous, Detrimental, Deleterious, Toxic, Poisonous, Venomous, Noxious
words.def: Dodrakos, Dodrakosh, Dodragor, Dodrakoseth (an.) Dodragor, Dodragras, Dodragrae, Dodragrath (inan.) 1. Causing or likely to cause damage or harm. 2. Causing or capable of causing death or illness if taken into the body.

words.loc: Rescue, Save, Extricate, Deliver From, Revive, Resuscitate, Bring Back
words.def: Drogme, Drogmēt, Drogmēkūs, Dogme, Dogmo, Dogmēned 1. To save from a dangerous or distressing situation. 2. To restore to life or consciousness.

words.loc: Put, Set, Place, Lay, Prop, Lean, Position
words.def: Dokhab, Dokhbet, Dokhabgūs, Dokeb, Dogbo, Dogbened 1. To put, lay, or stand something in a specified place or position.

words.loc: Set on Fire, Ignite, Kindle, Stir up, Incite, Instigate, Foment, Arouse, Attract, Turn on
words.def: Dermeb, Dermebut, Dednapkūs, Domeb, Dombo, Dombuned 1. To cause to be on fire. 2. To cause or provoke bad behaviors. (Typically of a violent or illegal nature) 3. (Slang) To excite sexually.

words.loc: Wash in Warm Water, Bathe in Warm Water
words.def: Dednē, Dedneket, Dednēkūs, Donē, Donko, Dongened 1. To wash or bathe in warm or hot water.

Drōph, Drōphet, Drōphkūs, Dōph, Dōpho, Dōphened 1. (Especially of a liquid) To be turned to ice or become solid as a result of extreme cold. 2. (Transitive) To lower something's temperature to the point that it freezes or becomes hard.

words.loc: Lower, Drop, Reduce, Shrink, Lessen, Shorten, Decrease, Diminish, Minimize
words.def: Drophae, Drophaet, Drophaekūs, Dophae, Dopho, Dophaened 1. To move someone or something in a downward direction. 2. (Transitive) To make smaller in amount, intensity, or degree.
Dedphel, Dedphelēt, Dedphelgūs, Dophel, Dophlo, Dophlened 1. (Typically used in military contexts) To bring or fetch water for drinking.

words.loc: Black, Dull Black, Sable, Jet, Pitch, Dark
words.def: Dor, Doras, Dorae, Dorath (an.) Dor, Dorās, Dora, Dorāth (inan.) 1. Of the very darkest color owing to the absence of or complete absorption of light, as of tar or the night sky. 2. (Poetic) Dull black (as opposed to rabash, shining black) of color.

Dor, Dores, Dor, Doreth (an.) Dor, Doras, Dorae, Dorath (inan.) NOTE: Can be declined in the dual number. 1. The entity or entities indicated or understood from context, especially if more remote in some way than one designated "this".
Dedrae, Dedraet, Dedraekūs, Dorae, Dorae, Doraened 1. To color or dye something black for aesthetic or cosmetic purposes.

words.loc: Harm, Hurt, Injure, Wound, Damage, Impair
words.def: Dodrak, Dodraket, Dodrakūs, Dorak, Dorget, Dorakned 1. To cause physical pain or injury to. 2. To cause physcial harm to something such that its value, usefulness, or normal function is impaired.

words.loc: Beat, Bang, Hit, Strike, Pound, Thump
words.def: Droren, Drornet, Drornekūs, Doren, Dorno, Dorened 1. To strike repeatedly so as to make a noise.
Dorgil, Dorgilae, Dorgilath (inan.) 1. An instance of being physically injured. 2. Physical harm caused to something in such a way that its value, usefulness, or normal function is impaired.

Dornephek, Dornephkae, Dornephkath (inan.) 1. A percussion instrument sounded by being struck with sticks or the hands, typically cylindrical, barrel-shaped, or bowl-shaped, with a taut membrane over one or both ends.
Dornūs, Dornumesh, Dornumer, Dornūseth (an.) 1. A large toothed sea mammal with distinctive black-and-white markings and a prominent dorsal fin.

words.loc: Grow, Increase, Expand, Cultivate, Raise, Rear, Parent, Nurture, Bring Up
words.def: Dodeg, Dodgit, Dodgūs, Dotek, Dotkit, Dotkined 1. (Transitive) To cause or allow something to become bigger, especially if of plants. 2. To bring up and care for a child until they are fully grown, especially in a particular manner or place.

words.loc: Anger, Enrage, Infuriate, Incense, Irk, Provoke, Aggravate, Vex, Gall
words.def: Drozhon, Drozhonet, Drozhohūs, Dozhon, Dozhono, Dozhoned 1. To fill someone or something with anger.

Derzhēs, Derzhēret, Derzhēskūs, Dozhēs, Dozhēro, Dozhērened 1. (Intransitive) To see imaginary events in one's mind while sleeping. 2. To have a spontaneous series of thoughts while awake not connected to immediate reality.

Drakh, Drakhae, Drakhath (inan.) NOTE: This noun takes the allative case. 1. The wind that originates in the north and blows in a southward direction. 2. The direction toward the point of the horizon left of where the sun rises. 3. The northern part of the world or of a specified country, region, or city.

words.loc: Lose One's Mind, Lose One's Marbles, Go Insane, Go Over to the Dark Side
words.def: 1. (Idiomatic; lit. to go into the north) To go insane, especially if in an evil way or as a result of a tragedy.

Drakhīn, Drakhīs, Drakhīn, Drakhīneth (an.) Drrakhīn, Drakhīnas, Drakhīnae, Drakhīneth (inan.) 1. Situated in the south or directed toward or facing the north. 2. Living in or originating from the north.
Drodon, Drodōs, Droden, Drodneth (an.) 1. A typically aquatic blood-sucking worm. 2. (Derogatory; Figurative) A person who can use magic.
Drāg, Drāgae, Drāgath (inan.) 1. A wild, carnivorous mammal of the dog family that lives and hunts in packs. 2. A dog used or trained for hunting purposes.
Drāgaur, Drāgaurae, Drāgaurath (inan.) 1. A member of the race of werewolves, which are accounted as Younger Children. 2. The race of werewolves as a whole.
Drāgis, Drāgish, Drāgir, Drāgiseth (an.) 1. A fast-flying, long-bodied predatory insect with two pairs of large, transparent wings which are spread out sideways at rest.
Drāgīn, Drāgīs, Drāgīn, Drāgīneth (an.) Drāgīn, Drāgīnas, Drāgīnae, Drāgīnath (inan.) 1. Pertaining to or resembling a wolf.

words.loc: Mad, Insane, Crazy, Deranged, Psychotic, Frenzied
words.def: Drūmor, Drūmores, Drūmor, Drūmoreth (an.) Drūmor, Drūmoras, Drūmorae, Drūmorath (inan.) 1. In a state of mind which prevents normal perception, behavior, or social interaction.

Drūs, Drūsh, Drūr, Drūseth (an.) 1. (Substantive Animate) The element of a person that enables them to be aware of the world and their experiences, to think, and to feel; the faculty of consciousness and thought.

words.loc: Conceive, Become Pregnant, Task, Charge, Entrust, Load, Fill, Pack, Stuff, Burden, Lade
words.def: Drudes, Drudsit, Drudesgūs, Dudes, Dudso, Dudsined 1. (Intransitive) To become pregnant with a child. 2. (Transitive) To assign the responsibility for doing something to someone. 3. (Transitive) To put a load or large amount of something on or in.
Dudsined, Dudsindis, Dudsindi, Dudsindith (an.) Dudsined, Dudsindas, Dudsindae, Dudsindath (inan.) 1. Carrying developing offspring within the body.
Dudsāsh, Dudsāshae, Dudsāshath (inan.) 1. The downwards force an object experiences due to gravity.

words.loc: Cargo, Freight, Load, Haul, Baggage, Goods, Merchandise
words.def: Dudsīl, Dudsīlae, Dudsīlath (an.) 1. Goods carried on a ship or other vehicle.
Dukhar, Dukhres, Dukher, Dukhreth (an.) 1. A capacity to do something well, especially if acquired or learned as opposed to innate.

words.loc: About, Concerning, Of, Because Of, On Account Of, Due To
words.def: 1. (LOC) On the subject of. 2. (COM) By reason of.

words.loc: Heavy, Hefty, Weighty, Big, Large, Hard, Difficult, Tough, Challenging, Smelly, Stinky, Pungent, Rank, Fetid
words.def: Durus, Durush, Dudes, Duruseth (an.) Dudes, Dudsas, Dudsae, Dudsath (inan.) 1. Of great weight; difficult to lift or move. 2. Needing much effort or skill to accomplish, deal with, or understand. 3. Having a foul smell.

words.loc: Burden, Load, Weight, Responsibility, Onus, Charge, Duty, Obligation
words.def: Duruzbu, Duruzbūs, Duruzbu, Duruzbūth (an.) 1. A heavy or bulky thing that is being carried or is about to be carried. 2. A duty, obligation, or liability for which someone is held accountable, especially if it causes hardship, anxiety, worry, or grief.

words.loc: Empire, Imperium, Dominion, Realm, State, Kingdom, Military Command
words.def: Durzenos, Durzenosh, Durzenor, Durzenoseth (an.) 1. (Substantive Animate) A political state, often a monarchy, that has achieved a much greater current size than its initial size by conquering surrounding territories, cities or nations. 2. (Substantive Animate) A geographical area or body of troops under the command of a single official or general.

words.loc: Order, Command, Direct, Enjoin, Send, Dispatch
words.def: Durzen, Durzenit, Durzenikūs, Duzen, Duzno, Duznined 1. To give an authoritative direction or instruction to do something. 2. T order or instruct to go to a particular destination or in a particular direction.

words.loc: Order, Command, Instruction, Directive, Decree, Edict, Mandate, Ordinance
words.def: Duznil, Duznilae, Duznilath (inan.) 1. An authoritative command, direction, or instruction.
Pronoun 1. Used by a speaker to refer to multiple

words.loc: Knock Down, Knock Over, Push Over, Pull Down, Bring Down, Topple, Demolish, Flatten, Level, Raze, Kill, Incapacitate, Strike Down, Cut Down, Overthrow, Depose, Unseat
words.def: Drōg, Drōgūs, Drōge, Dōg, Dōgo, Dōgened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To cause to fall. 2. To pull or knock down a building. 3. To kill or seriously incapacitate someone. 4. To remove forcibly from power.
Drōper, Drōprit, Drōprikūs, Dōper, Dōpro, Dōprined 1. To make someone afraid or anxious.

words.loc: Know, Understand, Be Familiar With, Recognize
words.def: Drū, Drūkhūs, Druke, Dū, Duko, Dukened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To be aware of through observation, inquiry, or information. 2. To have knowledge or information concerning. 3. To have developed a relationship with someone or something. 4. To be familiar or acquainted with someone or something. 5. To have a good command of a subject or language.

words.loc: Weep, Cry, Cry for, Pity, Sorry for, Feel for, Feel sorry for, Sympathize with
words.def: Derūgud, Derūgdet, Drugudgūs, Dūgud, Dūgdo, Dūgdened 1. To shed tears. 2. (Idiomatic; lit. to weep for) To feel sorrow and compassion at the misfortune or suffering of someone or something.

words.loc: Charred, Singed, Scorched, Burned, Blackened, Ill-Omened
words.def: Dūl, Dūles Dūl, Dūleth (an.) Dūl, Dūlas, Dūlae, Dūlath (inan.) 1. Burned such that the surface is blackened. 2. (Figurative) Having or marked by future misfortune and/or misery.

words.loc: Fireplace, Firepit, Hearth, Brazier, Home, House
words.def: Dūlban, Dūlbanae, Dūlbanath (inan.) 1. A place for a domestic fire. 2. A metal bowl used for holding fire as a source of light or heat. 3. (Figurative) The place where one lives permanently, especially as a member of a family or household.
Drūli, Drūlit, Drūlikūs, Dūli, Dūlo, Dulined 1. To make something black or dark, especially as a result of burning or decay.
Pronoun 1. Used by a speaker to refer to an animate entity previously mentioned or easily identified.

Dūpesh, Dūpeshae, Dūpeshath (inan.) 1. Facts, information, and skills acquired by a person through experience or education. 2. What is known in a particular field or in total. 3. Awareness or familiarity gained by experience of a fact or situation.
Dūpor, Dūpres, Dūper, Dūporeth (an.) Dūper, Dūpras, Dūprae, Dūprath (inan.) 1. Having knowledge or perception of a situation or fact.

words.loc: Forgive, Pardon, Excuse, Exonerate, Absolve, Acquit, Overlook
words.def: Drūsūl, Drūsūlet, Drūsūlgūs, Dūsūl, Dūsūlo, Dūsūlened 1. To stop feeling angry or resentful toward someone for an offense, flaw, or mistake.
Eb, Ebae, Ebath (inan.) 1. A length of strong cord made of wound fibers or wire.

Ebzhael, Ebzhaeles, Ebzhael, Ebzhaeleth (an.) Ebzhael, Ebzhaelas, Ebzhaelae, Ebzhaelath (inan.) 1. Lacking courage. 2. (Substantive Animate) A person who lacks the courage to do or endure dangerous or unpleasant things.

words.loc: Fall, Drop, Plummet, Collapse, Abate, Subside, Lessen, Die Away, Fall Away, Befall, Happen, Fall Upon, Suit, Befit, Be Suitable For, Die, Perish
words.def: Ēg, Ēgūs, Ēge, Eg, Ego, Egened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To move downward, typically rapidly and freely without control, from a higher to a lower level. 2. (Of a person) To lose one's balance and collapse. 3. To become less intense, violent, severe, or widespread. 4. (Of something bad) To happen to someone. 5. To be the responsibility or obligation of someone 6. To be convenient for or acceptable to. 7. To die in battle.
Egen, Egenos, Egen, Egenoth (an.) Egen, Egenas, Egenae, Egenath (inan.) 1. Awkward in movement or in handling things.

words.loc: Set, Go Down, Vanish, Disappear, Fall, Rain, Snow
words.def: Ēgoth, Ēgotret, Ēgothkūs, Egoth, Gotro, Gotrened 1. (Of heavenly bodies) To appear to move toward and below the horizon. 2. (Of rain, snow, ash, etc.) To fall.

Ēgrephek, Ēgrephket, Ēgrephkūs, Egrephek, Egrephko, Egrephkened 1. (Intransitive) To receive a benefit (from); To be a beneficiary. 2. (Transitive) To be or to provide a benefit to. 3. To confer or invoke divine favor upon.
Egrephgil, Egrephgelae, Egrephgelath (inan.) 1. Some kind of divine or supernatural aid, or reward.

words.loc: Luck, Chance, Possibility, Probability, Fortune, Good Fortune, Misfortune
words.def: Egus, Egrae, Egrath (inan.) 1. Success or failure apparently brought by chance rather than through one's own actions. 2. A possibility of something happening. 3. Chance considered as a force that causes good or bad things to happen.
Egzhael, Egzhaeles, Egzhael, Egzhaeleth (an.) Egzhael, Egzhaelas, Egzhaelae, Egzhaelath (inan.) 1. Having, bringing, or resulting from good luck.
Eker, Ekrae, Ekrath (inan.) 1. (Of Patrin nobility) A group of people related by birth or marriage and sharing a common family name.
Ekromatkar, Ekromatkares, Ekromatkar, Ekromatkareth (an.) 1. A well-known epithet of Palagon Patermāgor as well as the name of a Patrin epic poem.
Particle 1. Used with the verbs 'khab, es' and 'ānor, anor' to form existential predicate constructions.
Productive Affix 1. Used to create a feminine form of the original word.
Elkhon, Elkhōs, Elkhen, Elkheth (an.) 1. A female child.
Elrūkar, Elrūkres, Elrūker, Elrūkreth 1. A married woman in relation to her spouse.

Ēman, Ēmahus, Ēme, Eman, Emo, Emaned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To freely transfer possession or the holding of something to someone.
Emil, Enlae, Enlath (inan.) 1. A thing given willingly to someone without payment.

Empher, Mophres, Mopher, Mophereth (an.) Mopher, Mophras, Mophrae, Empherath (inan.) 1. Not having existed before; Made, introduced or discovered recently or for the first time. 2. Unusual or original.
Ēmūs, Ēmūret, Emūskūs, Emūs, Emūro, Emūrened 1. To carry something. (Typically in one's arms) 2. To take something or someone to a place. 3. To take and hand over someone or something to a recipient.
Ēneth, Ēnthokūs, Ēnthe, Eneth, Entho, Enthoned 1. To feel intense or passionate dislike for.

Engor, Engorae, Engorath (inan.) 1. The main woody stem of a tree as distinct from its branches and roots. 2. A part of the trunk or a large branch of a tree that has fallen or been cut off. 3. A person's or animal's body apart from the limbs and head.
Engorel, Engorlas, Engorlae, Engorlath (inan.) 1. The main body or stalk of a plant or shrub.
Engorūk, Engorūkis, Engorūki, Engorūkith (an.) 1. A person who fells trees, cuts them into logs, or transports them to a carpenter.

words.loc: Hated, Loathed, Hateful, Hostile, Antagonistic, Enemy, Foe
words.def: Enthos, Enthosh, Enthor, Enthoseth (an.) Enthor, Enthoras, Enthorae, Enthorath (inan.) 1. Strongly and passionately disliked. 2. Unfriendly and antagonistic. 3. (Of a person or nation; Substantive Animate) Someone or something that is hostile to, feels hatred towards, opposes the interests of, or intends injury towards someone else. 4. Used as an epithet for Gimīlhizar and, rarely, Laegos.
Enthōn, Enthōnos, Enthōn, Enthōnoth (an.) Enthōn, Enthōnas, Enthōnae, Enthōnath (inan.) 1. Able or likely to cause harm, injury, problems, or have adverse consequences.

words.loc: Hatred, Ill-Will, Aversion, Dislike, Disgust, Odium, Loathing, Enmity
words.def: Enthōs, Enthōrae, Enthōrath (inan.) 1. Intense dislike or animosity.

words.loc: Fear, Be Afraid Of, Dread, Worry For, Worry About, Concern
words.def: Ēper, Ēprit, Ēprikūs, Eper, Epro, Eprined 1. To be afraid of someone or something as likely to be dangerous, painful, or threatening. 2. To be anxious about.

words.loc: Fear, Fright, Dread, Terror, Horror, Phobia, Awe, Reverance, Wonder, Amazement
words.def: Eph, Eprae, Eprath (inan.) 1. An emotion caused by the belief that someone or something is dangerous, likely to cause pain, or a threat. 2. A fear of or aversion to something. 3. (Poetic) A feeling of reverential respect mixed with fear or wonder.
1. (LOC) Situated at a great distance from something. 2. (ERG) From one place to another very far away.

words.loc: Far, Far Off, Faraway, Distant, Remote, Further
words.def: Ephil, Ephles, Ephel, Ephileth (an.) Ephel, Ephlas, Ephlae, Ephlath (inan.) 1. Situated at a great distance in space or time.

Ēpu, Ēpūkūs, Ēpe, Epu, Epo, Pūned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To ask for someone to do or give something. 2. To plead with someone for help.

words.loc: Submerge, Descend, Dive, Sink, Go Into, Enter
words.def: Ēras, Ēdret, Ēraskūs, Eras, Edro, Edrened 1. (Intransitive) To enter into or descend below the surface of the water or ground.

words.loc: Be, Be At, Stand, Be With, Exist, Locative Copula, Passive Voice
words.def: Khab, Khabet, Khabgūs, Es, Ero, Erened NOTE: This verb has irregular narrative and pluperfect conjugations. The narrative and pluperfect use the stem 'in' with the narrative being conjugated using the gnomic tense paradigm and the pluperfect being conjugated using the pluperfect tense paradigm. NOTE: This verb can also be translated using the English "stand" with the meaning of "to be located or positioned at" 1. (When taking an ergative, locative, or genitive postpositional predicate) To occupy a position in space or move in some manner. 2. (When taking a comitative postpositional predicate) To be accompanied by. 3. (When used intransitively or when with an existential particle; Can only be used with positive existential predicate constructions) To exist. 4. (Auxiliary) Used to mark the passive voice. 5. (Auxiliary) Used to mark the impersonal passive voice when used with the existential particle to modify unergative--but not unaccusative--verbs.

1. (GEN) Expressing movement with that result that someone or something becomes enclosed or surrounded by something else. 2. (GEN) Expressing movement on top of something. 3. (GEN) To the detriment of.

words.loc: Very, Too, Very Much, Too Much, Too Many, A Lot, Exceedingly, Excessively, Extremely, Overly, Over
words.def: 1. To a higher degree than is desirable, permissable, or possible.

words.loc: Public, Common People, Citizenry, Population, Populace
words.def: Eshbar, Eshbrae, Eshbrath (inan.) 1. Members of the community or state in general.

words.loc: Officially, For the State, On Behalf of the State
words.def: 1. In a formal and public way. 2. With the authority of the state or community.

words.loc: Public, Popular, General, Common, Communal, Open, Shared, State Property, State Treasury, State Revenue, Public Road, Public Infrastructure, Republic, State, Nation
words.def: Eshbrīn, Eshbrīs, Eshbrīn, Eshbrīneth (an.) Eshbrīn, Eshbrīnas, Eshbrīnae, Eshbrīnath (inan.) 1. Of, pertaining to, concerning, or belonging to the people, state, or community. 2. Open to or shared by all the people of a community. 3. Of or involved in the affairs of the community, especially in government. 4. (Substantive Inanimate) Any property owned or controlled by the state. 5. (Substantive Animate) A state where sovereignty rests with the people or their representatives.

Et, Etae, Etath (inan.) NOTE: This noun takes the allative case. 1. The wind that originates in the south and blows in a northward direction. 2. The direction toward the point of the horizon right of where the sun rises. 3. 3. The southern part of the world or of a specified country, region, or city.

words.loc: Hair, Head Hair, Locks, Tresses, Mane
words.def: Ethel, Ethlae, Ethlath (inan.) 1. Any of the fine threadlike strands growing from a person's head. 2. A growth of long hair on the neck of a horse, lion, or other animal.
Etīn, Etīs, Etīn, Etīneth (an.) Etīn, Etīnas, Etīnae, Etīnath (inan.) 1. Situated in the south or directed toward or facing the south. 2. Living in or originating from the south.
Gadban, Gadbanae, Gadbanath (inan.) 1. A place where weapons are kept.

words.loc: Take, Seize, Grab, Grasp, Receive, Accept, Make, Cause
words.def: Gegel, Geglekūs, Gegle, Gal, Galo, Galened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To get into one's hands, possession or control, with or without force. 2. To be given, presented with, or paid something. 3. (Auxiliary) To compel someone to do something.
Gan, Gās, Gan, Ganeth (an.) 1. An adult female human. 2. (Of animals) Female.

Ganauk, Ganaukh, Ganauk, Ganauketh (an.) Ganauk, Ganaukas, Ganaukae, Ganaukath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to the female gender. 2. Having the qualities stereotypically associated with women by the Patrin: nurturing, intelligent, lazy.
Ganaukeg, Ganauges, Ganaug, Ganaukegeth (an.) Ganaug, Ganaugas, Ganaugae, Ganaugath (inan.) 1. (Derogatory, of a man or boy) Exhibiting behaviors or mannerisms considered typical of a woman.
Gar, Garae, Garath (inan.) 1. A yellowish-brown alloy of copper with up to one-third tin. 2. A thing designed or used for inflicting bodily harm or physical damage.
Garān, Garānae, Garānath (inan.) 1. A person who works metal.

Gathraes, Gathraesh, Gathraer, Gathraeseth (an.) NOTE: This word specifically connotes magic of good or neutral origins. See rās "sorcery" for a word referring to evil magicks. 1. Magic derived from the manipulation of That Which Is, as distinct from That Which Is Not. 2. The powers, blessings, omens, and insights granted by or of the Gods.
1. (LOC) During the period of time preceding.
Gegmig, Gegmigot, Gegmigūs, Gehig, Gehigo, Gehigoned 1. To begin a voyage
Gehiro, Gehirae, Gehirōth (inan.) 1. A piece of material extended on a mast to catch the wind and propel a boat or ship or other vessel.
Gegmegrukh, Gegmegrukhet, Gegmegrukhūs, Gehirukh, Gehirkho, Gehirkhened 1. (Of a ship) To fill with water and sink.

words.loc: Nausea, Queasiness, Motion Sickness, Sea Sickness, Sickness, Illness
words.def: Gehās, Gehārae, Gehārath (inan.) 1. A feeling of sickness with an inclination to vomit.
Gemek, Gemekh, Gē, Geheth (an.) NOTE: The stem for this word is "geh-" 1. A person who sails.
Gendel, Gendelis, Gendeli, Gendelith (an.) 1. A waterbird with a broad bill, short legs, webbed feet, and a waddling gait.
Gendes, Gendesh, Gender, Gendreth (an.) 1. The people of a particular nation, community, or ethnic group.
Gegendresh, Gegendrezhet, Gegendreshkus, Gendresh, Gendrezho, Gendrezhned 1. (Intransitive) To enter into marriage with another. 2. (Transitive) To take as husband or wife.

words.loc: Shut, Close, Extinguish, Douse, Quench, Smother, Put Out
words.def: Geges, Gegsot, Gegeskūs, Ger, Gero, Geroned NOTE: This verb is used in reference to closing things temporarily. See pepō, pō "seal" for a verb meaning to close with no intention of opening in the near future. 1. To move something into position so as to block an opening, especially if that thing is expected to be opened later. 2. (Of fire) To cause to cease to burn or shine.
Igren, Igrenet, Igrehūs, Geren, Gerno, Gerened 1. To bear or carry goods or people from one place to another by sea or specifically by ship.
Igerombar, Igrombaret, Igrombargūs, Gerombar, Grombaro, Grombarened 1. To bear or carry (people or goods) from one place to another by land.
Geroned, Gerondis, Gerondi, Gerondith (an.) Geroned, Gerondas, Gerondae, Gerondath (inan.) 1. Not open.

words.loc: Verdict, Ruling, Judgement, Decision, Sentence, Punishment
words.def: Gerpesh, Gerpeshae, Gerpeshath (inan.) 1. A decision on a disputed issue in a civil or criminal case.

Gerphek, Igrophkae, Igrophkath (inan.) 1. A flat, shallow container for holding or serving food. 2. A small, typically rectangular or round, flat, and rigid object upon which things (typically food and drink) are carried.

words.loc: Drink, Imbibe, Consume, Inhale, Gulp, Breathe In, Draw In, Suck In
words.def: Gegad, Gegadgūs, Gegde, Ghad, Ghado, Ghadened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To take a liquid into the body. 2. (Of air, gases, smoke, fumes, etc.) To breathe in.

words.loc: Drunk, Intoxicated, Inebriated, Bibulous, Absorbent, Porous, Permeable
words.def: Ghadbor, Ghadbres, Ghadber, Ghadboreth (an.) Ghadber, Ghadbras, Ghadbrae, Ghadbrath (inan.) 1. Affected by alcohol to the extent of losing control of one's faculties or behavior. 2. (Of a material) Able to soak up liquid easily. 3. (Of a material) Having minute spaces or holes through which liquid or air may pass.

words.loc: Drunkard, Alcoholic, Drinker, Partier, Reveller, Carouser
words.def: Ghadkar, Ghadkres, Ghadker, Ghadkreth (an.) 1. A person who is habitually drunk. 2. A person who frequently attends parties or celebrations.
Ghadus, Ghadrae, Ghadrath (inan.) 1. A liquid that can be swallowed as refreshment or nourishment.

Ghahur, Ghahures, Ghahur, Ghahureth (an.) 1. A person who does not speak Classical Patrinaic or adhere to Patrin customs. (Especially if they are a foreigner who is not from Bibunor, Gemenor, or Sarmath) 2. A person who is not ethnically Patrin. (Especially if they are also not ethnically Bibunic, Gemenite, or Sarmathi) 3. A person who does not not adhere to the Kembaric faith.

Ghahurauk, Ghahuraukh Ghahurauk, Ghahurauketh (an.) Ghahurauk, Ghahuraukas, Ghahuraukae, Ghahuraukath (inan.) 1. Relating to the Ghahurene tribes, their people, or their language. 2. (Substantive Inanimate) The Ghahurene language.

Ghahuros, Ghahurae, Ghahurath (inan.) 1. The continental landmass on the southern side of the Ephil Mountains. 2. (Archaic) Any region or land that is not Patrinor, especially if that land is not Bibunor, Gemenor, or Sarmath.
Ghahurīn, Ghahurīs, Ghahurīn, Ghahurīneth (an.) Ghahurīn, Ghahurīnae, Ghahurīnath (inan.) 1. Of, from, in, or characteristic of a country or language other than one's own.
Ghahurūk, Ghahurūkh, Ghahurūki, Ghahurūkith (an.) 1. A native or member of the Ghahurene tribes or a person of Ghahurene descent.
1. (Adverbial Allative) Toward the shore, or toward the interior of a land and away from the sea.

Ghal, Ghales, Ghal, Ghaleth (an.) Ghal, Ghalas, Ghalae, Ghalath (inan.) 1. (In the Singular) Used to refer to all the individual members of a set without exception. 2. (In the Plural) Used to refer to the whole quantity or extent of a particular group or thing.
Gharad, Ghazdae, Ghazdath (inan.) 1. Atmospheric water vapor frozen into ice crystals and falling in light white flakes or lying on the ground as a white layer.
Gharadbu, Gharadbūs, Gharadbu, Gharadbūth (an.) 1. A flake of snow, especially a feathery ice crystal.

Ghash, Ghazhae, Ghazhath (inan.) 1. A weapon with a long metal blade and a hilt with a hand guard, used for thrusting or striking and now typically worn as part of ceremonial dress. 2. Each of a series of slender curved bones articulated in pairs to the spine (twelve pairs in humans), protecting the thoracic cavity and its organs. 3. A large edible marine fish with a streamlined body and a long flattened swordlike snout. 4. The flattened oval internal skeleton of the cuttlefish, which is made of white, lightweight, chalky material.

words.loc: Arrival, Coming, Approach, Advent, Appearance, Emergence
words.def: Ghashdil, Ghashdilae, Ghashdilath (inan.) 1. The action or process of arriving. 2. The materialization of a new development, phenomenon, or product.
Ghashdus, Ghashdurae, Ghashdurath (inan.) 1. A thing that happens, especially one of importance.

words.loc: Visitor, Guest, Customer, Consumer, Buyer, Purchaser, Shopper, Client
words.def: Ghashdūk, Ghashdūkis, Ghashdūki, Ghashdūkith (an.) 1. A person visiting a person or place. 2. A person or organization that buys goods or services from a store or business.
Ghashkhar, Ghashkharae, Ghashkharath (inan.) 1. A cover for the blade of a sword or dagger, typically made of leather or metal.

Ghazdauk, Ghazdaukh, Ghazdauk, Ghazdauketh (an.) Ghazdauk, Ghazdaukas, Ghazdaukae, Ghazdaukath (inan.) 1. (Of weather, a period of time, or an area) Characterized by snowfall. 2. Covered with snow. 3. (Of hair) Of a white or grayish-white color.

words.loc: Come, Approach, Advance, Near, Arrive, Appear, Happen, Occur, Transpire
words.def: Ghekzhad, Ghekzhadgūs, Ghekzhade, Ghazhad, Ghashdo, Ghashdened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Intransitive) To move nearer to the point of perspective. 2. (Intransitive) To reach a place at the end of a journey or a stage in a journey. 3. (Intransitive) To take place.
Ghazūk, Ghazūkis, Ghazūki, Ghazūkith (an.) 1. A man who fights with a sword. 2. A man trained to fight with weapons against other men or wild animals in an arena.

Ghelgeglor, Ghelgeglorae, Ghelgeglorath (inan.) NOTE: A ghelgeglor is generally understood to refer to a large establishent, while a ringodan "tavern, pub, store, inn" can refer to a smaller inn. 1. An establishment providing housing, food, and drink, especially for travelers. 2. A large inn with a central courtyard for travelers, merchants, and caravans.

words.loc: Feces, Excrement, Dung, Manure, Scat, Poop, Shit
words.def: Ghem, Ghemae, Ghemath (inan.) 1. Waste matter discharged from the bowels after food has been digested.
Ghim, Ghimae, Ghimath (inan.) 1. The set of hard, calcareous structures present in the mouth of many animals, generally used for biting and chewing food.
Gho, Ghae, Ghōth (inan.) NOTE: This noun takes the allative case. 1. The edge of the land where it meets a body of water.
Productive Affix 1. Forms a verb meaning a reiteration of the original verb.
Ghedpher, Ghedpheret, Ghedphergūs, Ghodber, Ghodbero, Ghoberned 1. To go to see and spent time with socially. 2. To habitually go to someone to comfort them, especially someone ill or in distress.

words.loc: Stay, Stay Behind, Remain, Endure, Survive
words.def: Ghedkhab, Ghedkhabet, Ghedkhabgūs, Ghodgeb, Ghodgebet, Ghodgebened 1. To stay in the same place that one has been occupying. 2. To continue to exist or, especially after people or things have ceased to exist or departed.

words.loc: Remember, Recall, Recollect, Memorize, Mind, Be Mindful Of, Remind
words.def: Ghedlōger, Ghedlōgrit, Ghedlōgrikūs, Ghodlōger, Ghodlōgro, Ghodlōgrined 1. To recall from one's memory. 2. To commit something into memory. 3. To keep in mind. 4. To not forget. 5. to cause someone to remember something or to bring a fact, event, or person to their attention
Ghodmahar, Ghodmakres, Ghodmaker, Ghodmakreth (an.) 1. One who carries or brings messages.

Gherēman, Gherēmet, Gherēmahūs, Ghodman, Ghodmano, Ghodmaned 1. To give something back to its original owner. 2. To repay a loan, debt, or sum of money. 3. To say something that has already been said.

Ghodmanil, Ghodmanilae, Ghodmanilath (inan.) 1. The action of paying back a loan or debt. 2. Something awarded or offered to someone which corrects, makes up for, or is in return for some sort of loss. 3. The act of making good or compensating for some sort of loss.

Ghedbephek, Ghedbephkūs, Ghedbephke, Ghodphek, Ghodpheko, Ghodphekened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To make again or anew. 2. To build again or anew.

words.loc: Roll Up, Wind, Coil, Furl, Ball, Wad
words.def: Ghedresh, Ghedreshet, Ghedreshkūs, Ghodresh, Ghodresho, Ghodreshened 1. To wrap something round on itself; to form into a spherical or cylindrical body by causing to turn over and over.
Ghodserek, Ghodserkit, Ghodserkūs, Ghodsok, Ghodsoko, Ghodsokined 1. To make something or someone free of dirt, marks or mess, especially by wiping, washing, or brushing.

Ghedzer, Ghedzerit, Ghedzeriskūs, Ghodzir, Ghodziro, Ghodzirined 1. To revolve by turning over and over. 2. To impel forward with a revolving motion on a supporting surface. 3. To move upon rollers or wheels.

Ghodziril, Ghodzirilae, Ghodzirilath (inan.) 1. A collection of sheets of paper bound together to hinge at one edge, containing written material, pictures, etc. 2. A roll of paper, papyrus, or parchment; a writing formed into a roll.
Ghokh, Ghokis, Ghokthen (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. Sand that originates from beaches or coasts.

words.loc: Refuse, Decline, Reject, Forswear, Turn Down
words.def: Gegrū, Gegrūt, Gegrūkūs, Ghorū, Ghoro, Ghorūned 1. To choose not to do something, especially if a request or demand. 2. To choose not to accept.

Ghāz, Ghāza, Ghāzāth (inan.) 1. A woody plant taller than a shrub, typically having a single stem or trunk growing to a considerable height and bearing lateral branches at some distance from the ground. 2. (Poetic; Metonymic) Something made from a tree or wood.
Ghāzael, Ghāzaeles, Ghāzael, Ghāzaeleth (an.) Ghāzael, Ghāzaelas, Ghāzalae, Ghāzaelath (inan.) 1. Made of wood or trees.

words.loc: Rough, Uneven, Coarse, Bumpy, Unrefined, Crude, Unprocessed, Untreated, Unpurified
words.def: Ghāzauk, Ghāzaukh, Ghāzauk, Ghāzauketh (an.) Ghāzauk, Ghāzaukas, Ghāzaukae, Ghāzaukath (inan.) 1. Having an uneven or irregular surface. 2. (Of materials) Not having been removed of impurities or unwanted elements.
Ghāzban, Ghāzbanae, Ghāzbanath (inan.) 1. A piece of land planted with fruit trees. 2. An area in which trees have been planted, especially for commercial purposes.

words.loc: Arboreal, Arborescent, Treelike, Treeish, Branched, Dendroid, Dendriform
words.def: Ghāzīn, Ghāzīs, Ghāzīn, Ghāzīneth (an.) Ghāzīn, Ghāzīnas, Ghāzīnae, Ghāzīnath (inan.) 1. Of, relating to, or resembling a tree. 2. Having a branched structure similar to a tree.
Ghīn, Ghīs, Ghīn, Ghīneth (an.) Ghīn, Ghīnas, Ghīnae, Ghīnath (inan.) 1. Of, relating to, pertaining to, or near a coast.
Pronoun. 1. (Reflexive) Him, Her, It, They 2. (Emphatic) Used by a speaker to intensify the subject.
Gihar, Gihares, Gihar, Gihareth (an.) Gihar, Giharas, Giharae, Giharath (inan.) 1. A person who carves wood, stone, ivory, coral, etc., especially professionally. 2. An artist who makes sculptures.

words.loc: Star, Constellation, Meteor, Shooting Star, Pupil
words.def: Gimīl, Gimīlae, Gimīlath (inan.) 1. A luminous point in the night sky. 2. (Poetic) A collection of stars perceived as a figure or pattern. 3. A small meteor burning up on entering the atmosphere. 4. The dark opening in the center of the iris of the eye.
Gimīlīn, Gimīlīs, Gimīlīn, Gimīlīneth (an.) Gimīlīn, Gimīlīnas, Gimīlīnae, Gimīlīnath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to the stars.
Gegin, Gegnet, Gigihūs, Gin, Gino, Gined 1. To cut or shape something with a chisel. 2. To make a groove or hole with a sharp tool or blade.

words.loc: Glyph, Character, Letter, Carved Symbol, Handwriting, Writing System, Orthography, Epistle, Message
words.def: Gindas, Gindaes, Gindathen (inan.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. (Substantive Inanimate) Any non-verbal sign that imparts information, especially if it has been carved into wood or stone. 2. (Substantive Inanimate) A symbol in a writing system. 3. (Substantive Inanimate) The conventional spelling system of a language. 4. (Substantive Inanimate) The characteristic writing of a particular person. 5. (Substantive Inanimate) A written, generally formal communication, especially if sent by a messenger.

Gegindum, Gegindumet, Geginduhūs, Gindum, Gindumo, Gindumened 1. To draw attention to in a public medium in order to increase sales, public awareness, or public support, especially concerning politics. 2. To declare one's public approval or support of. 3. (Of law) To introduce and support a proposal or individual in a legislative assembly.

Gindumil, Gindumilae, Gindumilath (inan.) 1. A notice or announcement made in a public medium in order to increase sales, public awareness, or public support. 2. Informal inscriptions on walls or other surfaces as opposed to official inscriptions.
Gegnesh, Gignezhet, Gigneshkūs, Ginesh, Ginzho, Ginzhened 1. To write or carve text or symbols onto something, especially a hard surface. 2. To mark with a branding iron.
Gegneth, Gegnethet, Gegnethkūs, Gineth, Gīntho, Ginthened 1. To add to a document as an attachment or supplement.

Ginthil, Ginthilae, Ginthilath (inan.) 1. Additional content added at the end of a document, typically in order to correct, clarify, or supplement something. 2. A comment added to a text. 3. (Grammar) A dispensible phrase in a clause or sentence that modifies its meaning.
Ginzhil, Ginzhilae, Ginzhilath (inan.) 1. Text or symbols carved onto a hard surface.

words.loc: Eyelash, Trichome, Sensillum, Sensilla, Insect Hair, Insect Fur
words.def: 1. Each of the short hairs growing on the edges of the eyelids, serving to protect the eyes from dust particles. 2. A small hair or outgrowth from the epidermis of a plant. 3. (Of insects, arthropods, etc.) A simple sensory receptor that is typically hair- or rod-shaped.

Gis, Gish, Gir, Gireth (an.) 1. A flying insect characterized by a single pair of transparent wings and sucking mouthparts. 2. A generic term for any small arthopod with six legs, especially if it has one or two pairs of wings.
Gither, Githra, Githrāth (inan.) 1. A strong, hard, silver-gray metal that rusts, is attracted by magnets, and is used in making steel.
Githrael, Githraeles, Githrael, Githraeleth (an.) Githrael, Githraelas, Githraelae, Githraelath (inan.) 1. Made of iron or steel.

words.loc: Rare, Scarce, Infrequent, Uncommon, Precious, Valuable
words.def: Githrauk, Githraukh, Githrauk, Githrauketh (an.) Githrauk, Githraukas, Githraukae, Githraukath (inan.) 1. (Of a thing) Not found in large numbers and consequently of interest or value. 2. (Of an event, situation, or condition) Not occurring very often.
Githreg, Githregae, Githregath (inan.) 1. A malleable, ductile, silver-white metal that is resistant to corrosion.

words.loc: Land, Alight, Disembark, Go Ashore, Get Off
words.def: Gegez, Gegzat, Gegzakūs, Giz, Gizo, Gizaned 1. To come onto the land from the air or sea. 2. To leave a watercraft
1. (ERG) Away from the inside, center, or other point of reference.

words.loc: Chicken, Hen, Rooster, Cockerel, Chick, Pullet, Poultry
words.def: Gog, Goges, Gog, Gogeth (an.) 1. A domestic fowl kept for its eggs or meat.
Gogban, Gogbanae, Gogbanath (inan.) 1. A cage or pen for keeping poultry.

words.loc: Return, Go Back, Come Back, Revert, Regress, Recur, Reappear, Do Again
words.def: Gegme, Gegmēt, Gegmēkūs, Gome, Gomo, Gomēned 1. (Intransitive) To come or go back to. 2. (Intransitive) To change back to. 3. (Intransitive) To happen or do another time.

words.loc: Master, Owner, Lord, Nobleman, Aristocrat, Dominus, Camel
words.def: Gor, Gores, Gor, Goreth (an.) 1. Someone who has control over someone or something. 2. The master of a household. 3. A nobleman or aristocrat. 4. A beast of burden much used in desert areas.
Gorban, Gorbanae, Gorbanath (inan.) 1. A building set apart and adapted for keeping camels and horses.
Gorduk, Gordukae, Gordukath (inan.) 1. The military arm that fights while riding camels and horses.
Gorel, Gorlas, Gorlae, Gorlath (an.) 1. A young camel or horse.
Gorkhon, Gorkhonae, Gorkhonath (inan.) 1. The territory ruled by a specific monarch or other ruler.
Gorus, Gorae, Gorath (inan.) 1. The authority, status, and influence of a monarch or ruler. 2. The state or position of being a monarch.

words.loc: Horseman, Camel Rider, Cavalryman, Camelryman, Rider
words.def: Gorūk, Gorūkis, Gorūki, Gorūkith (an.) 1. A person who rides a horse or camel. 2. A soldier on horseback or camelback, especially one in the cavalry. 3. A mounted person.

words.loc: It, They, Thing, Object, Item, Stuff
words.def: Goth, Gothae, Gothath (inan.) 1. (Pronoun) Used by a speaker to refer to one or more inanimate entities previously mentioned or easily identified. 2. An object that one need not, cannot, or does not wish to give a specific name to. 3. Unspecified things or matters.
Gothban, Gothbanae, Gothbanath (inan.) 1. A formal examination of evidence before a judge, and typically before a jury, in order to decide guilt in a case of criminal or civil proceedings.
Gothel, Gothlas, Gothla, Gothlath (inan.) 1. A thing of little value or importance.

words.loc: Guilt, Remorse, Regret, Contrition, Guilty, Ashamed, Remorseful, Contrite, Sorry
words.def: Gothon, Gothonae, Gothonath (inan.) 1. A feeling of having done wrong or failed in an obligation.
Gothūk, Gothūkis, Gothūki, Gothūkith (an.) 1. A public official appointed to decide cases in a court of law, especially one who conducts a court that deals with major offenses or is largely urban.

words.loc: Sunset, Sundown, Nightfall, Twilight, Dusk, Gloaming
words.def: Gotril, Gotrilae, Gotrilath (inan.) 1. The time in the evening when the sun disappears or daylight fades.

Grephkepor, Grephkebres, Grephkeber, Grephkeporeth (an.) Grephkeber, Grephkebras, Grephkebrae, Grephkebrath (inan.) 1. Having divine aid, or protection, or some other form of blessing. 2. Worthy of worship. 3. Dedicated to a religious purpose or god. 4. Revered in a religion.
Gruma, Grumae, Grumāth (inan.) 1. A mass of small bubbles formed on or in liquid, especially that found on waves. 2. The line of foam formed by waves breaking on a seashore or reef.
Grumag, Grumagae, Grumagath (inan.) 1. Watery liquid secreted into the mouth by glands, providing lubrication for chewing and swallowing, and aiding digestion.

words.loc: Aqueous, Watery, Diluted, Vapid, Insipid, Boring, Uninteresting
words.def: Grumor, Grumores, Grumor, Grumoreth (an.) Grumor, Grumoras, Grumorae, Grumorath (inan.) 1. Resembling or characteristic of water. 2. Diluted or having too much water. 3. (Figurative) Offering nothing that is stimulating or challenging. 4. Lacking flavor.
Grumorbīl, Grumorbīlae, Grumorbīlath (inan.) 1. Spoken or written words that have no meaning or seem to have no meaning.

words.loc: Die, Be Slain, Fall, Perish, Decease, Pass Away, Expire
words.def: Gegrem, Gegremikūs, Gegreme, Gurem, Gurmo, Gurmined NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To stop living; to become dead. 2. To cease to exist.

words.loc: Underworld, Netherworld, Heaven, Hall of Passing
words.def: Gurmakhar, Gurmakhrae, Gurmakhrath (inan.) NOTE: This noun takes the allative case. 1. The place one's spirit enters upon death, often conceived as below the surface of the earth.

words.loc: Gand, Revenant, Wight, Ghoul, Zombie, Draugr, Living Dead
words.def: Gurmaur, Gurmaurae, Gurmaurath (inan.) 1. A corpse that has been possessed by a weak, evil spirit from the Void. 2. All gands taken collectively.
Gurmined, Gurmindis, Gurmindi, Gurmindith (an.) Gurmined, Gurmindas, Gurmindae, Gurmindath (inan.) 1. Deprived of the power of sensation.
Gurmūs, Gurmūsh, Gurmūr, Gurmūseth (an.) Gurmūr, Gurmūras, Gurmūrae, Gurmūrath (inan.) 1. Of, pertaining to, or causing death.

words.loc: Goodbye, Farewell, Bye, See You Later
words.def: Phatic Expression 1. Used whenever the speaker or person addressed is departing.

words.loc: Death, Good Death, Demise, End, Passing
words.def: Gurum, Gurmae, Gurmath (inan.) 1. The action or state of dying or being killed. 2. A particularly desirable or preferable way of dying.

Guthrūk, Guthrūkis, Guthrūki, Guthrūkith (an.) NOTE: This word specifically connotes a spellcaster who uses white magic. See rātān "sorceror, witch" for a word referring to a spellcaster of evil intentions. 1. Someone who uses or has skill with the magic of That Which Is.
Gegmek, Gegmekit, Gegmeke, Gē, Geho, Gehined 1. To travel in a boat or ship with sails.
Gīpesh, Gīpshae, Gīpshath (inan.) 1. The act of painting or illustrating.
Gīrkhil, Gīrkhilae, Gīrkhilath (inan.) 1. A ship that has sunk or run aground so that it is no longer seaworthy.
Habar, Habres, Haber, Habareth (an.) 1. A person who attacks and robs ships at sea.
Habrāsh, Habrāshae, Habrāshath (inan.) 1. The practice of attacking and robbing ships at sea.
1. (Adverbial) The day before today.

words.loc: Mouth, Language, Speech, Voice, Song, Bark, Roar
words.def: Hag, Hages, Hage, Hageth (an.) 1. The mouth; The opening in the lower part of the face that is surrounded by the lips. 2. The principal method of human communication, wherein words are used in a grammatical structure to convey meaning via speech, writing, or gestures. 3. A language spoken by a particular community or ethnic group. 4. (Poetic; Of animals) A noise created by an animal, such as a bark, roar, or birdsong. (Typically implies a positive connotation, like regality, loyalty, or beauty)
Hagar, Hagres, Hager, Hagareth (an.) 1. A large and aggressive creature living in or under the sea that attacks ships. 2. Any large marine animal.

words.loc: Huge, Giant, Massive, Colossal, Enormous, Gigantic, Mammoth, Vast, Immense, Great
words.def: Hagrauk, Hagraukh, Hagrauk, Hagrauketh (an.) Hagrauk, Hagraukas, Hagraukae, Hagraukath (inan.) 1. Of very great size or force
Han, Hās, Han, Haneth (an.) 1. The time when the Sun is above the horizon and it lights the sky.
Hanath, Hanthae, Hanthath (inan.) 1. An elevated landmass smaller than a mountain.

words.loc: The Day Before, The Previous Day, The Preceding Day
words.def: 1. (Adverbial) On the preceding day.
Hanīn, Hanīs, Hanīn, Hanīneth (an.) Hanīn, Hanīnas, Hanīnae, Hanīnath (inan.) 1. Of, pertaining to, or relating to days or daytime. 2. Done, produced, or occuring every day.
Har, Harus, Har, Haruth (an.) 1. A person whom one knows and with whom one has a bond of mutual affection, typically exclusive of sexual or family relations.
Heker, Hekrut, Hegrukūs, Har, Haro, Haruned 1. To become or act as a friend to.

words.loc: Popular, Well-Liked, Liked, Well-Connected, Connected, Rich In Friends, Abounding In Friends
words.def: Harael, Haraeles, Harael, Haraeleth (an.) Harael, Haraelas, Haraelae, Haraelath (inan.) 1. Liked, admired, or enjoyed by many people or by a particular group. 2. Having favorable rapport or involvement with many (typically powerful or influential) individuals. 3. Having many friends.

Harāsh, Harāshae, Harāshath (inan.) 1. The state of being friends. 2. An agreement between entities to cooperate in order to achieve a mutual goal, especially if temporary. 3. The state of being allied.
Hashiger, Hashigros, Hashiger, Hashigroth (an.) 1. (Substantive Animate) A person who shoots with a bow and arrows.

words.loc: Dock, Wharf, Quay, Pier, Jetty, Berth, Harbor, Mooring, Landing
words.def: Haub, Haubae, Haubath (inan.) 1. A structure extending alongshore or out from the shore into a body of water, to which boats may be moored.
Hekaubi, Hekaubīt, Hekaubikūs, Haubi, Haubo, Haubīned 1. (Of ships) To tie up at a dock, especially in order to load or unload passengers or cargo.

words.loc: On This Side Of, Across, Along, Down
words.def: 1. (LOC) To be at or on the hither side of. 2. (GEN) From one side to the other of. 3. (GEN) Moving in a constant direction on.

words.loc: Spider, Arachnid, Attercop, Daddy Long-Legs, Harvestman
words.def: Hengar, Hengaris, Hengari, Hengarith (an.) 1. An eight-legged predatory arachnid with an unsegmented body consisting of a fused head and thorax and a rounded abdomen.
Hengarkhon, Hengarkhonae, Hengarkhonath (inan.) 1. A network of fine threads constructed by a spider from fluid secreted by its spinnerets.

words.loc: Copper, Brown, Russet, Tawny, Hazel, Husky, Hoarse, Throaty, Gruff, Gravelly
words.def: Herauk, Heraukh, Herauk, Herauketh (an.) Herauk, Heraukas, Heraukae, Heraukath (inan.) 1. Resembling the metal copper, especially in color. 2. Of a color produced by mixing red, yellow, and blue, as of dark wood or rich soil. 3. (Of a person's voice) Harsh and rough sounding.

words.loc: Rust of Copper, Rust of Bronze, Verdigris
words.def: Hereg, Hergae, Hergath (inan.) 1. A blue-green rust that forms on copper, bronze, or other copper-containing materials.

words.loc: Turquoise, Blue-Green, Cyan, Teal, Aquamarine, Mint, Sea Green, Aeruginous, Verdigrisy, Rusty, Rusted
words.def: Hergauk, Hergaukh, Hergauk, Hergauketh (an.) Hergauk, Hergaukas, Hergaukae, Hergaukath (inan.) NOTE: Considered a shade of nīmlauk 'blue, turquoise, gray'. 1. Resembling the greenish-blue color of copper rust. 2. (Of painted, dyed, or brightly colored items) Of a greenish-blue color. 3. (Of copper or bronze) Covered in rust.

words.loc: Copper, Brass, Money, Pay, Fee, Fare, Payment, Debt
