words.def: Hes, Herae, Herath (inan.) 1. A reddish-brown metal that is the primary component of the alloy bronze. 2. A yellow-brown alloy of copper and zinc. 3. (Figurative) A medium of exchange in the form of coins; coins collectively. 4. (Figurative) An amount charged for a privilege, service, or transaction. 5. (Figurative) Money that one person or entity owes or is required to pay to another, generally as a result of a financial transaction or agreement.
Hespen, Hespened, Hespen, Hespeneth (an.) 1. A small bag, often made of leather, that is typically used for keeping money.

Het, Hetae, Hetath (inan.) 1. The season after winter and before summer, in which fertile land from the flooding of the Lash River is revealed and new crops and vegetation are beginning to grow; it spans from the middle of January to the middle of May.

Hetauk, Hetaukh, Hetauk, Hetauketh (an.) Hetauk, Hetaukas, Hetaukae, Hetaukath (inan.) 1. Pertaining to or occuring in spring. 2. Typical of spring. 3. (Figurative) Young, seemingly young, or characteristic of young people. 4. (Figurative; Of plants) Still green and not dried.

words.loc: Public Place, Public Square, Open Area, Marketplace, Plaza, Forum
words.def: Heterban, Heterbanae, Heterbanath (inan.) 1. A public, open space in a built-up area, especially if it houses a public market or is used for public commerce.

Heth, Hetis, Hethen (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. A domesticated mammal with a thick wooly coat kept in flocks for its wool or meat. 2. (Poetic) A whale.
Hezban, Hezbanae, Hezbanath (inan.) 1. A place where treasure or wealth is stored safely. 2. A strongbox or chest for holding valuables.
Hezhael, Hezhaeles, Hezhael, Hezhaeleth (an.) Hezhael, Hezhaelas, Hezhaelae, Hezhaelath (inan.) 1. Made of copper, brass, or bronze.

words.loc: Follower, Retainer, Assistant, Attendant, Companion, Servant, Retinue, Entourage, Suite, Court, Staff, Personnel, Train
words.def: Hezkaros, Hezkarosh, Hezkaror, Hezkaroseth (an.) 1. (Substantive Animate) A servant or follower of a noble, wealthy, or important person, especially one that has worked for a person or family for a long time. 2. (Substantive Animate; In the Plural) A group of advisors, assistants, or others accompanying an important person.

words.loc: Jump, Leap, Hop, Bound, Vault, Spring, Flicker, Glimmer, Twinkle
words.def: Heger, Hegrit, Hegrikūs, Hir, Hiro, Hirined 1. To propel oneself rapidly upward, downward and/or in any horizontal direction such that momentum causes the body to become airborne. 2. To burn or shine with a faint, unsteady light.

words.loc: Game, Sport, Play,  Offering, Sacrifice, Gift
words.def: His, Hish, Hir, Hiseth (an.) 1. A playful or competitive activity, especially if structured. 2. Any activity that uses physical exertion or skills competitively under a set of rules that is not based on aesthetics. 3. The killing (and often burning) of a living thing as an offering to a deity. 4. A living thing, object, or immaterial thing offered to a deity.
1. (Interrogative) For what or which cause, reason, or purpose.

words.loc: Hunt, Chase, Pursue, Go After, Come After, Stalk, Run Down, Track, Trail, Follow, Shadow, Hound, Tail
words.def: Hezkar, Hezkargūs, Hezkare, Hizar, Hizaro, Hizarened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To move or travel behind with the intention of catching up to. 2. To pursue and kill an animal for sport or food. 3. To follow a track or trail. 4. To move behind in the same path or direction.
Hizarbesh, Hizarbeshae, Hizarbeshath (inan.) 1. Something that follows an action, condition, or sequence of events.
Hiazaril, Hizarilae, Hizarilath (inan.) 1. An act of hunting wild animals or game. 2. An act of searching for someone or something.

words.loc: Hunter, Huntsman, Predator, Follower, Pursuer, Chaser, Stalker
words.def: Hizarūk, Hizarūkis, Hizarūki, Hizarūkith (an.) 1. A person or animal that hunts. 2. A person or thing that pursues another.

words.loc: Following, Next, Subsequent, Ensuing, After, Favorable, Fair
words.def: Hizren, Hizrenos, Hizren, Hizrenoth (an.) Hizren, Hizrenos, Hizrenae, Hizrenoth (inan.) 1. Coming after, either in sequence or in time. 2. (Of weather, wind, sea, etc.) Blowing or flowing in the direction of travel.
1. Walking rather than traveling by other means.

Hōter, Hōtrit, Hōtergūs, Hoter, Hotro, Hotrined 1. To move at a regular pace on the feet with at least one foot on the ground at all times. 2. To lift and set down one's foot in order to go somewhere or move to a new position.
1. (LOC) At a lower level than. 2. (GEN) To or toward a lower level than.

Hoth, Hotrad, Hoter, Hotheth (an.) 1. A limb on which a person or animal stands and walks. 2. The lower extremity of the leg below the ankle. 3. (Of furniture) A protrusion of an object such that it supports it from underneath.
Hotrīl, Hotrīlae, Hotrīlath (inan.) 1. A single step taken when walking or running.
1. (Idiomatic; lit. to make steps) To walk in a military manner with a regular measured pace. 2. (Idiomatic; lit. to make steps) To walk or march in public in a formal procession.
Hudnūs, Hudnūmesh, Hudnūmer, Hudnūseth (an.) 1. A very large marine mammal with a streamlined hairless body, a horizontal tail fin, and a blowhole on the top of its head for breathing.
Ōhush, Ōhushit, Ōhushe, Hush, Husho, Hushined 1. To release an arrow or missile from a projectile-firing weapon.
Huzar, Huzares, Huzar, Huzareth (an.) 1. Vibrations that travel through the air or another medium and can be heard when they reach a person's or animal's ear.
Hūzun, Hūzunit, Hūzungūs, Huzen, Huzno, Huznined 1. To perceive with the ear the sound made by someone or something. 2. To be told or informed of.
Hūzrūl, Hūzrūlet, Hūzrūlgūs, Huzrūl, Huzrūlo, Huzrūlened 1. (Intransitive) To act in accordance with a wish or command. 2. (Transitive) To comply with the command, direction, or request of.
Huzun, Huzned, Huzen, Huzneth (an.) 1. An organ by which animals hear.
Hāspis, Hāspirae, Hāspirath (inan.) 1. A tool typically used for chopping wood, usually a steel blade attached at a right angle to a wooden handle.

words.loc: Feather, Quill, Pinion, Plume, Plumage, Down
words.def: Hēgar, Hēgres, Hēger, Hēgreth (an.) 1. Any of the flat appendages growing from a bird's skin and forming its plumage, consisting of a partly hollow horny shaft fringed with vanes of barbs.

words.loc: Fly, Soar, Glide, Race, Speed, Dash, Dart, Shoot, Hurry
words.def: Ihēgo, Ihēgōt, Ihēgōkūs, Hēgo, Hēgo, Hēgōned 1. To travel through the air, or a gas or vacuum, without being in contact with a grounded surface. 2. Go or move quickly.

words.loc: Winged, Flying, Airborne, Flying Animal, Bird, Bat, Insect, Bug
words.def: Hēgōn, Hēgōnos, Hēgōn, Hēgōnoth (an.) Hēgōn, Hēgōnas, Hēgōnae, Hēgōnath (inan.) 1. Having wings. 2. Flying or soaring as if on wings. 3. Moving or able to move by means of flight. 4. (Substantive Animate; Poetic) An animal that is capable of flight.
Hēgōpesh, Hēgōpshae, Hēgōpshath (inan.) 1. The action or process of flying through the air.

words.loc: Fly At, Fly Towards, Swoop, Plunge, Plummet, Dive, Attack, Ambush, Pounce, Raid, Assault
words.def: Ihēgōsh, Ihēgōshet, Ihēgōshkūs, Hēgōsh, Hēgōsho, Hēgōshened 1. To fly towards. 2. (Intransitive) To fly or glide rapidly downwards. 3. (Figurative; Especially of animals) To carry out a sudden attack.
Hīmben, Hīmbēs, Hīmben, Hīmbeneth (an.) 1. A hinged and sharply-pointed limb used by an arthopod for feeding or defense.
Hīn, Hīna, Hīnāth (inan.) 1. A crustacean with a broad carapace, stalked eyes, and five pairs of legs, the first pair of which are modified as pincers.

Hōm, Hōs, Hōm, Hōmeth (an.) 1. The physical structure of a person or animal, including the bones, flesh, and organs. 2. (Of animals) A dead body. 3. The main body of a ship or other maritime vessel, including the bottom, sides, and deck but not the masts, rigging, other fittings, or oars. 4. A long, narrow part or section forming the handle or main body of a tool or weapon.
Hūzen, Hūzenos, Hūzen, Hūzenoth (an.) Hūzen, Hūzenas, Hūzenae, Hūzenath (inan.) 1. Complying or willing to comply with orders or requests.
Hūzunos, Hūzunosh, Hūzunor, Hūzunoseth (an.) 1. The assembled spectators or listeners at a public event.

words.loc: Vocative Particle, Hey, Oi, O, Oh
words.def: 1. Used as an exclamation to get attention. 2. (Literary) Used before a name in direct address, as in prayers and poetry.

words.loc: Hello, Hi, Hail, Greetings, Hullo, Hey
words.def: Phatic Expression 1. Used as a greeting. 2. Used when expressing puzzlement or discovery.
1. (Interrogative) In, at, near, or to what place.
1. (Interrogative) From what place or source.
Ida, Idae, Idāth (inan.) 1. The period of time between afternoon and night.

words.loc: Guard, Guardsman, Sentry, Sentinel, Watchman, City Guard, Police, Constabulary, Fireman, Firefighter
words.def: Idūk, Idūkis, Idūki, Idūkith (an.) 1. A person or thing that protects or watches over something. 2. (In the Plural) The civil force of a national or local government, responsible for the prevention and detection of crime, the maintenance of public order, and the prevention and elimination of fires.
Iedban, Iedbanae, Iedbanath (inan.) 1. A shallow place in a river or stream allowing one to move across.

Iēnda, Iēndākūs, Iēnde, Ienda, Iendo, Iendāned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To perform or undergo the first part of.
Iendākar, Iendākres, Iendāker, Iendākreth (an.) 1. An entity new to or inexperienced in a skill, field, or situation. 2. A person who learns from and is being trained by a teacher.

words.loc: Beginning, Start, Origin, Genesis, Dawn, Inception, Commencement, Onset, Opening
words.def: Iendāl, Iendālae, Iendālath (inan.) 1. The point in time or space at which something starts.
Iendāned, Iendāndis, Iendāndi, Iendāndith (an.) Iendāned, Iendāndas, Iendāndae, Iendāndath (inan.) 1. Having been initiated but not yet completed.

words.loc: Smaller, Smallest, Fewer, Fewest, Lesser, Least
words.def: Iepīr, Iepīris, Iepīri, Iepīrith (an.) Iepīr, Iepīras, Iepīrae, Iepīrath (inan.) NOTE: The stem "iep-" is used for the comparative and superlative grades of nuth 'small, little, few, less'. 1. Smaller in amount, extent or significance. 2. Smallest in amount, extent, or significance.

words.loc: Love, Adore, Be Infatuated With, Enjoy Greatly, Like Very Much
words.def: Iesu, Iesūt, Iēsūkūs, Ieru, Iero, Ierūned 1. To have a deep, strong affection for someone or something. 2. To be strongly inclined towards something.

words.loc: Charm, Ward, Protection Spell, Apotropaic Magic, Witch Mark
words.def: Ierūbros Shēs, Ierūbrosh Shēsh, Ierūbror Shēr, Ierūbroseth Shēseth (an.) 1. An object or marking kept, placed or worn to ward off evil, misfortune, or the evil eye.

words.loc: Fall Out Of Love, Become Disenamored
words.def: Iesūg, Iesūgot, Iesūgūs, Ierūg, Ierūgo, Ierūgoned 1. To cease to be in love with someone.

words.loc: Fornicate, Copulate, Couple, Have Sex With, Fuck, Screw, Shag, Bang
words.def: Iesūr, Iesūret, Iesūrgūs, Ierūr, Ierūro, Ierūrened 1. To have sexual intercourse.
Iesūsh, Iesūzhet, Iesūshkūs, Ierūsh, Ierūzho, Ierūzhened 1. To come to have feelings of love.

words.loc: Door, Doorway, Entrance, Opening, Portal, Ford, Crossing, Causeway, Connection, Bridge, Olive Branch, Overture, Peace Offering, Space, Period, Full Stop
words.def: Iet, Ieth, Iet, Ieteth (an.) 1. A portal of entry into a building, room, or vehicle, typically consisting of a rigid plane movable on a hinge. 2. (Archaic or Toponymic) A shallow place in a river or stream allowing one to move across. 3. A connection, real or abstract. 4. (In diplomacy) A statement, such as an offer, that signals a possibility of accord. 5. (In writing) A mark in the shape of a dot used to mark the boundaries between words.
Iēt, Iētet, Iētkūs, Iet, Ieto, Ietened 1. To cross a body of water at a shallow place.

Ietkar, Ietkres, Ietker, Ietkreth (an.) 1. A flat buoyant structure of timber or other materials fastened together, used as a boat or floating platform. 2. A boat or ship for conveying passengers and goods, especially over a relatively short distance and as a regular service. 3. A flat-bottomed boat or or other floating structure used with others to support a temporary bridge or floating dock.
1. (Adverbial Allative) To or in the outdoors or outside; to or in an area that is beyond the scope, limits or borders of a given place.
Ietūk, Ietūkis, Ietūki, Ietūkith (an.) 1. A person employed to control who goes through an entrance.

Īger, Īgrot, Igerkūs, Iger, Igro, Geroned 1. To carry something (typically on one's back or in the womb). 2. To carry something on one's person. 3. (Figurative) To carry or bear something metaphorically. 4. To experience or be subjected to something. (Typcially something bad or unpleasant) 5. To accept or endure something with patience.

words.loc: Appear, Present, Introduce, Bring Out, Bring Forth
words.def: Īgrob, Igrōbet, Igrōbgūs, Igrob, Grobo, Geroned 1. To come into sight or become visible or noticeable. 2. To bring someone or something into the presence of someone or something else. 3. To formally introduce. 4. To bring something forwards or outwards.
Iim, Iimae, Iimath (inan.) 1. A hard, brittle substance, typically transparent or translucent, made by fusing sand with soda, lime, and sometimes other ingredients and cooling rapidly.

words.loc: Glassy, Translucent, Semitransparent, Pellucid, High, High-Pitched, Shrill
words.def: Iimauk, Iimaukh, Iimauk, Iimauketh (an.) Iimauk, Iimaukas, Iimaukae, Iimaukath (inan.) 1. Of or resembling glass. 2. Allowing light, but not detailed shapes, to pass through. 3. (Of sounds) High in pitch.
Iimdān, Iimdānae, Iimdānath (inan.) 1. A person that makes or sells glass or glassware.

words.loc: Sandy, Powdery, Granular, Grainy, Gritty, Crumbly, Friable, Floury, Mealy, Cornmealy, Dry, Bitter, Sharp, Tart
words.def: Ikael, Ikaeles, Ikael, Ikaeleth (an.) Ikael, Ikaelas, Ikaelae, Ikaelath (inan.) 1. Covered in or mostly consisting of sand. 2. Consisting of or resembling powder. 3. Consisting of or easily breaking into small fragments. 4. (Of bread) Tasting of dough or meal. 5. (Of wine) Not sweet.

words.loc: Short, Small, Little, Tiny, Miniscule, Shallow, Brief, Fleeting, Low, Subordinate, Inferior
words.def: Iket, Iktes, Ikte, Ikteth (an.) Iket, Iktas, Iktae, Iktath (inan.) 1. Measuring a small distance from end to end. 2. (Of a person) Of comparatively small height. 3. (Of water) Of little depth. 4. (Of time) Having little duration. 5. Ranking below other people or things in importance or class.

Ikh, Ikis, Ikthen (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. Rock that is ground more finely than gravel, but is not as fine as silt. 2. Sand that originates from the desert.
Īlen, Īlnit, Īlohūs, Ilen, Ilno, Ilnined 1. To make sweet or sweeter. 2. To cause to have a pleasant odor.

words.loc: Sweet, Sugary, Saccharine, Fragrant, Aromatic, Fruit, Berry, Breasts, Tits, Boobs, Bosom
words.def: Ilon, Ilōs, Ilen, Iloneth (an.) Ilen, Ilnas, Ilnae, Ilnath (inan.) 1. Tasting of sugars. 2. Having a pleasant or sweet smell. 3. (Substantive Animate) The sweet and fleshy product of a tree or other plant that contains seed and can be eaten as food. 4. (Substantive Animate Plural; Vulgar slang) The two soft, protuding organs on the upper front of a woman's body that secrete milk.
Imba, Imbae, Imbāth (inan.) 1. A woman who gives birth to a child. 2. A female parent.
1. (GEN) Moving in one side and out the other side of something. 2. (GEN) Throughout the course or duration of a period of time.

words.loc: Metropole, Mother Country, Mother State, Mother City, Capital City, Metropolis, Home Town, Motherland
words.def: Imbāgos, Imbāgosh, Imbāger, Imbāgreth (an.) 1. The parent state or city of a colony. 2. A city that is a seat of government. 3. The town or region of one's birth or early life or of one's present fixed residence.
Imbīn, Imbīs, Imbīn, Imbīneth (an.) Imbīn, Imbīnas, Imbīnae, Imbīnath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to a mother. 2. Behaving as or characteristic of a mother.

words.loc: Strange, Unusual, Unfamiliar, Odd, Peculiar, Exotic, Foreign, Alien, Stranger, Outsider, Foreigner
words.def: Iobor, Iobres, Iober, Ioboreth (an.) Iober, Iobras, Iobrae, Iobrath (inan.) 1. Unusual or surprising in a way that is unsettling or hard to understand. 2. Originating in or characteristic of a distant foreign country. 3. (Substantive Animate) A person whom one does not know or with whom one is not familiar.
1. (Interrogative; Literary) To what place.
Ioph, Iophae, Iophath (inan.) 1. Frozen water.

Iophauk, Iophaukh, Iophauk, Iophauketh (an.) Iophauk, Iophaukas, Iophaukae, Iophaukath (inan.) 1. Pertaining to, resembling, or abounding in ice. 2. Covered with ice, wholly or partially. 3. Having undergone the process of freezing.
Iorauk, Ioraukh, Iorauk, Iorauketh (an.) Iorauk, Ioraukas, Ioraukae, Ioraukath (inan.) 1. Of or relating to a numen or minor deity. 2. Relating to the spiritual world.

words.loc: Minor God, Minor Deity, Numen, Spirit
words.def: Ios, Iosh, Ior, Ioseth (an.) 1. A local, minor deity, as opposed to the Kembar. 2. An influence or force supernatural in origin.
Ira, Irae, Irāth (inan.) 1. The time when the Sun is below the horizon and the sky is dark. 2. The state of being dark as a result of it being nighttime.
Irād, Irādae, Irādath (inan.) 1. A period of twenty-four hours as a unit of time, reckoned from one midnight to the next, corresponding to a rotation of Nimlad on its axis.
Ith, Ithae, Ithath (inan.) 1. The hard whitish tissue making up the skeleton in humans and other vertebrates.

words.loc: Yellow, Pale, Amber, Lemon, Saffron, Light, Faint, Pallid, Wan, Frothy, Foamy, Gold
words.def: Iud, Iudes, Iude, Iudeth (an.) Iud, Iudas, Iudae, Iudath (inan.) 1. Of the color between green and orange, as of ripe lemons or egg yolks. 2. Being light or pale in color or having little color. 3. (Of human skin, typically the face) Deficiency of color, especially as a sign of sickness or distress. 4. (Of water) Full of or covered with a mass of small bubbles. 5. (Of gold) Low quality or impure (as opposed to rhūs, high-quality gold).

words.loc: Pale, Blanch, Fade, Dim, Dwindle, Wane, Disappear, Vanish
words.def: Iud Khān, Iud Khāhūs, Iud Khāne, Iud Kān, Iud Kāno, Iud Kāned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: This verb is fully ergative, with ergative alignment being used in all instances. Case frames will still be given along with the definition for the sake of completeness, however. 1. (Idiomatic; lit. to turn pale) To become pale in the face from shock or fear. 2. (Idiomatic; lit. to turn pale) To gradually grow faint and disappear.
1. (LOC) Within.
1. (Interrogative) To what degree or extent.
1. (Interrogative) At what time.

Kadar, Kadrae, Kadrath (inan.) 1. The influence a person acquires throughout his life, including personal reputation, moral standing, and ethical worth, along with the person's entitlement to respect owing to the standing of his family.

words.loc: Urban, Metropolitan, Municipal, Civic, Public, Elegant, Refined, Sophisticated, Politics, Government, Affairs of State, Public Matters
words.def: Kadrīn, Kadrīs, Kadrīn, Kadrīneth (an.) Kadrīn, Kadrīnas, Kadrīnae, Kadrīnath (inan.) 1. In, relating to, or characteristic of a town or city. 2. Relating to the administration of a city or town. 3. (Of speech) Pleasingly graceful and stylish in manner. 4. (Substantive Inanimate; In the Singular) The activities associated with the governance of a country or other area, especially the debate or conflict among individuals or parties having or hoping to achieve power. 5. (Substantive Inanimate; In the Plural) Matters that the government of a state or city deals with.

words.loc: City Life, Urbanity, Politeness, Sophistication, Refinement
words.def: Kadrīnāsh, Kadrīnāshae, Kadrīnāshath (inan.) 1. The lifestyle and activities of people living in a city. 2. Suavity, courteousness, and refinement of manner.

words.loc: Citizen, Freeman, Subject, National, Compatriot, Countryman
words.def: Kadrūk, Kadrūkis, Kadrūki, Kadrūkith (an.) 1. A legally recognized subject or national of a state or city, either native or naturalized. 2. A fellow citizen or national of a country.
Kadrūkus, Kadrūkurae, Kadrūkurath (inan.) 1. The position or status of being a citizen of a particular state or city.
1. (LOC) Used in comparisons to denote the standard of comparison. 2. (With Superlative Adjectives) To the greatest extent or degree possible.

words.loc: Throw, Hurl, Toss, Fling, Pitch, Launch, Cast, Mold, Forge
words.def: Khaet, Khaetkūs, Khaete, Kaet, Kaeto, Kaetened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To propel something with force through the air by a movement of the arm and hand. 2. To shape metal or other material by pouring it into a mold while molten. 3. To cause light or shadow to appear on a surface.

words.loc: Discard, Scrap, Reject, Dispose Of, Throw Away, Throw Out, Get Rid Of
words.def: Khaetel, Khaetelet, Khaetelgūs, Kaetel, Kaetelo, Kaetelened 1. Get rid of someone or something as no longer useful or desirable.
Khaeteph, Khaetphet, Khaetphekūs, Kaeteph, Kaetpho, Kaetphened 1. To put something on top of or in front of something, especially in order to protect or conceal it.
Kaetephek, Kaetephkae, Kaetephkath (inan.) 1. An outdoor overgarment, typically sleeveless, that hangs loosely from the shoulders.

words.loc: Inspire, Motivate, Encourage, Rouse, Animate, Move, Stir
words.def: Khaetesh, Khaetshet, Khaeteshkūs, Kaetesh, Kaetsho, Kaetshened 1. To fill someone with the urge or ability to do or feel something.

words.loc: Win, Triumph, Prevail, Succeed, Defeat, Beat, Best, Overcome, Conquer, Vanquish
words.def: Khaeth, Khaethet, Khaethe, Kaeth, Kaetho, Kaethened 1. (Intransitive) To be successful or victorious in something. 2. (Transitive) To win a victory over someone in a battle or other contest. (NOM defeat ACC) 3. (Transitive) To overcome and take control of a place or people by use of military force.
Kaetheskar, Kaetheskres, Kaethesker, Kaetheskreth (an.) 1. A person who defeats an enemy or opponent in a battle, game, or other competition. 2. A person who conquers a place or people.
Kaethespor, Kaethespres, Kaethesper, Kaethesporeth (an.) Kaethesper, Kaethespras, Kaethesprae, Kaethesprath (inan.) 1. Having won a victory.

1. A large arching structure, often made of stone, designed to commemorate military victories or important events, and often both marking the entrance to a city or town and featureing carvings, sculptures, or other decorative elements that celebrate the victory or event being honored
Kaethril, Kaethrilae, Kaethrilath (inan.) 1. The condition or state of having won a battle or competition, or having succeeded in an effort
Khaetob, Khaetobet, Khaetobgūs, Kaetob, Kaetobo, Kaetobened 1. To present or proffer something for someone to accept or reject as so desired.
Kaetphek, Kaetphekae, Kaetphekath (inan.) 1. A small pointed missile that can be thrown or shot. 2. An object which is forcibly propelled at a target, either by hand or from a weapon.
Kagat, Kagath, Kaged, Kagdath (an.) 1. A hardened rectangular block of mud, clay, etc. used for building. 2. A solid block of more or less pure metal, often bricklike in shape.
Kagdūk, Kagdūkis, Kagdūki, Kagdūkith (an.) 1. A person whose job is to build walls, houses, and other structures with bricks.
Kagphum, Giphmae, Kagphumath (inan.) 1. A small to medium-sized primate that typically has a long tail and a flat, dry nose. 2. A large non-human primate that lacks a tail and has a flat, dry nose.
Kamauk, Kamaukh, Kamauk, Kamauketh (an.) Kamauk, Kamaukas, Kamaukae, Kamaukath (inan.) 1. In, relating to, or characteristic of the countryside as opposed to the city.
Kami, Kamae, Kamīth (inan.) 1. An adult female of a domesticated breed of ox, kept to produce milk or beef. 2. A domestic bovine animal, regardless of sex or age.

words.loc: Shield, Aegis, Scutum, Pavise, Buckler, Tortoise
words.def: Kamīdo, Kamīdae, Kamīdōth (inan.) 1. A broad piece of metal or another suitable material, held by straps or a handle attached on one edge, used as a protection against blows or missiles. 2. A slow-moving, land-dwelling reptile whose body is enclosed in a domed shell into which it can retract its head and thick legs.

words.loc: Bovine, Taurine, Cowlike, Stupid, Slow, Dimwitted, Unintelligent
words.def: Kamīn, Kamīs, Kamīn, Kamīneth (an.) Kamīn, Kamīnas, Kamīnae, Kamīnath (inan.) 1. Relating to or resembling a cow or cattle. 2. (Of a person) Slow-moving and lacking in intelligence.
Karab, Karbae, Karbath (inan.) 1. A large, herbivorous mammal with solid hoofs and a flowing main and tail used for riding, racing, and to carry and pull loads.

Karan, Karās, Kaden, Karneth (an.) 1. A large, golden-brown colored cat that lives in prides, with the males having shaggy manes. 2. (Figurative) An individual that bears the perceived characteristics of lions: brave, noble, and savage.

Karko, Karkae, Karkōth (inan.) 1. A medicine or other substance used to treat an illness, relieve a symptom, or otherwise induce some effect in the body for a specific purpose. 2. Any plant with leaves, seeds, or flowers used for medicinal purposes.

words.loc: Grassy, Green, Virescent, Light Green, Lime Green, Yellow-Green, Chartreuse
words.def: Karkīn, Karkīs, Karkīn, Karkīneth (an.) Karkīn, Karkīs, Karkīnae, Karkīnath (inan.) NOTE: Considered by some to be a shade of rabash 'blue, green'. 1. Covered with grass. 2. Resembling, relating to, or characteristic of grass. 3. Of the color between blue and yellow in the spectrum, as of grass or emeralds. 3. Of a light or pale green in color.
Karkōban, Karkōbanae, Karkōbanath (inan.) 1. (Literary) An expanse of grass.
Karkōbu, Karkōbūs, Karkōbu, Karkōbūth (an.) 1. A single leaf of a grass plant.

words.loc: Dry Grass, Hay, Feed, Fodder, Forage, Provender, Pasture, Herbage
words.def: Karkōg, Karkōgae, Karkōgath (inan.) 1. Grass that is stiff, dry, and typically yellowed. 2. Food, especially dried hay or feed, for domestic animals.
Karkōgis, Karkōgish, Karkōgir, Karkōgiseth (an.) 1. A plant-eating insect with long hind legs that are used for jumping and for producing a chirping sound.
Karkōgūk, Karkōgūkis, Karkōgūki, Karkōgūkith (an.) 1. A person who sells and trades in hay.

words.loc: Joy, Happiness, Gladness, Cheer, Happy, Joyful, Glad, Cheerful, Fertile, Lush, Rich
words.def: Karlaen, Karlaes, Karlaen, Karlaeneth (an.) Karlaen, Karlaenas, Karlaenae, Karlaenath (inan.) 1. (Substantive Inanimate) A feeling of pleasure or contentment. 2. (Of land or soil) Capable of growing abundant crops.

words.loc: Interior, Inside, Inland, Center, Heartland, Hinterland
words.def: Kaugos, Kaugosh, Kauger, Kaugreth (inan.) 1. The inside of a building, container, cavern, or other enclosed structure. 2. The inside regions of a country or region, distanced from the borders or coasts.
1. (Adverbial Allative) Within or towards the interior of something; within the scope or limits of something (a place), especially a building.

words.loc: Middle, Mid, Medial, Central, Midway, Halfway, Equidistant
words.def: Kauk, Kaukh, Kauk, Kauketh (an.) Kauk, Kaukas, Kaukae, Kaukath (inan.) 1. At an equal distance from the extremities of something
Kebar, Kebrae, Kebrath (inan.) 1. The world or the state of things as they actually exist, as opposed to an idealistic or notional idea of them.
1. In an immediate manner.

Kebesh, Kebshae, Kebshath (inan.) 1. A structure that prevents movement or access. 2. An improvised barrier erected across a street or other thoroughfare to prevent or delay the movement of opposing forces.

words.loc: Steward, Custodian, Caretaker, Magistrate, Judge, Reeve
words.def: Kebgar, Kebgres, Kebger, Kebgareth (an.) 1. A person employed to manage another's property, especially a large house or estate. 2. A civil officer or judge who administers the law, especially one who conducts a court that deals with minor offenses or is largely rural.
Kebil, Kebilae, Kebilath (inan.) 1. The relative social, professional, or other standing of someone or something.

words.loc: Block, Obstruct, Plug, Jam, Seal, Dam
words.def: Khabob, Khabet, Khabobgūs, Kebob, Kebo, Kebened 1. To make the movement or flow through something difficult or impossible.

words.loc: Certain, Sure, Positive, Definite, Indisputable, Undeniable, Irrefutable
words.def: Kebor, Kebres, Keber, Keboreth (an.) Keber, Kebras, Kebrae, Kebrath (inan.) 1. Absolutely confident in truth of something. 2. Known for sure.
Kebus, Kebrae, Kebrath (inan.) 1. The quality or state of being true. 2. A thing that is known or proved to be true.

words.loc: Stable, Steady, Firm, Fixed, Secure, Anchored, Moored
words.def: Kebīn, Kebīs, Kebīn, Kebīneth (an.) Kebīn, Kebīnas, Kebīnae, Kebīnath (inan.) 1. Firmly fixed, supported, or balanced; not shaking or moving. 2. (Of boats) Fixed in place either by an anchor on the sea floor or by cables attached to the shore or dock.
Kebīnāsh, Kebīnāshae, Kebīnāshāth (inan.) 1. The state of being stable.

Kem, Kemae, Kemath (inan.) 1. A substance, such as mud or dust, that soils something. 2. The upper layer of the world in which plants grow; A black or dark brown material typically consisting of a mixture of organic remains, clay, and rock particles.

words.loc: Mote, Mote of Dust, Fleck of Dirt, Atom
words.def: Kembu, Kembūs, Kembu, Kembūth (an.) 1. A tiny piece of a substance. 2. A very small piece of dust or dirt. 3. (Technical) The basic unit of a chemical or alchemical element.

words.loc: Think, Ponder, Reflect, Consider, Contemplate, Muse
words.def: Khen, Khehūs, Khene, Ken, Keno, Kened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: Khen, ken is used in reference to the concept of pondering something. See ūneba, umba "feel, think" for a verb meaning "to have an opinion". 1. To direct one's mind toward someone or something. 2. To have some statement in one's mind.
Kened, Kendas, Kendae, Kendath (inan.) 1. (Substantive Inanimate) An idea or opinion produced by thinking, or occurring suddenly in the mind.

words.loc: Lead, Guide, Steer, Direct, Command, Captain, Head, Oversee, Manage, Mentor, Coach, Instruct, Train, Teach
words.def: Kheph, Khephet, Khephkūs, Keph, Kepho, Kephened 1. To guide or conduct with the hand, or by means of some physical contact connection. 2. To guide or conduct in a certain course, or to a certain place or end, by making the way known. 3. To conduct or direct with authority; to have direction or charge of; to command, especially a military or business unit. 4. To act as a counselor or teacher to another.

words.loc: Honorable, Decent, Righteous, Noble, Gracious, Compassionate, Kind, Merciful
words.def: Kephen, Kephenos, Kephen, Kephenoth (an.) Kephen, Kephenas, Kephenae, Kephenath (inan.) 1. Behaving in a manner that shows integrity, honor, and decency. 2. Moral and virtuous. 3. Kind, warmly courteous, tactful, and compassionate. 4. Showing mercy.
Kham, Khamae, Khamath (inan.) 1. A barrier, railing, or other upright structure enclosing an area of ground to mark a boundary, control access, or prevent escape.

words.loc: House, Home, Abode, Lodgings, Residence, Habitation
words.def: Khar, Kharae, Kharath (inan.) NOTE: This noun takes the allative case. 1. A building for human habitation, especially one that is lived in by a family or small group of people. 2. The place where one lives permanently, especially as a member of a family or household.

words.loc: Tame, Domesticate, Break In, Train, Master, Enslave, Subjugate
words.def: Kekhar, Kekhret, Kekhargūs, Khar, Kharo, Kharened 1. (Of animals) To make tame. 2. (Of people) To make into a slave.

Khardum, Khardūs, Khardum, Khardumeth (an.) 1. A heavy plant-eating animal with a prehensile trunk, long curved ivory tusks, and large ears. 2. A hard creamy-white substance composing the main part of the tusks of an elephant.

Kharened, Kharendis, Kharendi, Kharendith (an.) Kharened, Kharendas, Kharendae, Kharendath (inan.) 1. (Of animals) Tamed and kept as a pet or on a farm. 2. (Of plants) Cultivated for food or other resources
Kharos, Kharosh, Kharor, Kharoseth (an.) 1. (Substantive Animate) A person who trains animals or slaves.

Khidi, Khidae, Khidīth (inan.) 1. A structure spanning a body of water, ravine, valley, road or other obstacle from an elevated height, allowing for the passage of people and vehicles. 2. (Nautical) The floorlike covering of the horizontal section of a ship.

Khop, Khoph, Khop, Khopeth (an.) Khop, Khopas, Khopae, Khopath (inan.) 1. Taking a long time to move or go a short distance, or to perform an action. 2. Doing something or taking place after the expected, proper, or usual time. 3. Belonging or taking place near the end of a particular time or period.

words.loc: Slow, Slow Down, Decelerate, Lag, Tarry, Fall Behind, Delay, Detain, Hinder, Hamper, Impede, Stall, Retard
words.def: 1. (Intransitive) To reduce one's speed. 2. (Intransitive) To fall behind in movement, progress, or development. 2. (Transitive) To make someone or something late or slow.

words.loc: Train, Procession, Line, File, Column, Caravan, Convoy
words.def: Khophesh, Khophshae, Khophshath (inan.) 1. A succession of vehicles, pack animals, or people traveling in the same direction.

words.loc: Pile, Stack, Heap, Mound, Make Feel, Move, Stir, Gladden, Sadden, Anger, Bury, Inter, Entomb
words.def: Okhuz, Okhuzut, Okhuzgūs, Khuz, Khuzut, Khuzuned 1. To place things atop one another. 2. To provoke an emotion. 3. To ritualistically place in a grave or tomb.

words.loc: Pile, Heap, Stack, Mound, Rampart, Motte, Hill, Knoll, Hillock, Barrow, How, Tomb, Grave
words.def: Khuzul, Khuzulae, Khuzulath (inan.) 1. A collection of things laid or lying on top of each other. 2. An artificial hill or elevation of earth. 3. A place of burial for a dead body, typically underground.
Kekhzhud, Kekhzhudit, Kekhzhudgūs, Khēzhed, Khēzhdo, Khēzhdined 1. To move hurriedly or furtively with short, quick steps.
Khēzhud, Khēzhdae, Khēzhdath (inan.) 1. An insect distinguished by forewings typically modified into hard wing cases that cover and protect the hind wings and abdomen.

Khōg, Khōgae, Khōgath (inan.) 1. The place where a river flows into a larger body of water. (Typically used when flowing into a sea, bay, or large lake) 2. A triangular tract of sediment deposited at the mouth of a river, typically where it diverges into multiple outlets.

words.loc: River, Tributary River, Current, Blood Vessel
words.def: Khōr, Khōres, Khōr, Khōreth (an.) 1. A small river (as opposed to negril, a large river). 2. A river or stream flowing into a larger river or lake. 3. A body of water or air moving in a definite direction, especially through a surrounding body of water or air in which there is less movement. 4. A tubular structure which carries blood.

words.loc: Aim For, Make For, Head For, Seek, Strive, Try, Attempt, Endeavor, Aspire, Aim At
words.def: Khendar, Khendaret, Khendargūs, Kindar, Kindaro, Kindarened 1. To have the intention of achieving or reaching. 2. To set out to go somewhere. 3. To point or direct something at a target.
Kinik, Kihae, Kihath (inan.) 1. A small piece of shaped metal with incisions cut to fit the wards of a particular lock, which is inserted into a lock and turned to open or close it.

words.loc: Empty, Vacant, Unoccupied, Uninhabited, Clear, Hollow, Zero, Nil, Nought
words.def: Kish, Kish, Kish, Kisheth (an.) Kish, Kishas, Kishae, Kishath (inan.) 1. Containing nothing; not filled or occupied. 2. Having a hole or empty space inside. 3. (Substantive Inanimate) No quantity or number.

words.loc: Root System, Rootage, Colony, Settlement, Outpost
words.def: Kohabar, Kohabrae, Kohabrath (inan.) 1. The network of roots belonging to a plant. 2. An area under the political control or influence of another country and typically occupied by settlers from it, or by their descendants.
Kohabrīn, Kohabrīs, Kohabrīn, Kohabrīneth (an.) Kohabrīn, Kohabrīnas, Kohabrīnae, Kohabrīnath (inan.) 1. Relating to or characteristic of a colony or colonies.
Kekohae, Kekohaet, Kekħaekūs, Kohae, Koho, Kohaened 1. (Of a country or its citizens) To send a group of settlers to a place and establish political control over it.

words.loc: Necessary, Needed, Vital, Essential, Crucial, Critical, Pivotal, Key
words.def: Kohager, Kohagros, Kohager, Kohageroth (an.) Kohager, Kohagras, Kohagrae, Kohagrath (inan.) 1. Required to be done, achieved, or present. 2. Extremely important.
Kohūk, Kohūkis, Kohūki, Kohūkith (an.) 1. A settler in or inhabitant of a colony.

words.loc: Ask About, Question, Inquire, Query, Interrogate, Seek, Search, Look
words.def: Kekel, Keklet, Keglekūs, Kol, Kolo, Kolened 1. To ask for information about something. 2. To try to find. 3. To direct one's gaze towards someone or something or in a specified direction. 3. To look in a place for something.
Kondonūs, Kondonumesh, Kondonumer, Kondonūseth (an.) 1. A large marine or semiaquatic reptile with a domed shell.
1. (LOC) Located or situated on every side of something. 2. (GEN) So as to pass something or someone in a curved or circular route.

words.loc: Ash, Slag, Chaff, Dross, Debris, Detritus, Rubbish, Trash, Garbage, Junk
words.def: Kub, Kubae, Kubath (inan.) 1. The powdery residue left after the burning of a substance. 2. Stony waste matter separated from metals after the smelting or refining of ore. 3. The inedible parts of a grain-producing plant. 4. (Figurative) Something regarded as worthless.

words.loc: Leader, Director, Head, Commander, Captain, General, King, Chief, Ruler, Guide, Conductor
words.def: Kuphūk, Kuphūkis, Kuphūki, Kuphūkith (an.) 1. Any person who leads or directs.

words.loc: Become, Begin to be, Inchoative Aspect
words.def: Khān, Khāhūs, Khāne, Kān, Kāno, Kāned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: This verb is fully ergative, with ergative alignment being used in all instances. Case frames will still be given along with the definition for the sake of completeness, however. 1. (Copulative) To begin to be; To turn into. 2. (Auxiliary) Denotes the beginning of the action expressed by the lexical verb.
Kārel, Kārlas, Kārlae, Kārlath (an.) Kārel, Kārlās, Kārla, Kārlāth (inan.) 1. Of or at a somewhat low temperature.

words.loc: Cold, Chilly, Tired, Weary, Fatigued, Frail, Sickly, Languid
words.def: Kās, Kāsh, Kār, Kāseth (an.) Kār, Kāras, Kārae, Kārath (inan.) 1. Of or at a low temperature 2. Weak or faint from illness or fatigue.

words.loc: Fight, Battle, Combat, Brawl, Box, Wrestle, Shave, Shear, Clip
words.def: Kegmes, Kegmeret, Kegmeskūs, Kēs, Kēro, Kērened 1. To take part in a violent struggle involving the exchange of physical blows or the use of weapons. 2. To cut the hair off oneself. 3. To cut the wool off a sheep or other animal.
Kēskar, Kēskres, Kēsker, Kēskreth (an.) 1. A person who cuts hair, especially men's, and shaves or trims beards as an occupation.

Kēsphek, Kēsphekae, Kēsphekath (inan.) 1. A cutting instrument in which two blades move past each other. 2. An instrument with a sharp blade or combination of blades, used to remove hair from the face or body.
Khī, Khihet, Khihūs, Kī, Kihet, Kihened 1. To fasten or secure something with a lock.
Kīdmūs, Kīdmūsh, Kīdmūr, Kīdmūseth (an.) Kīdmūr, Kīdmūras, Kīdmūrae, Kīdmūrath (inan) 1. Happening or done before the usual or expected time. 2. Happening or done in the morning.
Kīr, Kīrae, Kīrath (inan.) 1. The period of time between sunrise and noon.

Kō, Kohae, Kohath (inan.) 1. The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body and absorbs and stores water and nutrients. 2. The lowest load-bearing part of a building.
Kōron, Kōrnae, Kōrnath (inan.) 1. Meat that has been prepared as food by heating.
Kōrondān, Kōrondānae, Kōrondānath (inan.) 1. A person whose trade is cutting up and selling meat in a shop.

Laego, Laegae, Laegōth (inan.) 1. Freedom from disturbance; tranquility. 2. A state or period in which there is no war or a war has ended. 3. (Poetic) Relief from work or activity by sleeping or resting. 4. (Poetic) A state of comfort without pain or hardship.
Lelaego, Lelaegōt, Lelaegōkūs, Laego, Laego, Laegōned 1. To bring peace to (a place or situation) by means of ending war, fighting, violence, anger, or agitation.

words.loc: Peace-Bringing, Peaceful, Peace Treaty, Truce, Armistice
words.def: Laegōmūs, Laegōmūsh, Laegōmūr, Laegōmūseth (an.) Laegōmūs, Laegōmūras, Laegōmūrae, Laegōmūrath (inan.) 1. Not at war; not disturbed by strife or turmoil. 2. Inclined to peace. 3. (Substantive Inanimate) A legal agreement between enemies or opponents formally ending a state of war or conflict. 4. (Substantive Inanimate) An agreement between enemies or opponents to stop fighting or arguing for a certain period of time.
Laegōphek, Laegōphekae, Laegōphekath (inan.) 1. A person who brings about peace. 2. An independent person or body that settles a dispute.

words.loc: Silence, Quiet, Make Quiet, Be Quiet, Be Silent About, Be Quiet About, Stop, Suppress
words.def: Lelaeker, Lelaekrit, Lelaekrikūs, Laeker, Laekro, Laekrined 1. To prohibit or prevent from speaking. 2. To stop or suppress a noise or sound. 3. To refrain from talking about something. 4. (Reflexive) To be quiet. 5. (Figurative) To forcibly put an end to something.

words.loc: Quiet, Silent, Calm, Still, Tranquil, Serene
words.def: Laekos, Laekres, Laeker, Laekoreth (an.) Laeker, Laekras, Laekrae, Laekrath (inan.) 1. Not moving or making a sound. 2. Making little or no noise. 3. (Of the weather or environment) Pleasantly free from wind or turmoil.
1. In a way that produces little or no noise. 2. In a manner that shows no agitation or strong emotion.
Laekrāsh, Laekrāshae, Laekrāshath (inan.) 1. The absence of noise or sound.

words.loc: Season, Period, Time, Era, Age, Epoch, Eon, Span, Lifetime, Life, Life Span
words.def: Laer, Laerae, Laerath (inan.) 1. One of the three divisions of the year (winter, stirring, and low tide) marked by particular weather patterns and corresponding to the annual flooding pattern of the Lash River in Sārmath Land. 2. A long and distinct period of history with a particular feature or characteristic. 3. The duration of a person's life.

words.loc: Sense, Feeling, Sensation, Perception, Sight, Hearing, Smell, Touch, Taste
words.def: Lahar, Lohares, Lohar, Lohareth (an.) NOTE: In order to convey a meaning like "sense of sight", one must take the relevant verb, place it in the imperfective participle, and have it modify this noun (i.e. phernos lahar "seeing sense"). 1. Any of the manners by which living beings perceive the world, such as sight, smell, hearing, touch, or taste.
Lamon, Lamōs, Lamon, Lamoneth (an.) 1. A swift-running deerlike mammal native to Patrinor with smooth hair and upward-pointing horns.

words.loc: Feel, Touch, Sense, Mention, Touch On, Hint At, Bring Up, Hit, Strike, Punch, Beat, Smite, Bother, Annoy, Irritate
words.def: Lelen, Lelahūs, Lelne, Lan, Lano, Lanoned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Copulative) To become aware of through a sense of touch or through the skin. 2. To come into or be in physical contact with. 3. To perceive by a sense or senses. 4. To instinctively be aware of. 5. To refer to something briefly and without going into detail. 6. To bring one's hand or a tool or weapon into contact with someone or something quickly and forcefully. 7. (In negative constructions; Figurative) To make annoyed, impatient, or angry.

words.loc: News, Tidings, Report, Rumor, Talk, Gossip, Hearsay, Reputation
words.def: Lanondas, Lanondaes, Lanondathen (inan.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. (Substantive Inanimate) New information of interest, especially about recent or important events. 2. (Substantive Inanimate) Information received from others that one cannot adequately substantiate. 3. (Substantive Inanimate) The beliefs or opinions that are generally held about someone or something.
Lanīn, Lans, Lanīn, Lanīneth (an.) Lanīn, Lanīnas, Lanīnae, Lanīnath (inan.) 1. Actually existing as a thing or occurring in fact; not imagined or supposed. 2. Perceptible by touch.

words.loc: Beyond, On the Other Side Of, (Go) Past
words.def: 1. (LOC) To be at or on the further side of. 2. (GEN) To the further side of.

words.loc: Play Music, Perform Music, Sound, Blow
words.def: Lalur, Lalurit, Lalurgūs, Laur, Lauro, Laurined 1. To produce sound using an instrument.

words.loc: Musical, Melodic, Melodious, Pleasant-Sounding, Dulcet, Euphonious, Mellifluous
words.def: Laurīn, Laurīs, Laurīn, Laurīneth (an.) Laurīn, Laurīnas, Laurīnae, Laurīnath (inan.) 1. Relating to, set to, fond of, or skilled in music. 2. Having a pleasant sound.

Laus, Laurae, Laurath (inan.) 1. A series of sounds organized in time so as to produce beauty of form, harmony, and expression of emotion. 2. A short poem or other set of words set to music or meant to be sung.
Lauzbu, Lauzbūs, Lauzbu, Lauzbūth (an.) 1. A musical or vocal sound with reference to its pitch, quality, and strength.

words.loc: Dig, Mine, Quarry, Thrust, Jab, Stab, Spear, Impale, Poke, Prod, Wound
words.def: Ilaz, Ilazgūs, Ilaze, Laz, Lazo, Lazaned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To break up and move earth with a tool or machine, or with hands, paws, snout, etc. 2. To push or poke something in or into. 3. To extract stone or minerals from a mine or quarry. 4. To thrust a pointed weapon into someone or something so as to wound, damage, or kill.
Lazaned, Lazandas, Lazandae, Lazandath (inan.) 1. (Substantive Inanimate) A long, narrow, channel dug in the ground, especially if dug for military use or as part of a fortification.
Lazban, Lazbanae, Lazbanath (inan.) 1. An excavation in the earth for extracting stone, metal, or other minerals.

words.loc: Digger, Delver, Miner, Worker, Laborer, Gravedigger, Sapper, Combat Engineer
words.def: Lazdān, Lazdānae, Lazdānath (an.) 1. A person or animal that digs earth. 2. A person who works in a mine. 3. A person doing unskilled manual work for wages. 4. A person who digs graves. 5. A soldier responsible for tasks such as building and repairing roads and bridges, digging trenches, tunneling under fortifications, etc.
Lazgar, Lazgres, Lazger, Lazgreth (an.) 1. A weapon with a long shaft and a pointed tip, typically of metal, used for thrusting or throwing.
Lazgreduk, Lazgredukae, Lazgredukath (inan.) 1. The military arm that fights and marches on foot; foot soldiers collectively.
Lazgrus, Lazgrurae, Lazgrurath (inan.) 1. Service in an army or other military organisation, whether as a chosen job or as a result of an involuntary draft

words.loc: Spearman, Professional Soldier, Foot Soldier, Infantryman, Infantry
words.def: Lazgrūk, Lazgrūkis, Lazgrūki, Lazgrūkith (an.) 1. A person, especially a soldier, who uses a spear. 2. A person who serves in an army, especially if well-trained or belonging to the infantry.
Lazh, Lazhes, Lazhe, Lazheth (an.) 1. A low area or land between hills or mountains, especially when narrow or small.

words.loc: Desk, Work Surface, Lectern, Table, Counter
words.def: Legimban, Legimbanae, Legimbanath (inan.) 1. A piece of furniture with a flat or sloped surface on which one can read, write, and do other work.
Legimbesh, Legimbeshae, Legimbeshath (inan.) 1. The activity or skill of writing words and composing text. 2. The ability to both read and write.

Legimbor, Legimbres, Legimber, Legimbreth (an.) Legimber, Legimbras, Legimbrae, Legimbrath (inan.) 1. Having much learning and knowledge. 2. Characterized by refined taste, manners, and good education. 3. Able to both read and write.
Legimphek, Legimphekae, Legimphekath (inan.) 1. A person who has written a particular text. 2. A person who writes original texts as an occupation.

words.loc: Write, Write to, Record, Inscribe, Collaborate, Cooperate
words.def: Ūrsulae, Ūrsulaet, Ūrsulaekūs, Legin, Legno, Legined 1. To form letters, words, or symbols on a surface in order to communicate. 2. To compose, write, and send a message to someone. 3. To work jointly on an activity, especially to produce or create something.

Legined, Legindas, Legindae, Legindath (inan.) 1. (Substantive Inanimate) Anything that has been written. 2. (Substantive Inanimate) A written work, regarded in terms of its contents rather than its physical form.
Legnehar, Legneharas, Legnehar, Legnehareth (an.) 1. A person who keeps records, takes notes, and handles clerical work. 2. A person who copies written documents as a profession.
Lelgen, Lelgenet, Lelgehūs, Legnen, Lelgeno, Lelgened 1. To make a similar or identical version of.

Lek, Lekae, Lekath (inan.) 1. A wide way leading from one place to another, especially one with a specially prepared surface which vehicles can use. 2. A public road in a city or town, typically with houses and buildings on one or both sides.
1. Another time; once more.
Lektān, Lektānae, Lektānath (inan.) 1. A religious clergyman who is trained to perform services or sacrifices at a church or temple.

Len, Lenae, Lenath (inan.) 1. A precious or semiprecious stone, especially when cut and polished or engraved. 2. Personal ornaments, such as necklaces, rings, or bracelets, that are typically made from or contain jewels and precious metal.
Lendān, Lendānae, Lendānath (inan.) 1. A person that makes or sells jewels or jewelry.
Leph, Lepesh, Leper, Lepheth (an.) 1. A warm-blooded egg-laying vertebrate distinguished by the possession of feathers, wings, and a beak.

Lephdrāg, Lephdrāgae, Lephdrāgath (inan.) NOTE: To distinguish squid from cuttlefish, one must use the phrase 'thel lephdrāg' (long squid) for squid and 'kamīn lephdrāg' (cow squid) for cuttlefish. 1. An elongated, fast-swimming mollusk with ten limbs. 2. A swimming mollusk with ten limbs and an internal cuttlebone. 3. A dark brown ink or pigment made from the secretions of a cuttlefish.

words.loc: Dinosaur, Saurian, Theropod, Tyrannosaur, Dromaeosaur, Raptor, Spinosaur, Ornithomimosaur
words.def: Lepher, Lephras, Lephrae, Lephrath (an.) 1. Any member of the clade dinosauria other than birds. 2. A category of dinosaur whose members are typically bipedal, lack beaks or bills, and are carnivorous.

words.loc: Counsel, Advise, Recommend, Warn, Caution, Beware, Be Warned, Be Advised, Heed, Mind, Take Care
words.def: Lelker, Lelkrit, Lelkrikūs, Liker, Likro, Likrined 1. To give advice to. 2. To make someone aware of something impending. 3. To caution someone against unwise or unacceptable behavior. 4. (In the Passive) To be cautious, pay attention, or guard against a potential risk.

words.loc: Counsel, Advice, Guidance, Judgement, Wisdom, Plan, Intention, Design, Purpose, Scheme, Plot, Tactic, Stratagem, Maneuver
words.def: Likh, Likish, Liker, Likheth (an.) 1. An opinion offered to guide behavior in an effort to be helpful. 2. A set of intended actions, usually mutually related, through which one expects to achieve a goal. 3. (Of militaries) A planned movement or approach used against an enemy.
Leler, Lelret, Lelorgūs, Lor, Loro, Lorened 1. To direct one's gaze toward someone or something or in a specified direction.
1. Used to draw attention to an interesting event or point out something with emphasis.

words.loc: Climb, Ascend, Mount, Straddle, Climb On, Get On
words.def: Lelot, Lelotkūs, Lelte, Lot, Loto, Lotened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: This verb is used chiefly when climbing gentle slopes. Use sereth, seth "climb" instead when referring to steep, vertical slopes. 1. To go or come up a slope or other rising surface on an incline, especially by means of one's feet and sometimes hands. 2. To get on an animal in order to ride it.
Lotphek, Lotephkae, Lotephkath (inan.) 1. A structure consisting of a series of bars or steps between two upright lengths of wood, metal, or rope, used for climbing up or down something.

Lugal, Lugles, Lugel, Lugaleth (an.) 1. A generally tubular invertebrate of or resembling annelids. 2. Any of a number of creeping or burrowing invertebrates with long, slender bodies and no limbs. 3. (Poetic) A long, limbless reptile.

words.loc: Wormridden, Worm-eaten, Full of worms, Worn, Shabby, Run down, Tattered, Threadbare
words.def: Lugalger, Lugalgros, Lugalger, Lugalgeroth (an.) Lugalger, Lugalgras, Lugalgrae, Lugalgrath (inan.) 1. Full of or parasitized by worms. 2. Eaten into by worms or termites 3. (Figurative) Damaged or aged to the point of being in poor condition or longer being useable. 4. (Figurative; Of clothing) Old or torn; in poor condition.

words.loc: Wormy, Worm-like, Vermiculated, Lined, Striped, Stripey
words.def: Luglauk, Luglaukh, Luglauk, Luglauketh (an.) Luglauk, Luglaukas, Luglaukae, Luglaukath (inan.) 1. Of or like a worm or worms; shaped like a worm or worms 2. Patterned with sinuous, wavy, worm-like lines
Lugreg, Lugreges, Lugreg, Lugregeth (an.) 1. A soft-boddied, typically legless, juvenile insect, especially that of a fly found in decaying matter.
Lugrūk, Lugrūkis, Lugrūki, Lugrūkith (an.) 1. One who offers advice.
Phatic Expression 1. Used as an expression of gratitude or politeness in response to something done or given.
Līrauk, Līraukh, Līrauk, Līrauketh (an.) Līrauk, Līraukas, Līraukae, Līraukath (inan.) 1. (Of weather, a period of time, or an area) Having a great deal of rainfall.

words.loc: Rain, Rainfall, Precipitation, Downpour, Shower, Sea Spray, Spindrift
words.def: Līs, Līrae, Līrath (inan.) 1. Moisture condensed from the atmosphere that falls visibly in separate drops. 2. Spray blown from the crests of waves by the wind.
Līzbu, Līzbūs, Līzbu, Līzbūth (an.) 1. A small round or pear-shaped portion of liquid that hangs or falls or adheres to a surface. 2. A single drop of rain.

words.loc: Rainbow, Halo, Nimbus, Corona, Aura, Air, Vibe, Feeling, Mood, Spirit
words.def: Līzet, Līzeth, Līzet, Līzeteth (an.) 1. An arch of colors formed in the sky in certain circumstances, caused by the refraction and dispersion of the sun's light by rain or other water droplets in the atmosphere. 2. A circle of white or colored light around the sun, moon, or other luminous body caused by refraction through ice crystals in the atmosphere. 3. A distinctive atmosphere or quality surrounding or associated with something or someone.

words.loc: Say, Speak, Tell, Utter, Declare, State, Curse, Cham, Hex, Jinx, Bewitch, Ensorcell
words.def: Lelōk, Lelōkūs, Lelōke, Lōk, Lōko, Lōkened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Intransitive) To utter words so as to convey something. 2. (Transitive) To convey something to someone with speech. 3. To invoke or use a curse against.
Lōkar, Lōkres, Lōker, Lōkreth (an.) 1. A person who speaks. 2. A person who delivers a speech or lecture. 3. A public speaker, especially one who is eloquent or skilled.
Lelōkgrud, Lelōkgrudet, Lelōkgrudkūs, Lōkgrud, Lōkgrudo, Lōkgrudned 1. To express disagreement with something by means of argument. 2. To deny the truth or validity of a statement or statements.
Lōkgrudil, Lōkgrudlae, Lōkgrudlath (inan.) 1. The act of contradicting. 2. A statement that contradicts itself. 3. A logical inconsistency between two or more elements or propositions.

words.loc: Speech, Speaking, Talking, Articulation, Address, Oration, Lecture, Discourse, Utterance
words.def: Lōkil, Lōklae, Lōklath (inan.) 1. The expression of or the ability to speak. 2. A formal address delivered to an audience. 3. Written or spoken communication or debate. 4. A spoken word, statement, or sound.

words.loc: Recount, Narrate, Report, Describe, Relate, Repeat, Retell
words.def: Lelōkimbar, Lelōkimbret, Lelōkimbarkūs, Lōkimbar, Lōkimbro, Lōkimbrened 1. To relate a story or series of events in speech or writing. 2. To relate the details of. 3. To repeat something one has heard.
Lelōknūs, Lelōknūret, Lelōknuskūs, Lōknūs, Lōknūro, Lōknūsened 1. To speak to a person or assembly, typically in a formal way. 2. To welcome in a friendly manner.
Lūrūk, Lūrūkis, Lūrūki, Lūrūkith (an.) 1. A person who plays a musical instrument, especially as a profession. 2. A person who is musically talented.

words.loc: Purple, Violet, Burgundy, Magenta, Fuschia, Lilac, Plum, Lavender
words.def: Mahael, Mahaeles, Mahael, Mahaeleth (an.) Mahael, Mahaelas, Mahaelae, Mahaelath (inan.) 1. Of a color between red and blue, as of grapes or lavender.
Mahar, Mahares, Mahar, Mahareth (an.) 1. One who gives. 2. One who makes a donation. 3. An influential, wealthy individual who financially supports an artist, craftsman, scholar, or other noble.

Emahorum, Emahormet, Emakruhūs, Mahorum, Mahormo, Mahormened 1. To throw in various random directions. 2. To cover a surface with objects thrown or spread randomly over it. 3. To plant seeds by scattered them across the ground. 4. To share pieces of something among a group of people. 5. (Of ideas, principles, opinions, information, etc.) To spread widely. 6. To make widely known to the public.

words.loc: Help, Aid, Assist, Avail, Use, Utilize, Employ
words.def: Mehīn, Mehīhūs, Mehīne, Makīn, Makīno, Makīned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: The participial forms of this verb can mean either "help" or "use", depending on context. 1. To provide assistance to. 2. (In the participial forms) To take, hold, or deploy something as a means of accomplishing a purpose or achieving a result.

words.loc: Listen To, Hear, Attend, Hark, Hearken, Heed, Mark, Pay Attention To
words.def: Melen, Melnet, Malahūs, Malen, Malno, Malnoned 1. To give attention to a sound.
Mambor, Mambres, Mamber, Mamboreth (an.) Mambor, Mambras, Mambrae, Mamborath (inan.) 1. Showing a readiness to give and share more than is expected or needed.
Māpher, Māphret, Māphergūs, Māper, Mābro, Mābrened 1. To look steadily and intently.

words.loc: Summon, Send For, Call On, Invoke, Appeal To, Read, Repeat, Recite, Cite, Quote
words.def: Memer, Memret, Memrekūs, Mar, Maro, Marened 1. To authoritatively or urgently call on someone to be present. 2. To call on a deity or spirit in prayer, as a witness, for inspiration, or for an urgent or serious request. 3. To look at and interpret letters or other information that is written. 4. To speak aloud words or other information that is written, especially when from memory.

words.loc: Beautiful, Pretty, Handsome, Fair, Comely, Attractive, Good-Looking
words.def: Mara, Marās, Mara, Marāth (an.) Mara, Marās, Marae, Marāth (inan.) 1. Attractive and pleasant to the sight or senses.

words.loc: Chapter, Reading, Recitation, Passage, Excerpt, Quotation
words.def: Marbesh, Marbeshae, Marbeshath (inan.) 1. A main division of a book, typically with a number or title. 2. A piece of literature read aloud to a group of people.
Mardān, Mardānae, Mardānath (inan.) 1. A person who makes or sells archers' bows.

words.loc: Reader, Teacher, Tutor, Instructor, Pedagogue, Educator
words.def: Markar, Markres, Marker, Markreth (an.) 1. A person who reads or is fond of reading. 2. A private teacher, typically one who teaches a single student or a small group.
Marāsh, Marāshae, Marāshath (inan.) 1. The quality of being beautiful.
Marēl, Marēlae, Marēlath (inan.) 1. An authoritative or urgent call to someone to be present or do something. 2. A written or verbal request inviting someone to go somewhere or to do something.

words.loc: Close, Near, Adjacent, At Hand, Imminent, Available, Free, Unoccupied, Reachable, Accessible, Obtainable
words.def: Marīn, Marīs, Marīn, Marīneth (an.) Marīn, Marīnas, Marīnae, Marīnath (inan.) NOTE: See 'praer' for the comparative and superlative grades. 1. A short distance away or apart in space or time. 2. (Of a person) Not otherwise occupied or free to do something. 3. Able to be used or obtained.

words.loc: Join, Unite, Link, Connect, Fasten, Fix, Yoke, Harness, Attach
words.def: Mendak, Mendaket, Mendakūs, Matak, Matko, Matkened 1. To connect or combine into one. 2. To fasten something securely in a particular place or position.

words.loc: Draft Animal, Beast of Burden, Working Animal
words.def: Matkos, Matkosh, Matkor, Matkoseth (an.) 1. (Substantive Animate) Any domesticated animal used in drawing heavy loads.

words.loc: Host, Horde, Army, Mob, Multitude, Throng, Crowd
words.def: Maur, Maurae, Maurath (inan.) 1. (Derogatory) A large group of people. 2. (Of enemies or opponents) A large group of people arrayed as an army.

words.loc: Count, Enumerate, Calculate, Have, Comprise, Span, Attribute, Tell, Say, Speak, Narrate, Recount, Declare, Announce, Proclaim, Prophesy
words.def: Mēz, Māzukūs, Mēze, Maz, Mazo, Mazuned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: This word is rarely, if ever, seen in its fifth sense. It almost always connotes a sense of antiquity and is often used of gods, prophets, and other historical or mythological figures. 1. To determine the number of. 2. To recite numbers in sequence. 3. To be made up of some numerical value. 4. To regard something as being caused by someone or something. 5. (Archaic; Intransitive) To utter words so as to convey something.

words.loc: Story, Tale, Fable, Discourse, Narrative, Poem, Play
words.def: Mazul, Mazulae, Mazulath (inan.) 1. An account of imaginary or real people and events usually told for entertainment.

words.loc: Word on the Street, Word on the Wire, Grapevine, Rumor
words.def: Idiom 1. (Typically used as the subject of lelōk, lōk "say") The rumor or news going around.

Mazun, Mazunos, Mazun, Mazunoth (an.) Mazun, Mazunae, Mazunath (inan.) 1. Having or showing the ability to speak fluently, coherently, and often persuasively. 2. Able to speak or write a particular foreign language easily and accurately.

words.loc: Reckon, Regard As, Consider, Deem, Judge
words.def: Mēzut, Māzgūrut, Mēzēt, Mazut, Mazōt NOTE: This verb is fully ergative, with ergative alignment being used in all instances. Case frames will still be given along with the definition for the sake of completeness, however. NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: This is a deponent verb. Every tense suffix that ends in a consonant takes an '-ut' suffix, while every tense suffix that ends in a final vowel lengthens that vowel and takes a '-t' suffix. This verb has no participial forms. 1. To think of in a specified way.
Mēzūsh, Māzuzhet, Māzuzhe, Mazūsh, Mazuzho, Mazuzhened 1. To distribute resources or tasks for a specific purpose. 2. To designate something for a specific purpose.
Megnen, Megnenos, Megnen, Megnenoth (an.) Megnen, Megnenas, Megnenae, Megnenath (inan.) 1. Giving or ready to give help. 2. Able to be used for a practical purpose or in several ways.

words.loc: Meanwhile, Simultaneously, Concurrently, At the same time
words.def: 1. At the same time.
Mē, Mehit, Mehikūs, Mek, Meko, Mekined 1. To move or act with great haste.

words.loc: Fast, Quickly, Swiftly, Rapidly, Speedily, Hastily
words.def: 1. At a fast speed.

words.loc: Speed, Quickness, Swiftness, Haste, Rapidity, Celerity, Velocity.
words.def: Mekus, Megrae, Megrath (inan.) 1. The state of moving quickly or the capacity for rapid motion. 2. The rate of motion or action.

words.loc: Crag, Cliff, Rock Face, Bare Rock
words.def: Mekāsh, Mekāshae, Mekāshath (inan.) 1. A bare vertical surface of natural rock, typically steep or rugged. 2. A bare surface of rock.

Mekīr, Mekīris, Mekīri, Mekīrith (an.) Mekīr, Mekīras, Mekīrae, Mekīrath (inan.) NOTE: The stem "mek-" is used for the comparative and superlative grades of pheraen 'fast, quick, rapid'. 1. Moving with a greater or capable of moving at a greater speed. 2. Happening in a shorter time or at a faster pace.

words.loc: Universe, Cosmos, Creation, Totality, Heaven and Earth
words.def: Meldad, Meldar, Melethad NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. All existing matter and space considered as a whole.

words.loc: Void, Unlife, That Which Is Not, Vacuum, Emptiness, Nothingness
words.def: Melduth, Melduthae, Melduthath (inan.) 1. The darkness that dwells behind the sun, moon, and stars. 2. All that lies beyond the boundaries of the universe. 3. (Technical) A space completely devoid of matter.

Meldīn, Meldīs, Meldīn, Meldīneth (an.) Meldīn, Meldīs, Meldīnae, Meldīnath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to the heavens and the divine. 2. Relating to the void or outer space, regarding as the realm of the sun, moon, and stars.

words.loc: Heavens, Firmament, Sky, Afterlife, Outer Space, Region of the Stars, Creation, Universe, That Which Is
words.def: Meleth, Meldis, Melthen (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. The abode of the Gods, celestial bodies, and spirits of the dead. 2. The heavens or the sky, especially when depicted as a tangible thing or as where the sun, moon, and stars appear. 3. The physical universe beyond Nīmlad's atmosphere. 4. (Metonymically) All which exists.
1. Used to express uncertainty or possibility.
1. Used to introduce a new fact or argument.
Mestelbar, Mestelbrae, Mestelbrath (inan.) 1. A group of islands. 2. A sea or stretch of water containing many islands.
Mhalat, Mhalatae, Mhalatath (inan.) 1. A domesticated hoofed mammal with long ears and a braying call, typically used as a beast of burden.
Mhol, Mholae, Mholath (inan.) 1. A mountain covered in trees. 2. A forest that grows on mountains.
Mikīmbesh, Mikīmbeshae, Mikīmbeshath (inan.) 1. The action of helping someone to do something; assistance.

Mihīnet, Mihīhūret, Mihīnēt, Mikīnet, Mikīnōt NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: This verb is fully ergative, with ergative alignment being used in all instances. Case frames will still be given along with the definition for the sake of completeness, however. NOTE: This is a deponent verb. Every tense suffix that ends in a consonant takes an '-et' suffix, while every tense suffix that ends in a final vowel lengthens that vowel and takes a '-t' suffix. This verb has no participial forms. 1. To take, hold, or deploy something as a means of accomplishing a purpose or achieving a result.

words.loc: Mushroom, Root for Mushrooms, Forage for Mushrooms, Gather Mushrooms
words.def: Memingor, Memingorit, Memingorgūs, Mingor, Mingoro, Mingorened 1. To gather or search widely for mushrooms
Mingorim, Mingorimae, Mingorimath (inan.) 1. A member of the race of swams, which are accounted as Elder Children. 2. The race of swams as a whole.

Mingos, Mingorae, Mingorath (inan.) 1. A fungal growth that typically takes the form of a domed cap on a stalk, with gills on the underside of the cap, especially if edible. 2. A living organism that typically grows in a permanent site, feeds on organic matter, and reproduces via spores.

words.loc: Crown, Ordain, Swear In, Enthrone, Inaugurate
words.def: Mīnri, Mīnrit, Mīnrikūs, Minri, Minro, Minrined 1. To install a monarch on the throne, especially during a ceremony. 2. To admit someone formally to public office. 3. (Figurative; Of a system, policy, or period) To begin or introduce.

Mener, Menrikūs, Menre, Mir, Miro, Mirined NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To be engaged in physical or mental activity in order to achieve a result.
Mirdān, Mirdānae, Mirdānath (inan.) 1. A worker in a skilled trade, especially one that involves making things by hand.

words.loc: Job, Task, Duty, Assignment, Chore, Project
words.def: Mirīl, Mirīlae, Mirīlath (inan.) 1. A task or tasks to be undertaken; something a person or thing has to do.

words.loc: Go Across, Go Through, Go Over, Pass Over, Cross, Traverse, Pass, Travel, Ply
words.def: Mindimbar, Mindimbaret, Mindimbargūs, Mishimbar, Mishimbaro, Mishimbarened 1. To travel across, through, or over.

words.loc: Erase, Strike Out, Cross Out, Blot Out, Expunge, Efface, Delete
words.def: Moher, Moherot, Moherokūs, Mogreg, Mogrego, Mogregoned 1. (Of writing or marks) To rub out or remove. 2. To draw a line through or otherwise censor text, with the purpose of removing that text from the rest of the document. 3. (Of debts or obligations) To remove completely.

Mogrekar, Mogrekres, Mogreker, Mogrekreth (an.) 1. A person's hand when the fingers are bent inwards toward the palm and held their tightly, typically in order to strike or grasp something. 2. (Adverbial; in the Instrumental Plural) With the use of the hands rather than with a machine.
Megrekpesh, Megrekpezhae, Megrekpezhath (inan.) 1. The action of interrupting or being interrupted. 2. An act, utterance, or period that interrupts someone or something.
Mogrened, Mogrendis, Mogrendi, Mogrendith (an.) Mogrened, Mogrendas, Mogrendae, Mogrendath (inan.) 1. Marked or decorated with spots or patches of color.

Mogrephek, Mogrephkae, Mogrephkath (inan.) 1. Anything that is used to sting, as a means of attack or defense. 2. A stiff and sharp projection on the stem or other part of a plant. 3. A sharp projection near the end of an arrow or hook, or similar item that is angled away from the main point so as to make removal difficult.

words.loc: Element, Fundamentals, Rudiments, Basics, Essentials, Component
words.def: Mok, Mokħae, Mokħath (inan.) 1. One of the six basic building blocks of matter in the theories of philosophers and alchemists: air, wood, water, stone, clay, and fire. 2. A central or primary rule or principle on which something is based. 3. One of the simplest or most essential parts or principles of which something consists.
Moher, Moheret, Mohergūs, Moker, Mokro, Mogrened 1. To go into or through something by means of a small, sharp object. 2. To wound or stab with a stinger.
Mokerkil, Mokerkilae, Mokerkilath (inan.) 1. The marks used in writing to separate sentences and their elements and to clarify meaning.

words.loc: Point, Dot, Spot, Portion, Moment, Instant
words.def: Mokril, Mokrilae, Mokrilath (inan.) 1. A small, circular mark. 2. (In mathematics, science) A zero-dimensional mathematical object. 4. An individual element in a larger whole. 5. A particular position, or relatively small location on a map, object, or surface. 6. A particular, relatively short point in time. 7. A brief, unspecificed amount of time.
Moherā, Moherket, Moherkūs, Mokrā, Mokerko, Mokerkened 1. (Of writing) To mark or divide with punctuation marks. 2. To stop the continuous progress of something.

Menros, Menroskūs, Memorze, Moros, Morzo, Morzened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Intransitive) To emit or reflect light. 2. (Of a day) To begin.
Morzil, Morzilae, Morzilath (inan.) 1. The time in the morning when the sun appears or daylight arrives.

words.loc: Ghost, Phantom, Wraith, Spirit, Specter, Apparition
words.def: Morzin, Morzinae, Morzinath (inan.) 1. A soul of a dead person which is believed to appear or become manifest to the living, typically as a nebulous image.

Mudmūs, Mudmūsh, Mudmūr, Mudmūseth (an.) Mudmūr, Mudmūras, Mudmūrae, Mudmūrath (inan.) 1. Based on or behaving according to what is morally right and fair. 2. (Of a person or conduct) morally right or justifiable.
Endran, Endranet, Endrangūs, Mudran, Mudrano, Mudraned 1. To exchange or express diverging or opposite views, typically in a heated or angry way.
Mugnūk, Mugnūkis, Mugnūki, Mugnūkith (an.) 1. A person who ranks below another. 2. A person who helps in a particular type of work.
Mumak, Mumakh, Mumāk, Mumāketh (an.) 1. A small insect, often with a sting, that lives eusocially in a complex colony with one or more breeding queens.
Mumākauk, Mumākaukh, Mumākauk, Mumākauketh (an.) Mumākauk, Mumākaukas, Mumākaukae, Mumākaukath (inan.) 1. Tending to work with energy and commitment.
Munkhuzul, Munkhuzulae, Munkhuzulath (inan.) 1. A nest in the form of a mound built by ants or termites.
Productive Affix 1. Prefix that reinforces/augments the idea of the original word.
Mābebar, Mābebret, Mābarkūs, Mābar, Mābro, Mābrened 1. To eat hungrily or quickly.

words.loc: Dry up, Dry out, Wither, Dessicate, Dehydrate
words.def: Mādedbat, Mādedbath, Mādedbatkūs, Mādabat, Mādabdo, Mādabdened 1. (Intransitive) To become completely or excessively dry. 2. (Intransitive; Of plants) To become shriveled and dry. (Especially from a lack of water) 3. (Transitive) To cause something to be completely or excessively dry.

Mādrak, Mādrakūs, Mādrake, Mādak, Mādgo, Mādgened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To kill someone unlawfully and with premeditation. 2. To kill someone in a cruel or violent way.

words.loc: Go Out, Come Out, Get Out, Exit, Leave, Depart, Get Off, Disembark, Go Down, Come Down, Get Down, Climb Down, Step Down, Descend, Retire, Become, Turn Into, Change Into, Transform
words.def: Mamādeg, Mamādgot, Mamādgokūs, Mādeg, Mādgo, Mādgoned 1. To go out of or leave a place. 2. To go out of a vessel or transport. 3. To move from being on top of something to not being on top of it. 4. To move downwards. 5. To leave one's occupation. 6. To change one's nature or character. 7. (Poetic) To change greatly the appearance or form of.
Mādgil, Mādgilae, Mādgilath (inan.) 1. The unlawful killing of one person by another. 2. The killing of something.

words.loc: Go Towards, Approach, Get Closer To, Go Near
words.def: Mamādnūs, Mamādnūret, Mamādnūskūs, Mādnūs, Mādnūro, Mādnūrened 1. To come near or nearer to someone or something in distance or time.

words.loc: Face, Countenance, Visage, Appearance, Front, Fore, Surface, Exterior, Nerve, Confidence, Pluck, Backbone, Spirit, Heart, Mettle
words.def: Māg, Māges, Māge, Māgeth (an.) 1. The front part of a person's head from the forehead to the chin, or the corresponding part in an animal. 2. The side or part of an object that presents itself to view or that is normally seen or used first; the most forward part of something. 3. The outside part or uppermost layer of something, especially with reference to its texture, form, or extent. 4. A person's steadiness, courage, and sense of purpose when facing a demanding situation.
Māgeth, Māgethae, Māgethath (inan.) 1. The part of the face above the eyebrows.

words.loc: King, Queen, Ruler, Monarch, Sovereign, Head of State, Chief, Tyrant, Potentate
words.def: Māgor, Māgres, Māger, Māgoreth (an.) 1. The ruler of an independent state.
Māgos, Māgosh, Māger, Māgreth (inan.) NOTE: This noun takes the allative case. 1. A major Patrin city that with its surrounding territory forms an independent state.
Māgrīn, Māgrīs, Māgrīn, Māgrīneth (an.) Māgrīn, Māgrīnas, Māgrīnae, Māgrīnath (inan.) 1. Of or relating to a ruler and their family. 2. Having the status of a ruler or a member of their family.

Mākh, Mākis, Mākthen (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. A dry, barren area of land, especially one covered with sand, that is characteristically desolate, waterless, and without vegetation.
Māko, Mākōs, Māko, Mākōth (an.) 1. A long-bodied, chiefly marine fish whose front part of the body is encased in broad flat bony plates.
Mākrad, Mākradae, Mākradath (inan.) 1. A heavy fall of snow, especially with a high wind.

words.loc: Evil, Bad, Wicked, Wrong, Malevolent, Malicious, Nefarious, Immoral, Ignoble, Sinful, Villainous
words.def: Māldor, Māldres, Mālder, Māldreth (an.) Mālder, Māldras, Māldrae, Māldrath (inan.) 1. Profoundly immoral and wicked.
Māndauk, Māndaukh, Māndauk, Māndauketh (an.) Māndauk, Māndaukas, Māndaukae, Māndaukath (inan.) 1. (Of a statement) Conforming to the actual state of reality or fact.
Māndo, Māndae, Māndōth (inan.) 1. An especially large rock. (Typically has been worn smooth by erosion)
Māndu, Māndūs, Māndu, Māndūth (an.) 1. A rule defining correct procedure or behavior believed to originate from a deity. 2. The desire or intent of a deity.
Manemeb, Manembut, Manembukūs, Mānep, Mānepo, Mānpuned 1. To destroy something completely by means of burning. 2. To dispose of a corpse by means of burning. (Typically after a funeral ceremony)

Mānrauk, Manraukh, Manrauk, Manrauketh (an.) Manrauk, Manraukas, Manraukae, Manraukath (inan.) 1. Extremely dirty. 2. Highly unpleasant to the senses, especially if through having a revolting smell or taste 3. Arousing revulsion and/or disgust.
Mānē, Mānekh, Mānek, Māneheth (an.) Mānek, Mānekas, Mānekae, Māneheth (inan.) 1. Extremely hot.
Māphkil, Māphkilae, Māphkilath (inan.) 1. A relatively rare or difficult deed.

words.loc: Want, Desire, Yearn, Long For, Covet, Crave, Lust For
words.def: Māphāgo, Māphāgōkūs, Māphāge, Māpāgo, Māpāgo, Māpāgōned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To have an intense wanting for something. 2. To have a strong sexual desire for someone.
Mārem, Mārēs, Mārem, Māremeth (an.) 1. A heavily-built, omnivorous, nocturnal mammal of the weasel family

words.loc: Antiquity, Ancient Times, Distant Past, Classical Times
words.def: Mārgash, Mārgāshae, Mārgāshath (inan.) 1. The ancient past.

words.loc: Ancient, Bygone, Time-Honored, Venerable, Relic, Artifact
words.def: Mārog, Mārges, Mārog, Mārogeth (an.) Mārog, Mārgas, Mārgae, Mārogath (inan.) 1. Belonging to the very distant past. 2.
