(Of a custom or tradition) Respected or valued because it has existed for a long time. 3. Given much respect because of age, wisdom, or experience. 4. (Substantive Inanimate) An object surviving from an earlier time or the distant past.
Mārek, Mārkit, Mārkūs, Mārok, Mārko, Mārkened 1. To remove contaminants from. 2. To make thoroughly clean. 3. (Of metal) To remove impurities or unwanted elements from.
Mārosh, Māroshae, Māroshath (inan.) 1. A series of mountains or hills arranged in a line and connected by high ground.
Mārtum, Mārtumae, Mārtumath (inan.) 1. A ceremonial seat for a sovereign, consul, or similar figure.
Mārēgop, Mārēgbes, Mārēgbe, Mārēgbeth (an.) 1. A person, typically more remote than a grandparent, from whom one is descended. 2. A member of the past generations of one's family or people.

words.loc: Go, Move, Travel, Advance, Proceed, Accede, Go Over, Accompany, Will, Future Tense
words.def: Memāt, Memātkūs, Memāte, Māt, Māto, Mātened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Intransitive) To move from one place to another through space. 2. (Intransitive) To move or travel through time by means of memory or the historical record. 3. (Intransitive) To move onwards. 4. (Of law) To join the opposing side or opinion in voting or debate. 5. To travel alongside 6.. (Auxiliary) Used to mark the future tense.

words.loc: Leave, Go Away From, Depart, Quit, Forsake
words.def: Mendel, Mendelet, Mendelgūs, Mātel, Mādlo, Mādlened 1. To go away from, especially if from a location or object.
Mūtel, Mūtelet, Mūtelgūs, Mātel, Mātelo, Mātelened 1. To refuse to allow.

words.loc: World Ocean, Global Ocean, Seven Seas
words.def: Māzru, Māzrae, Māzrūth (inan.) 1. The global, interconnected body of salt water of Nīmlad.
Māzōr, Māzōres, Māzōr, Māzōreth (an.) 1. A very large or fiercly burning fire. 2. A large fire that is dangerously out of control. (Typically destroying or at risk of destroying land or property)

words.loc: Then, Next, After, Later, Afterward, Subsequently, Thereupon
words.def: 1. At a later or future time.
Mēshīn, Mēshīs, Mēshīn, Mēshīneth (an.) Mēshīn, Mēshīnas, Mēshīnae, Mēshīnath (inan.) 1. Of, based on, or concerned with the law.

words.loc: Legal, Lawful, Legitimate, Licit, Appropriate, Suitable, Proper, Fitting, Apt
words.def: Mētauk, Mētaukh, Mētauk, Mētauketh (an.) Mētauk, Mētaukas, Mētaukae, Mētaukath (inan.) 1. Permitted by law. 2. Suitable or proper in the circumstances.

words.loc: Law, Human Law, Rule, Right, Custom
words.def: Mētu, Mētūs, Mētu, Mētūth (an.) 1. A rule defining correct procedure or behavior established by a community. 2. (In the plural) The body of binding rules established by a community's legislative and judicial authorities. 3. A moral or legal entitlement to have or obtain something or to act in a certain way.

words.loc: Swear, Promise, Take an Oath, Vow
words.def: Endu, Endūkūs, Ende, Mētu, Mēto, Mētūned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. Make a solemn statement or promise undertaking to do something or affirming that something is the case.

words.loc: Fate, Doom, Destiny, Providence, Lot, Speech, Declaration, Oration, Proclamation, Prophecy, Prediction
words.def: Mēzōr, Māzōras, Māzōrae, Māzōrath (inan.) 1. The development of events beyond a person's control, regarded as determined by a supernatural power. 2. (Of a god, prophet, or mythological figure) An address or discourse delivered to an audience.

words.loc: Storyteller, Narrator, Author, Poet, Bard, Fabulist
words.def: Mēzōs, Mēzōsh, Māzōr, Māzōseth (an.) 1. A person who tells stories.
Mīhar, Mīkres, Mīker, Mīkreth (an.) 1. A device, especially one held in the hand, used to carry out a particular function.
Mīk, Mīkh, Mīk, Mīketh (an.) 1. A very young female child, especially one newly or recently born.
Mīkreduk, Mīkredukae, Mīkredukath (inan.) 1. The food, water, and equipment necessary for an army or for people engaged in a particular project or expedition.

words.loc: Circle, Orbit, Band, Crown, Stadium, Racecourse
words.def: Mīs, Mīnrae, Mīnrath (inan.) 1. The geometric figure known as a circle or any shape or arrangement of objects that resembles a circle. 2. A flat, thin strip or loop put around something. 3. A circular ornament, typically made of precious metals and with gemstones, worn on the head by a monarch as a symbol of authority. 4. (Typically of racecourses) A space where games or sports are held.

words.loc: Town, Fortified Town, City, Fortress, Fort, Stronghold, Castle, Citadel
words.def: Mīth, Mīthae, Mīthath (inan.) 1. A settlement larger than a village, especially if it has fortifications. 2. A place that has been fortified so as to protect it against attack.
Memūr, Memūrit, Memrikūs, Mūr, Mūro, Mūrined 1. To gather a crop or crops as a harvest. 2. To cut down grass or crops with a bladed implement.

words.loc: Lightning, Lightning Bolt, Thunderbolt, Hot Lightning
words.def: Mūres, Mūrenrae, Mūrenrath (inan.) NOTE: This word specifically connotes lightning that is nearby and has audible thunder. See doblekar "cold lightning" for a word referring to distant, inaudible lightning. 1. A flash of light produced by a short-duration discharge of electricity within a cloud or between a cloud and the earth, especially when close in proximity and accompanied by thunder.

words.loc: Farmer, Farmhand, Tenant Farmer, Sharecropper, Serf
words.def: Mūrikar, Mūrikres, Mūriker, Mūrikreth (an.) 1. A worker on a farm. 2. A person who farms land rented from a landlord.
Mūrīl, Mūrīlae, Mūrīlath (inan.) 1. The process or period of gathering crops.

Mūs, Mumesh, Mūmer, Mūseth (an.) 1. A unit of time of one twenty-fourth of the day. 2. A less definite but still lengthy period of time. 3. The favorable or appropriate time to do something. 4. (Poetic) The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events. 6. (Adverbial; When modifying a numeral from one to twelve) Indicates the time within a twelve-hour period, either from midnight to noon or noon to midnight.

words.loc: Light, Light Source, Brightness, Splendor, Brilliance, Magnificence, Star
words.def: Mūs, Mūrae, Mūrath (inan.) 1. The natural agent that stimulates sight and makes things visible. 2. A source of illumination. 3. The quality of having a magnificent appearance, display or grandeur. 4. (Poetic) A fixed luminous point in the night sky which is a large, remote incandescent body like the sun.

words.loc: Courier, Carrier, Bringer, Porter, Galley, Merchantman, Merchant Ship
words.def: Mūskar, Mūskres, Mūsker, Mūskreth (an.) 1. One who transports goods or documents. 2. A person or object that carries something or someone. 3. A person or object that brings something or someone somewhere. 4. A ship used for trading or transporting.

Emūstel, Emustelet, Emustelgūs, Mūstel, Mūstelo, Mustelened 1. To take something or someone away from or off of a particular place or location. 2. To get rid of. 3. (Typically poetic) To sweep away by means of wings, waves, or any other quick motion.

words.loc: Except, Excepting, Besides, Save, Excluding, Omitting, Barring, Other Than, Aside From
words.def: Mūstelened, Mūstelendis, Mūstelendi, Mūstelendith (an.) Mūstelened, Mūstelendas, Mūstelendae, Mūstelendath (inan.) 1. Not including; other than.

words.loc: Low Tide, Summer, Harvest Season, Low Water Season, Early Autumn, Early Fall
words.def: Mūstelnār, Mūstelnāres, Mūstelnār, Mūstelnāreth (an.) 1. The state of the tide when it is at its lowest level. 2. The warmest season of the year, defined by the period in which the Lash River is at its lowest; it spans from the middle of May to the middle of September. 3. The period of time in which crops are harvested.
NOTE: In order to gain a sense of "as opposed to some point previous" or if the phrase is negative, tom "now, already" should be used. 1. At the present time or moment.
1. (LOC) At or to the back or far side of.

words.loc: Back, Rear, Hind, Posterior, Dorsal, Aft
words.def: Nadīr, Nadīris, Nadīri, Nadīrith (an.) Nadīr, Nadīras, Nadīrae, Nadīrath (inan.) NOTE: This word is used to refer to the rearmost surface or part of anything that is not an animal or person. See sūlith "back" for a word referring to the backside or dorsal part of an animal or person. 1. Located at or near the rear. 2. (Substantive Inanimate) The side of an object which is farthest away from the front.
Nagmag, Nagmages, Nagmage, Nagmageth (an.) 1. A hard, protective head covering, typically part of armour.

Nemeb, Nembut, Nembukūs, Nap, Napo, Napuned 1. (Of a fire) To produce flames and heat while consuming a material such as wood or coal. 2. To destroy, damage, or injure by means of heat or fire. 3. (Of eyes) To glow.
Pronoun 1. Used by a speaker to refer to themselves.
Naun, Naus, Naun, Nauneth (an.) 1. A small, domesticated, carnivorous mammal with soft fur, a short snout, and retractable claws.
Naunīn, Naunīs, Naunīn, Naunīneth (an.) Naunīn, Naunīnas, Naunīnae, Naunīnath (inan.) 1. 1. Pertaining to or resembling a cat.

words.loc: Lathcat, Werecat, Witch Cat, Race of Lathcats
words.def: Naurim, Naurimae, Naurimath (inan.) 1. A member of the race of lathcats, which are accounted as Younger Children. 2. The race of lathcats as a whole.

Negril, Negrilae, Negrilath (inan.) 1. An especially large or important river (as opposed to khōr, a small river) that itself is not a tributary. 2. An overflowing of any large amount of water beyond its normal confines, especially over dry land.
Negrilnār, Negrilnāres, Negrilnār, Negrilnāreth (an.) 1. The state of the tide when at its highest level.
Nehauk, Nehaukh, Nehauk, Nehauketh (an.) Nehauk, Nehaukas, Nehaukae, Nehaukath (inan.) 1. Full of juice or sap. 2. (Of food) Recently made or obtained.

Nehiphek, Nehiphekae, Nehiphekath (inan.) 1. The upper and lower bony structures in vertebrates forming the framework of the mouth and containing the teeth. 2. The protruding part of the face below the mouth, formed by the apex of the lower jaw. 3. A horizontal architectural element of a building, projecting forward from the main walls and often seen surmounting a frieze. 4. An ornamental molding around the wall of a room just below the ceiling.

words.loc: Flow Strongly, Flood, Submerge, Engulf, Inundate, Swamp, Drown, Overflow, Soak, Drench,
words.def: Nenir, Neniret, Nenirgūs, Nekir, Negro, Negrened 1. (Of a liquid) To move along steadily, continously, and with great force and speed in a current or stream. 2. To cover or submerge a place or area with water. 3. (Of a river or sea) To become swollen and overflow. 4. To make something thoroughly wet by immersing it in liquid.

words.loc: Violent, Brutal, Vicious, Aggressive, Warlike, Martial
words.def: Nekmūs, Nekmūsh, Nekmūr, Nekūrseth (an.) Nekmūr, Nekmūras, Nekmūrae, Nekmūrath (inan.) 1. Using or involving physical force intended to hurt, damage, or kill someone or something.
Nekāsh, Nekāshae, Nekāshath (inan.) 1. The quality of being hot or warm. 2. A source or level of high temperature.
Nembos, Nembres, Nembor, Nemboseth (an.) Nembor, Nembras, Nembrae, Nembrath (inan.) 1. On fire. 2. Very hot. 3. (Figurative) Full of passion, eagerness, or enthusiasm.

Nemek, Nemekh, Nē, Neheth (an.) NOTE: The stem for this word is "neh-" 1. The soft substance consisting of muscle and fat that is found between the skin and bones of an animal or a human, especially as food. 2. The soft fleshy part of a fruit.

words.loc: Field, Meadow, Grassland, Plain, Pasture, Valley, Vale, Dale
words.def: Nen, Nenae, Nenath (inan.) 1. A large open area of country that is typically flat and covered with grass. 2. Land covered with grass and other low plants suitable for grazing animals. 3. (Poetic) A low area of land between hills or mountains, especially when broad or wide.
1. (Indeclinable) No longer alive.

words.loc: Pastoral, Nomadic, Nomad, Migrant, Traveler, Wanderer, Planet
words.def: Nenīn, Nenīs, Nenīn, Nenīneth (an.) Nenīn, Nenīs, Nenīnae, Nenīnath (inan.) 1. (Especially of land or of a farm) Used for or related to the keeping or grazing of domestic animals, especially sheep or cattle. 2. Living the life of or pertaining to nomads. 3. (Substantive Animate) A member of a people having no permanent home, especially if that people travels from place to place to find fresh pasture for their livestock. 4. (Substantive Animate; In astronomy) A celestial body that has apparent motion (as opposed to "fixed" stars) in the sky, including the sun, the bright star, and the moon.

words.loc: Oyster, Mussel, Clam, Scallop, Cockle, Bivalve, Shellfish
words.def: Nhandon, Nhandonae, Nhandonath (inan.) 1. An aquatic mollusk that has a compressed body enclosed within a hinged shell.

Nikh, Nikish, Niker, Nikheth (an.) 1. Military operations of some particular kind. 2. The science and art of military command as applied to the overall planning and conduct of warfare. 3. A broad, overarching plan of action intended to accomplish a specific military goal.
Niskīphek, Niskīphkae, Niskīphkath (inan.) 1. Items which are or were offered or inteded for sale.

words.loc: Banner, Standard, Flag, Legion, Battalion, Company, Regiment, Division, Unit
words.def: Nogi, Nogae, Nogath (inan.) 1. A piece of cloth, often decorated with an emblem, used as a visual signal, a symbol, or to signify a military commander, legion, monarch, nation or people. 2. (By extension) The military unit under such a flag or standard.
Nor, Norae, Norath (inan.) 1. The solid surface of the world.
Nener, Nenrit, Nenrikūs, Nor, Noro, Norined 1. To make dirty or filthy.
1. (Adverbial Allative) Toward a lower level, whether in physical space, in a hierarchy, or in amount or value; away from the source or origin.
Norauk, Noraukh, Norauk, Norauketh (an.) Norauk, Noraukas, Noraukae, Noraukath (inan.) 1. Dirty; Covered in or marked with an unclean substance.

words.loc: Rot, Decay, Stench, Stink, Reek, Miasma
words.def: Nozdihar, Nozdihares, Nozdihar, Nozdihareth (an.) 1. Rotten or decayed matter. 2. A strong and very unpleasant smell.
Nukhez, Nukhzae, Nukhzath (an.) 1. A person who manages or executes the plans and directives of another, more senior military leader.

words.loc: General, Admiral, Legate, Commander, Captain, Military Leader, War Chief, Strategist, Tactician
words.def: Nukhūs, Nukhumesh, Nukhumer, Nūkhuseth (an.) 1. The commander of an army. 2. A person who devises military strategies and tactics.

words.loc: Small, Little, Less, Insignificant, Unimportant, Trivial
words.def: Nuth, Nuthos, Nuth, Nuthoth (an.) Nuth, Nuthas, Nuthae, Nuthath (inan.) NOTE: See 'iepīr' for the comparative and superlative grades. 1. Not large or big in size. 2. Of little value or importance.

words.loc: Please, Prithee, You're Welcome, No Problem, Don't Mention It
words.def: Phatic Expression 1. Used when making a request or asking for a favor. 2. Used as a response to being thanked.
NOTE: See nūskīs gindas "merchant numbers" for the set of numerals used in commerce. 1. The simple set of numerals used for everyday writing.
Nāk, Nākae, Nākath (inan.) 1. Organized, large-scale, armed conflict between national, ethnic, or other sizeable groups, usually but not always involving active engagement of military forces.
Nanāk, Nanākit, Nanākikūs, Nāk, Nāko, Nākined 1. To actively engage in, carry on, or participate in a prolonged conflict or military campaign.

words.loc: Hero, Champion, Savior, Warrior, Epitome, Embodiment, Personification, Paragon, Archetype
words.def: Nākar, Nākres, Nāker, Nākreth (an.) 1. Somebody who possesses great bravery and carries out extraordinary or noble deeds. 2. The perfect or ultimate example of a specific quality, type, or concept

words.loc: Lake, Pool, Mere, Tarn, Loch, Waterhole, Lagoon, -Water
words.def: Nār, Nārus, Nār, Nāruth (an.) 1. A large, landlocked body of water surrounded by land. 2. A toponymic suffix meaning '-water' or '-waters'.

words.loc: Warm, Hot, Impassioned, Vehement, Rash, Reckless
words.def: Nē, Nekh, Nek, Neheth (an.) Nek, Nekas, Nekae, Neheth (inan.) 1. Having a high degree of heat or a high temperature. 2. Showing strong emotion or feeling, especially in a forceful, passionate, or intense manner. 3. (Of a person or their actions) Without thinking or caring about the consequences of an action.
Nenē, Nenehit, Nenehikūs, Nē, Neho, Nehined 1. Bite and work in the mouth with the teeth, especially to make it easier to swallow.

Nēban, Nēbnae, Nēbnath (inan.) 1. The act or process of immersing and washing one's body in a large container of water. 2. A container wherein bathing occurs. 3. Any vessel used for cooking over an open fire.

words.loc: Shoulder, Axle, Axletree, Keel, Ridge, Reef, Shoal, Sandbar
words.def: Nēgel, Nēglād, Nēglae, Nēglath (an.) 1. The part of an animal's body between the base of the neck and arm socket. 2. The rod or spindle on which a wheel revolves, or which revolves with a wheel. 3. A large beam along the underside of a ship’s hull from bow to stern. 4. The line along which two sloping surfaces meet which diverge towards the ground. 5. A chain or range of rocks, sand, or coral lying at or near the surface of the water.

Nēgo, Nēgōd, Nēgo, Nēgōth (an.) 1. (Of birds) A modified forelimb that bears large feathers and is used for flying. 2. (Of aquatic animals) A flattened appendage used for propelling, steering, and balancing in the water. 3. The broad, flat edge of a sword, axe, knife, oar, or other tool or weapon.
Nēkhon, Nēkhonae, Nēkhonath (inan.) 1. The liquid obtained from or present in plants, especially when from fruits and vegetables.

Nīmil, Nīmlae, Nīmlath (inan.) 1. The region of the atmosphere and outer space seen from Nīmlad, especially when referring to the near sky in which weather and flying animals appear. 2. The invisible gaseous substance constituting Nīmlad's atmosphere. 3. (Philosophy, Alchemy) One of the six elements by which the Patrin categorize the world's substances. 4. (Poetic) The sea.

words.loc: Earth, World, Globe, Nīmlad, Sky and Sea
words.def: Nīmlad, Nīmlar, Nīmilthad (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. The moon on which the Patrin live as a physical whole.

words.loc: Blue, Blue-Green, Gray, Light Blue, Sky Blue, Turquoise, Cyan, Teal, Blue-Gray, Ashen, Hoar, Hoary, Blue-Eyed, Gray-Eyed
words.def: Nīmlauk, Nīmlaukh, Nīmlauk, Nīmlauketh (an.) Nīmlauk, Nīmlaukas, Nīmlaukae, Nīmlaukath (inan.) 1. Of a bright or pale blue color. 2. Of a greenish-blue color. 3. Of a bluish-gray color. 4. Having a color between white and black, as of ashes or lead. 5. (Of a person) Having eyes blue or gray in color. 6. Used as an epithet of spirits and entities related to the sky, clouds, and wind. 7. Used as an epithet for Kurūgan, referring to her gray eyes.

Nīmliger, Nīmligros, Nīmliger, Nīmligeroth (an.) Nīmliger, Nīmligras, Nīmligrae, Nīmligrath (inan.) 1. (Poetic) Possessing great and impressive power or strength. 2. Used as an epithet for both Gimīlhizar and Thobrauk.
Nunūr, Nunūrit, Nūskūs, Nūr, Nūro, Nūrīned 1. To catch or try to catch fish, typically by using a net or hook and line.
Nūrauk, Nūraukh, Nūrauk, Nūrauketh (an.) Nūrauk, Nūraukas, Nūraukae, Nūraukath (inan.) 1. Relating to or resembling a fish.
Nūrūk, Nūrūkis, Nūrūki, Nūrūkith (an.) 1. A person who catches fish for a living or for sport.
1. (GEN) Move in the direction of 2. (GEN) Move into physical contact with something, so as to be supported by or colliding with it.
Nūs, Numesh, Numer, Nūseth (an.) 1. A limbless, cold-blooded, vertebrate animal with gills and fins and living wholly in water.

words.loc: Merchant, Trader, Seller, Dealer, Vendor, Purveyor
words.def: Nūsek, Nūskis, Nūski, Nūskith (an.) 1. A person who buys and sells goods and is involved in trade.
Nūskhon, Nūskhonae, Nūskhonath (inan.) 1. A fermented fish sauce made of small fish and brine used as a condiment.
Nenūski, Nenūskīt, Nenūskīkūs, Nūski, Nūsko, Nūskīned 1. To buy and sell goods and services. 2. To transfer goods or provide services in exchange for money or goods.
Nūskīl, Nūskīlae, Nūskīlath (inan.) 1. The action of buying and selling goods and services.
Nūskīn, Nūskīs, Nūskīn, Nūskīneth (an.) Nūskīn, Nūskīnas, Nūskīnae, Nūskīnath (inan.) 1. Relating to trade or commerce.

words.loc: Merchant Numbers, Capital Numbers, Uppercase Numbers
words.def: NOTE: See nuthos gindas "small numbers" for the set of numerals used in everyday writing. 1. The complex set of numerals used in commercial, accounting, or financial contexts in order to discourage forgery and fraud.
Nūzban, Nūzbanae, Nūzbanath (inan.) 1. A small body of still water. 2. A freshwater pond stocked with fish.
Nūzrim, Nūzrimae, Nūzrimath (inan.) 1. A large group of fish or other sea life.
1. (LOC) In a position further forward of or at the front part of someone or something else.

Ōbos, Ōboskūs, Ōbze, Obos, Obzo, Obzened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To have on one's body or a part of one's body as clothing, decoration, protection, or for some other purpose.

words.loc: Flat, Level, Even, Calm, Still, Tranquil, Motionless
words.def: Og, Ogis, Ogi, Ogith (an.) Og, Ogas, Ogae, Ogath (inan.) 1. Having few or no variations in height. 2. (Of the sea) Not disturbed by large waves.
Ogael, Ogaeles, Ogael, Ogaeleth (an.) Ogael, Ogaelas, Ogaelae, Ogaelath (inan.) 1. Having much or varied color. 2. Having a color or colors, especially as opposed to being black, white, or neutral.

words.loc: Color, Hue, Shade, Tint, Pigment, Dye, Colorant, Personality, Character, Nature, Disposition, Temper
words.def: Ogi, Ogae, Ogīth (inan.) 1. A particular set of visible spectral compositions, perceived or named as a class. 2. A natural or synthetic substance used to add a color to or change the color of something. 3. (Figurative; Of people) The combination of characteristics or qualities that form an individual's distinctive character.

words.loc: Color, Tint, Tinge, Dye, Stain, Paint
words.def: Ōgi, Ōgīt, Ōgīkūs, Ogi, Ogo, Ogīned 1. To change or add color to something by painting, dyeing, or shading it.

Ōgon, Ōgohūs, Ōgne, Ogon, Ogno, Ogoned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To choose one or more elements of a set.
Ogīkar, Ogīkres, Ogīker, Ogīkreth (an.) 1. An artist who paints pictures. 2. A laborer or workman who paints surfaces.
Ogīl, Ogīlae, Ogīlath (inan.) 1. An artwork done with the use of paint.
Ogīphek, Ogīphkae, Ogīphkath (inan.) 1. A brush for applying paint.

words.loc: Worry, Anxiety, Dread, Disquiet, Unease, Unrest, Foreboding, Trepidation, Worried, Anxious
words.def: Okhuzor, Okhuzoras, Okhuzorae, Okhuzorath (inan.) 1. (Substantive Inanimate) A strong feeling of worry or apprehension, especially over an imminent event or something with an uncertain outcome. 2. (Substantive Inanimate; Of the public) A state of great uncertainty or fear.
Ornauk, Ornaukh, Ornauk, Ornauketh (an.) Ornauk, Ornaukas, Ornaukae, Ornaukath (inan.) 1. Easily frightened.
Orno, Ornōs, Orno, Ornōth (an.) 1. A fast-running, long-eared mammal that resembles a large rabbit, having long hind legs and occurring typically in grassland or open woodland.
Ornōl, Ornōlas, Ornōlae, Ornōlath (an.) 1. A burrowing, gregarious, plant-eating mammal with long ears, long hind legs, and a short tail.

Os, Osh, Or, Oseth (an.) Or, Oras, Orae, Orath (inan.) NOTE: The stem "or-" is used for the comparative and superlative grades of ēgor 'good'. 1. Of a more excellent or effective type or quality. 2. Of the most excellent, effective, or desirable type or quality.

words.loc: Leave, Go Away From, Separate From, Part Ways With, Come Apart, Break Up, Abandon, Leave Behind, Desert, Forsake, Give Up On
words.def: Ōset, Ostakūs, Oste, Oset, Osto, Ostaned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Intransitive) To depart from, especially if from a person. 2. (Intransitive) To end one's connection or affiliation with. 3. To leave permanently, especially when in spite of a duty or responsibility.
Ospa, Ospae, Ospāth (inan.) 1. Food made of flour, water, and yeast or another leavening agent, mixed together and baked.
Opsabu,

Ospād, Ospār, Ospāthad (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. Any solid substance that can be consumed by living organisms, especially by eating, in order to sustain life.
Ospātān, Ospātānae, Ospātānath (inan.) 1. A person who prepares and cooks food, especially as a job or in a specified way.
Ot, Otas, Otae, Otath (an.) Ot, Otās, Ota, Otāth (inan.) 1. (Of hair) Pale yellow of color.
Othān, Othānae, Othānath (inan.) 1. A person who makes and sells arrows.
Pab, Pabes, Pab, Pabeth (an.) 1. A man who fathers a child. 2. A male parent.
Pabad, Pabar, Pabthad (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. The mother and father as a unit.
Pabel, Pablas, Pablae, Pablath (an.) 1. Little Father; An affectionate term for one's father. 2. Thumb
Pabmor, Pabamres, Pabamer, Pabmoreth (an.) 1. One whose parents are dead or absent for a long time. 2. One whose father is either dead or absent.
Pabīn, Pabīs, Pabīn, Pabīneth (an.) Pabīn, Pabīs, Pabīnae, Pabīnath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to a father. 2. Behaving as or characteristic of a father.

words.loc: Break, Shatter, Smash, Fracture, Crack, Pop, Burst, Rupture, Unfurl, Set Sail, Lower, Violate, Breach, Infringe
words.def: Phaera, Phaerākūs, Phaere, Paera, Paero, Paerāned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To separate or cause to separate into pieces as a result of a blow, shock, or strain. 2. To come suddenly and violently apart, spilling the contents, typically as a result of an impact or internal pressure. 3. (Of sails) To make or become spread out from a rolled or folded state, especially in order to be open to the wind. 4. (Figurative) To crush the emotional strength, spirit, or resistance of. 5. (Figurative) To fail to adhere to or obey (a law, treaty, agreement, or promise).

words.loc: Fragment, Piece, Bit, Chip, Shard, Ruins, Remnants, Remains, Wreck
words.def: Paerāned, Paerāndaes, Paerāndae, Paerāndath (inan.) 1. (Substantive Inanimate) A small part broken or separated off something. 2. (Substantive Inanimate; In the Plural) The remains of a building, typically an old one, that has suffered much damage or disintegration.
Paerīn, Paerīs, Paerīn, Paerīneth (an.) Paerīn, Paerīnas, Paerīnae, Paerīnath (inan.) 1. Easily broken or damaged.
Paharel, Paharelas, Paharelae, Paharelath (an.) 1. A hole in a lock into which the key is inserted.
Pahīn, Pahīna, Pahīnāth (inan.) 1. A crustacean with a soft asymmetrical abdomen, which lives in a castoff mollusk shell for protection.
Pamos, Pamosh, Pamer, Pamoseth (an.) 1. A person who is held as the property of another, and whose labor and body (and often life) is subject to the owner's will.

words.loc: Bull, Ox, Steer, Cattle, Aurochs, Wild Cattle
words.def: Par, Parus, Par, Paruth (an.) 1. An adult male of a domesticated breed of ox, kept to breed cows and produce beef. 2. A wild ox, regardless of age or gender.

words.loc: Charge, Rush, Attack, Storm, Run At, Stampede
words.def: Peber, Pebrit, Pebrikūs, Par, Paro, Parined 1. To move forward quickly and forcefully 2. (Of militaries) To attack by moving forward quickly. 3. (Of herds of animals) To run away or rush off in a wild, uncontrollable manner, especially due to fright.
Parel, Parlas, Parlae, Parlath (inan.) 1. A young bovine animal.
Path, Pathae, Pathath (inan.) 1. A viscous yellow-brown fluid made by insects from nectar that is eaten as a sweet food.
Pepath, Pepthit, Pepathkūs, Path, Patho, Pathined 1. To gather or produce honey.

words.loc: Honeyed, Sweetened, Honey-Sweet, Honey, Sweetie, Dear, Babe, Darling, Sweetheart
words.def: Pathauk, Pathaukh, Pathauk, Pathauketh (an.) Pathauk, Noraukas, Pathaukae, Pathaukath (inan.) 1. (Of food) Containing or coated with honey. 2. Having a rich sweetness of taste or smell. 3. (Figurative; Substantive Animate) Used as a term of endearment or as an affectionate form of addresss.
Pathban, Pathibnae, Pathibnath (inan.) 1. An artifical structure wherein bees are kept for the purpose of collecting honey.
Pathphek, Pathphekae, Pathphekath (inan.) 1. A person who works with and breeds bees, especially for their honey.

Patrin, Patrīs, Petren, Peterneth (an.) Petren, Peternas, Peternae, Peternath (inan.) NOTE: The stem of this word is "petern-" 1. Relating to Patrinor or its city-states, history, or people. 2. (Substantive Animate) A native or member of the Patrin cities or a person of Patrin descent. 3. (Substantitve Inanimate) The Paternic language.
Patrinor, Patrinorae, Patrinorath (inan.) 1. The coastal region where the Patrin peoples reside, lying to the west of Gemenor, north of the Māgahur Desert, and south of the Great Chasmous Sea.

words.loc: However, Nevertheless, Nonetheless, Even so, In Spite of This, Regardless
words.def: 1. Used to introduce a statement that contrasts with or seems to contradict something that has been said previously.
Pen, Pen, Peneth 1. (Numeral) Five.

words.loc: Afar, Aloof, At Long Range, Beyond Reach, Out of Reach
words.def: 1. At or from a great distance. 2. Beyond the capacity of someone to attain or achieve something.
Penel, Penlād, Penlae, Penilath (an.) 1. The end part of a person's arm beyond the wrist, including the palm, fingers, and thumb. 2. The joint connecting the hand to the forearm.

words.loc: Hand-to-Hand, Close Combat, Close Quarters, Close Up, Very Near
words.def: 1. (Of fighting) Involving people who are close enough to touch. 2. (Of people) Very near to one another.

Pher, Phergūs, Phere, Per, Pero, Perned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To perceive with the eyes. 2. To become aware of by means of one of the senses, especially that of sight.
Perbesh, Perbezhae, Perbezhath (inan.) 1. Care taken to avoid danger or mistakes.
Perbor, Perbres, Perber, Perboreth (an.) Perber, Perbras, Perbrae, Perborath (inan.) 1. Able to be seen. 3. Easily understood; clear, self-evident, or apparent.
Perbor, Perobres, Perober, Perobreth (an.) Perober, Perobras, Perobrae, Perobrath (inan.) 1. Making sure to avoid potential danger, mishap, or harm.
1. In a visible or open manner. 2. In a way that is easily perceived or understood.
1. So as to give the impression of a certain quality.
Phereph, Pherphet, Pherphekūs, Pereph, Perpho, Perphened 1. To observe and direct someone or something, especially in an official capacity. 2. To have a view of from above.
Pheresh, Pherezhet, Phereshkūs, Peresh, Perezho, Pershened 1. To inquire about, study, or scrutinize in order to ascertain information or discover something hidden.

Pheret, Phergūret, Pherēt, Peret, Perōt NOTE: Unlike English, this verb can take clauses as subjects i.e. "that John is an elephant seems", meaning "it seems that John is an elephant" NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: This is a deponent verb. Every tense suffix that ends in a consonant takes an '-et' suffix, while every tense suffix that ends in a final vowel lengthens that vowel and takes a '-t' suffix. This verb has no participial forms. 1. (Copulative) To be perceived as or appear to be.
Phergrum, Phergormet, Phergoruhūs, Pergrum, Pergormo, Pergormened 1. To perceive or recognize something, especially something intangible or that has been obscured or hidden.
Peril, Perilae, Perilath (inan.) 1. A prediction, especially one made by a prophet or under divine or magical inspiration. 2. The interpetation of the will of the gods.

words.loc: Stormy, Choppy, Rough, Turbulent, Tempestuous, Violent
words.def: Permūs, Permūsh, Permūr, Permūseth (an.) Permūr, Permūras, Permūrae, Permūrath (inan.) 1. (Of the sea) Having large waves and fierce winds because of stormy conditions.

Phernad, Phernaret, Phernadgūs, Pernad, Pernaro, Pernarned 1. (Typically of a person) To feel intense dislike or digust for, especially in secret. 2. To feel bitterness or indignation at. 3. To desire to have a quality, possession, or other desirable attribute belonging to another.
Pherob, Pherobet, Pherobgūs, Perob, Perbo 1. To say or estimate that something will happen in the future or be a consequence of something.
1. In a way that deliberately avoids harm or errors.
Pertor, Pertores, Pertor, Pertoreth (an.) 1. A facial appearance usually associated with an emotion.

words.loc: Rain Cloud, Stormcloud, Bad Weather, Inclement Weather
words.def: Perun, Perūs, Peren, Peruneth (an.) 1. A cloud or a mass of clouds that yields rain. 2. Weather that is unpleasant or unsuitable for outdoor activities.

words.loc: Pull, Draw, Drag, Trail, Tug, Tow, Pluck, Pick, Gather, Derive, Deduce, Extract, Take Away, Prolong, Extend, Lengthen, Protract
words.def: Phester, Phestergūs, Phestre, Pester, Pestro, Pestrened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To apply a force to an object so that it comes toward the person or thing applying the force. 2. To collect with the hand by grasping and pulling, or by drawing toward oneself. 3. To obtain from a body of information. 4. To extend the duration of.
Pestren, Pestrenos, Pestren, Pestrenoth (an.) Pestren, Pestrenas, Pestrenae, Pestrenath (inan.) 1. Tending to stick.
Phestresh, Phestreshet, Phestreshkūs, Pestresh, Pestreshet, Pestreshened 1. To cause someone to do or believe something through reasoning or argument.

words.loc: Understand, Comprehend, Grasp, Take In, Perceive, Know
words.def: Phestrur, Phestruret, Phestrurgūs, Pestrur, Pestruro, Pestrurened 1. To perceive the intended meaning of. 2. To be thoroughly familiar with. 3. To know the intent, motives or character of.

words.loc: Forest, Wood, Woodland, Greenwood, Holt, Jungle, Grove, Copse
words.def: Phaendar, Phaendarae, Phaendarath (inan.) 1. A large area covered chiefly with trees and undergrowth.

words.loc: Wood, Lumber, Timber, Planks, Logs, Firewood, Kindling, Fuel
words.def: Phaes, Phaenis, Phaenthen (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. The hard fibrous material that forms the main substance of the trunk or branches of a tree or shrub. 2. (Philosophy, Alchemy) One of the six elements by which the Patrin categorize the world's substances.

words.loc: Find, Locate, Discover, Spot, Come Upon, Come Across
words.def: Pepheg, Pephgūs, Pephge, Phag, Phago, Phaganed NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To discover or perceive something by chance, unexpectedly, or as the result of a search.

Pharaz, Pharzae, Pharzath (inan.) NOTE: The Paternic culture distinguishes between high-quality gold and low-quality gold. The former is described as being 'red gold' while the latter is described as 'pale gold'. 1. A yellow, precious metal, used especially in jewelry and decoration.

words.loc: Goodbye, Farewell, Bye, See You Later, You Too
words.def: Phatic Expression 1. Used in response to a valediction such as 'gurneth' (goodbye).
Pharzael, Pharzaaeles, Pharzael, Pharzaeleth (an.) Pharzael, Pharzaelas, Pharzaelae, Pharzaelath (inan.) 1. Made of gold. 2. Adorned with, covered by, or decorated with gold.
Pharzauk, Pharzaukh, Pharzauk, Pharzauketh (an.) Pharzauk, Pharzaukas, Pharzaukae, Pharzaukath (inan.) 1. Having a yellow-brown color and luster similar to that of gold.
Pephraze, Pephrazēt, Pephrazekūs, Pharze, Pharzo, Pharzēned 1. To cover thinly with gold.
Pharzeg, Pharzegae, Pharzegath (inan.) 1. A yellow alloy of copper and zinc.
Pharzetān, Pharzetānae, Pharzetānath (inan.) 1. A person who makes and repairs things made out of gold, especially jewelry.

words.loc: Therefore, Thus, Thusly, Hence, Consequently, Ergo, Wherefore
words.def: 1. As a result or consequence of this.
1. In addition.

words.loc: Bad, Poor, Unpleasant, Unkind, Hostile, Evil, Wicked, Destructive, Harmful, Unhealthy, Spoiled, Rotten
words.def: Phaug, Phauges, Phauge, Phaugeth (an.) Phaug, Phaugas, Phaugae, Phaugath (inan.) 1. Of poor quality or of a low standard. 2. Not pleasant to the senses. 3. Lacking kindness or sympathy. 4. Intending to harm or causing harm. 5. Conducive to poor health. 6. (Of food) In a state of decay.
Phaugbīl, Phaugbīlae, Phaugbīlath (inan.) 1. A disrespectul or scornful remark or action. 2. The action of making a false spoken statement damaging to a person's reputation.

words.loc: Insult, Offend, Affront, Abuse, Taunt, Jeer, Slander, Defame
words.def: Phauglelōk, Phauglelōkūs, Phauglelōke, Phauglōk, Phauglōko, Phauglōkened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To speak to or treat with disrespect or scorn. 2. To provoke or challenge with insulting remarks. 3. To make false and damaging statements about someone.

words.loc: Evil Eye, Cast an Evil Eye
words.def: Pephaugzīm, Pephaugzīmit, Pephaugzīme, Phaugzīm, Phaugzīmo, Phaugzīmined 1. To cast an evil eye upon someone or something.

Phaugzīmad, Phaugzīmed, Phaugzīnthad (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. A wicked look conveying dislike or envy that is believed to be able to cause bad luck or injury.

words.loc: Badly, Poorly, Wrongly, Improperly, Evilly, Wickedly
words.def: 1. In an unsatisfactory, inadequate, or unsuccessful manner. 2. In an evil manner.

words.loc: Hold, Have, Understand, Comprehend, Keep, Retain, Protect, Watch, Guard, Defend, Hold Back, Restrain, Restrict, Detain, Bind, Fetter, Hug, Embrace
words.def: Pephzāg, Pephzāgūs, Pephzāge, Phazāg, Phazāgo, Phazāgened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To grasp, carry, or support with one's hands. 2. To perceive the intended meaning of. 3. To continue to have possession of something. 4. To protect from harm or danger. 5. To control or keep in check. 6. To deprive of liberty. 7. To keep from proceeding. 8. To squeeze someone tightly in one's arms, typically to express affection.

words.loc: Refrain, Abstain, Forgo, Avoid, Eschew, Go Without
words.def: Pephzāgdel, Pephzāgdelet, Pephzāgdelgūs, Phazāgdel, Phazāgdelo, Phazāgdelened 1. To keep away from or stop oneself from doing something.

Phazāgen, Phazāgenos, Phazāgen, Phazāgeneth (an.) Phazāgen, Phazāgenas, Phazāgenae, Phazāgenath (inan.) 1. Having or showing dogged determination not to change one's attitude or position on something, especially in spite of good arguments or reasons to do so.

Phazāgus, Phazāgurae, Phazāgurath (inan.) 1. A theoretical line (physically or mentally) followed from a point of origin or towards a destination. 2. The direction or position of something, or the direction of movement, relative to a fixed point.
1. (LOC) At a higher level or layer than. 2. (GEN) Expresses movement such that someone or something goes above or over something.

words.loc: Open, Sunny, Clear, Transparent, Limpid, Unclouded
words.def: Phe, Phēs, Phe, Phēth (an.) Phe, Phēs, Phae, Phēth (inan.) 1. Not closed or shut. 2. Exposed to the air; Not covered. 3. (Of weather or a day) Having much sunshine. 4. (Of weather or a day) Being without clouds. 5. (Of liquids) Allowing light to pass through so that objects behind can be distinctly seen.

Pephek, Pephkūs, Pephke, Phek, Pheko, Phekened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: Pephek, phek is telic as opposed to sazhā, shā "do, act", which is atelic. 1. To perform; To execute; To carry out. 2. To form something by combining or manipulating materials or parts.

words.loc: Action, Act, Deed, Doing, Accomplishment, Achievement
words.def: Phekil, Phekilae, Phekilath (inan.) 1. An action that is performed intentionally or consciously. 2. The successful completion of a task.

words.loc: Go Around, Skirt, Circle, Circulate, Circumnavigate
words.def: Pephkrum, Pephkrumet, Pephkrume, Phekrum, Phekrumo, Phekrumened 1. To move around the border or edge of. 2. To move in circles.

Phel, Phelae, Phelath (inan.) 1. A liquid that forms the seas, lakes, rivers, and rain and is the basis of the fluids of living organisms. 2. A general term for any stretch or area of water, such as a sea, lake, or river. 3. (Philosophy, Alchemy) One of the six elements by which the Patrin categorize the world's substances.

Phelban, Phelbanae, Phelbanath (inan.) 1. A basin used for washing. (Typically used for the hands and face) 2. A vessel used for holding and pouring liquids. 3. A bucket used specifically for carrying liquids.
Phelbu, Phelbūs, Phelbu, Phelbūth (an.) 1. A droplet of water.
Phelger, Phelgros, Phelger, Phelgeroth (an.) 1. (Substantive Animate) One whose job is to carry and provide water, especially if a slave.
Phelgis, Phelgish, Phelgir, Phelgiseth (an.) 1. A small free-swimming crustacean with an elongated body.

Phelmūr, Phelmūrae, Phelmūrath (inan.) 1. An artificially dug channel that is constructed to carry water from one place to another, typcially for agricultural purposes. 2. A structure for carrying water from a source to a distant distribution point, typically in the form of a bridge or similar design.

Phelnad, Phelnadae, Phelnadath (inan.) 1. A trail of disturbed water left behind by the passage of a ship. 2. A long narrow trench made in the ground by a plow, especially for planting seeds or for irrigation. 3. A line of poetry.
Phelīn, Phelīs, Phelīn, Phelīneth (an.) Phelīn, Phelīs, Phelīnae, Phelīnath (inan.) 1. Filled with water. 3. (Of eyes) Filled with tears or about to cry.
Pronoun 1. Used by speakers to refer to themselves and one or more others, excluding the addressee.

words.loc: Fast, Quick, Swift, Rapid, Fleet, Nimble
words.def: Pheraen, Pheraes, Pheraen, Pheraeneth (an.) Pheraen, Pheraenas, Pheraenae, Pheraenath (inan.) NOTE: See 'mekīr' for the comparative and superlative grades. 1. Moving with or capable of moving great speed. 2. Happening in a short time or at a fast pace.

words.loc: Soon, Shortly, Before Long, In a Moment
words.def: 1. Short in length of time from the present.
Pheru, Pherae, Pherūth (inan.) 1. An alcoholic drink made from fermented grape juice.
Pherūban, Pherūbanae, Pherūbanath (inan.) 1. A plantation of grapevines, typically producing grapes made in winemaking.
Pherūphek, Pherūphekae, Pherūphekath (inan.) 1. A person engaged in the making of wine.

words.loc: Shrink, Diminish, Contract, Dwindle, Lessen, Shorten
words.def: Pophae, Pophaet, Pophaekūs, Phohae, Phoho, Phohaened 1. (Intransitive) To become smaller in size or amount.
Phum, Phumae, Phumath (inan.) 1. The part projecting above the mouth on the face of a person or animal, containing the nostrils and used for breathing and smelling.

Phumauk, Phumaukh, Phumauk, Phumauketh (an.) Phumak, Phumaukas, Phumaukae, Phumaukath (inan.) 1. Covered in or having the feel or consistency of slime. 2. (Substantive Animate) A mollusk with a single spiral shell into which the whole body can be withdrawn. 3. (Substantive Animate) A mollusk with a long body that typically lacks a shell and secretes a film of mucus.

words.loc: Mucus, Snot, Phlegm, Slime, Ooze, Sludge, Goo
words.def: Phumkhon, Phumkhonae, Phumkhonath (inan.) 1. A slimy substance secreted by mucous membranes and glands. 2. A moist, soft, and slippery substance, typically regarded as repulsive.

words.loc: Scornful, Contemptuous, Derisive, Mocking, Scoffing, Sneering, Jeering, Snide
words.def: Phunger, Phungros, Phunger, Phungroth (an.) Phunger, Phungras, Phungrae, Phungrath (inan.) 1. Feeling or expressing contempt, scorn, or ridicule. 2. Making fun of someone in a cruel way.
Pronoun 1. Used by a speaker to refer to refer to themselves and one or more others, including the addressee.

words.loc: Hiding Place, Refuge, Shelter, Sanctuary, Lair, Den
words.def: Phēdban, Phēdbanae, Phēdbanath (inan.) 1. A place where someone or something may be hidden, especially if from pursuit, danger, or trouble. 2. A place where a wild animal, especially a fierce or dangerous one, lives.
Phēt, Phētes, Phēte, Phēteth (an.) 1. A cephalopod mollusk with a light external shell that is typically of spiral shape and that has tentacles around the mouth.

words.loc: Lurk, Skulk, Hide, Sneak, Slink, Creep, Prowl
words.def: Pephēte, Pephētēt, Pephētekūs, Phēte, Phēto, Phētēned 1. To be or remain hidden so as to wait in ambush for someone or something. 2. To move or go in a stealthy manner, especially when in search of or when approaching prey.

words.loc: Low, Small, Short, Slight, Lowly, Humble, Poor, Insignificant, Poor, Obscure, Lower
words.def: Phō, Phohas, Phohae, Phohath (an.) Phoha, Phohās, Phoha, Phohāth (inan.) 1. Situated close to, or even below, the ground or another normal reference plane. 2. (Of birth, fortune, or worth) Low in status or importance. 3. (In the comparative) Less high in position. 4. (In the comparative) Situated on less high land or toward the sea.

words.loc: Dark, Dim, Black, Unlit, Shadowy, Vague, Unclear, Esoteric, Obscure, Unknown
words.def: Pith, Pith, Pith, Pitheth (an.) Pith, Pithas, Pithae, Pithath (inan.) 1. With little or no light. 2. (Of a color or object) Approaching black in shade; not reflecting much light. 3. (Figurative) Vague, indistinct, or ill-defined. 4. (Figurative) Likely to be understood only by people with certain knowledge or a specific interest. 5. (Poetic) Not discovered or known about. 6. (Poetic) Not well known.

Pithger, Pithgros, Pithger, Pithgeroth (an.) Pithger, Pithgras, Pithgrae, Pithgrath (inan.) 1. (Of an edge or blade) Lacking the ability to cut easily; not sharp. 2. (Of pain) Not intense or felt only slightly.

words.loc: Blunt, Dull, Weaken, Deaden, Dampen, Soften
words.def: Phithger, Phithgret, Phithgerkūs, Pithger, Pithgro, Pithgrened 1. To make or become less sharp. 2. To reduce the force of something. 3. To make a noise or sensation less intense.
Podgotrel, Podgotrelas, Podgotrelae, Podgotrelas (an.) 1. A horny covering on the upper surface of the tip of the finger and toe.

words.loc: Lock, Bolt, Bar, Latch, Clasp, Fastener, Buckle, Hasp, Clip, Hook, Pin
words.def: Pohar, Pohares, Pohar, Pohareth (an.) 1. A mechanism for keeping a door, lid, etc., fastened, typically operated only by a key of a particular form. 2. A device with interlocking parts used for fastening things together.

words.loc: Storm, Thunderstorm, Tempest, Gale, Squall, Hurricane
words.def: Pon, Pōs, Pon, Poneth (an.) 1. A violent disturbance of the weather with strong winds and usually rain, thunder, lightning, or snow.

words.loc: Stormy, Tempestuous, Turbulent, Cloudy, Turbid, Muddy, Murky, Opaque
words.def: Ponauk, Ponaukh, Ponauk, Ponauketh (an.) Ponauk, Ponaukas, Ponaukae, Ponaukath (inan.) 1. (Of the weather and skies) Characterized by strong winds and usually rain, thunder, lightning, or snow. 2. (Of liquids) Not transparent or clear.

words.loc: Sea, Ocean, Deep Sea, Sea in Motion, Sea Color, Anxiety, Stress
words.def: Pontor, Pontores, Pontor, Pontoreth (an.) 1. The open or high sea. 2. (Poetic) The deep; The depths of the sea. 3. (Poetic, In the Plural) The sea in motion; A collection of billowing waves. 4. (Poetic) The color of the sea. 5. (Figurative) Sea of thoughts. 6. (Figurative) Anxiety and/or stress.
Pontorīn, Pontorīs, Pontorīn, Pontorīneth (an.) Pontorīn, Pontorīnas, Pontorīnae, Pontorīnath (inan.) 1. (Technical) Of or pertaining to the open ocean.
Postān, Postānae, Postānath (inan.) 1. A living organism that feeds on organic matter, typically having specialized sense organs and nervous system and able to respond rapidly to stimuli.
1. (Idiomatic; lit. the animal in the story) An expression sometimes used when a person mentioned in the current conversation happens to arrive on the scene.

words.loc: Kraken, Great Red Krake, Giant Krake, Animalfish
words.def: Postānūs, Postānūmesh, Postānūmer, Postānūseth (an.) 1. An enormous, predatory, deep-sea nautilus that reaches a length of 40 feet or more.

words.loc: Go Up To, Come Up To, Go As Far As, Come As Far As, Go Before, Come Before, Approach, Enter the Presence Of
words.def: Pephel, Pephlit, Pephle, Poz, Pozit, Pozined 1. (Intransitive) To go to a position of equal advance to or slightly less advanced than another. 2. (Intransitive) To appear publicly in front of someone.
1. (LOC) At the side of. 2. (GEN) Towards the side of.

Praer, Praeris, Praeri, Praerith (an.) Praer, Praeras, Praerae, Praerath (inan.) NOTE: The stem "prae-" is used for the comparative and superlative grades of marīn 'close, near, available'. 1. Shorter in distance away or apart in space or time. 2. The shortest in distance away or apart in space or time

words.loc: Side, Half, Edge, Border, Boundary, Margin, Brim, Face, Surface, Plane
words.def: Praesh, Praeshae, Praeshath (inan.) 1. A position on the left or right of an object, place, or central point. 2. One of two equal or corresponding parts (left or right, top or bottom, front or back, etc.) into which something is or can be divided. 3. The outside limit of an object, area, or surface 4. A bounding straight edge of a two-dimensional shape. 5. A flat surface of a three-dimensional object.
Prākar, Prākres, Prāker, Prākreth (an.) 1. A tool with a heavy metal head mounted at right angles at the end of a handle, used for tasks such as breaking things and driving in nails.
Prāndo, Prāndae, Prāndōth (inan.) 1. A large underground chamber, typically of natural origin, in a hillside or cliff.

words.loc: Chasm, Gorge, Ravine, Gully, Canyon, Rift, Abyss
words.def: Prānor, Prānorae, Prānorath (an.) 1. A deep fissure in the earth, rock, or another surface.

Pepudeg, Pepudgit, Pepudgūs, Pudeg, Pudgit, Pudgined 1. To transmit information, instructions, or commands by means of a gesture, action, or sound. 2. To inform someone in advance of an impending or possible danger, problem, or other unpleasant situation.

Pudgeth, Pudgetis, Pudgethen (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. A hardy, domesticated mammal that has backward curving horns and, typically, a beard.

words.loc: Signal, Sign, Cue, Field Signal, Warison
words.def: Pudgiphek, Pudgiphkae, Pudgiphkath (inan.) 1. A gesture, action, or sound that is used to convey information, instructions, or commands, typically by prearrangement between the concerned parties. 2. A field signal given by a horn or trumpet.
Pudgoth, Pudgotrad, Pudgoter, Pudgotheth (an.) 1. The horny part of the foot of an ungulate animal. 2. A curved, pointed nail on each digit of the foot of an animal.

words.loc: Dinosaur, Ornithischian, Hadrosaur, Ceratopsid, Sauropod, Stegosaur, Ankylosaur, Pachycephalosaur
words.def: Pudkhar, Pudkharas, Pudkharae, Pudkharath (an.) 1. A category of dinosaur of a group whose members are typically quadrapedal, have beaks or bills, and are herbivorous.
Pun, Pūs, Pun, Puneth (an.) 1. A person who steals another's property, especially by stealth and without using force or violence.

words.loc: Steal, Thieve, Rob, Plunder, Loot, Purloin, Pilfer
words.def: Peben, Pebnit, Pebnekūs, Pun, Puno, Punined 1. To take another person's property without permission or legal right and without intending to return it.

words.loc: Thieving, Thievish, Larcenous, Inclined to Steal
words.def: Punauk, Punaukh, Punauk, Punauketh (an.) Punauk, Punaukas, Punaukae, Punaukath (inan.) 1. Alike to a thief. 2. Having a tendency to steal things.
Puned, Pundis, Pundi, Pundith (an.) Puned, Pundas, Pundae, Pundath (inan.) 1. Something that has taken illegally without the owner's permission and without an intention of returning.

words.loc: Horn, Antler, Tusk, Beak, Bill, Naval Ram, Branch, Arm, Wing, Flank, Side, Trumpet
words.def: Puruk, Pudgad, Pudeg, Puruketh (an.) 1. A hard growth of keratin that protrudes from the head of an animal. 2. One of a pair of elongated, pointed teeth that extend outside the mouth of an animal. 3. (Of birds, squids, turtles, etc.) A horny, projecting jaw. 4. The substances of which horns, tusks, and beaks are made. 5. Any substance similar to the material of a horn. 6. A projection at the prow of a warship used to pierce the hulls of other ships. 7. A major section of an organization. 8. The right or left side of an army's main body. 9. A part of a large building, especially one that projects from the main body. 10. An instrument made from an animal horn and played by lip vibration.
Purukphek, Purukphekae, Purukphekath (inan.) 1. Someone or something that blows a horn or trumpet.

Phāgo, Phāgōkūs, Phāge, Pāgo, Pāgo, Pāgōned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To have a desire to possess something or do something. 2. To wish for something to happen or be the case.

Pāgōl, Pāgōlae, Pāgōlath (inan.) 1. A strong feeling of wanting to have something. 2. A very strong sexual desire. 3. A very strong and selfish desire for something, especially wealth, power, or other possessions.

Pāgōs, Pāgōsh, Pāgōr, Pāgōseth (an.) Pāgōr, Pāgōras, Pāgōrae, Pāgōrath (inan.) 1. Feeling sexual excitement. 2. Having, showing, or tending to have feelings of sexual desire. 3. Having, showing, or tending to have strong feelings of greed.
Pāli, Pālae, Pālīth (inan.) 1. A large deer with large antlers, a sloping back, and a growth of skin hanging from the neck.

words.loc: Escape, Evade, Elude, Lose, Get Away, Break Out, Break Free, Slip Away
words.def: Phārāg, Phārāget, Phārāgūs, Pārāg, Pārāgo, Pārāgoned 1. (Intransitive) To free oneself from confinement, control, or pursuit.

words.loc: Corrupt, Pervert, Deprave, Distort, Warp, Defile, Sully, Taint
words.def: Phārāmbar, Phārāmbret, Phārāmbargūs, Pārāmbar, Pārāmbro, Pārāmbrened 1. To cause to become immoral or wicked. 2. To alter something from its original course, meaning, or state to a distortion or corruption of what was first intended. 3. To damage the purity or integrity of.
Phārār, Phārāret, Phārārgūs, Pārār, Pārāro, Pārārened 1. To enter or gain access to by force or illicit means. 2. To enter or gain access to (an organization, place, etc.) surreptitiously.

words.loc: Break Off, Snap Off, Pull Off, Detach, Sever, Separate
words.def: Phārātel, Phārātelet, Phārātelgūs, Pārātel, Pārātelo, Pārātelened 1. To remove something from a larger unit or whole, especially by bending, pulling, cutting, or slicing.
Pēs, Pēs, Penthen NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. A unit of measurement approximately 18 inches in length.

words.loc: Seal, Shut, Close Up, Plug, Stopper, Caulk, Jail, Imprison, Incarcerate
words.def: Pepō, Pepket, Pepohūs, Pō, Poko, Pokened NOTE: This verb is used in reference to closing things for a long period of time. See geges, ger "close" for a verb meaning to close temporarily. 1. To fasten or close securely, with no intention to be opened or breached in the near future. 2. To apply a nonporous coating to a surface to make it impervious. 3. To put or keep in prison or a place like a prison.

Pōs, Ponesh, Poner, Pōseth (an.) NOTE: Can be used pronominally or as a determiner. 1. (Interrogative) Asking for information specifying something, especially if semantically animate. 2. (Interrogative; In the dual) Asking for information specifying one thing from a set of two, especially if semantically animate. 3. (Interrogative; In the plural) Asking for information specifying one or more things from a set of more than two, especially if semantically animate.

words.loc: Repeal, Revoke, Abolish, Abrogate, Annul, Recall
words.def: Ēpūg, Ēpūgot, Ēpūgūs, Pūg, Pūgo, Pūgoned 1. To end a law, right, or formal agreement.

words.loc: Annulment, Nullification, Voiding, Repeal, Revocation, Aboliton, Abrogation
words.def: Pūgol, Pūgolae, Pūgolath (inan.) 1. The formal repeal of a law, right, or formal agreement.
Pūkar, Pūkres, Pūker, Pūkreth (an.) 1. One who proposes a law or bill to the people, senate, or king.

words.loc: Law, Bill, Proposal, Measure, Act, Proposed Law
words.def: Pūkūl, Pūkūlae, Pūkūlath (inan.) 1. A proposal to the people, senate, or king for passing a law or decree. 2. A proposed law, decree or bill.
1. In a secret way; without others knowing. 2. In a cautious and possibly surreptitious manner so as not to be seen or heard.
Pūphek, Pūphkae, Pūphkath (inan.) 1. A worded or expressed sentence, phrase, or word which asks for information, a reply, or a response.
Pūs, Punish, Puner, Pūseth (an.) 1. The action or crime of stealing.

words.loc: Blue, Green, Black, Dark Blue, Cobalt, Indigo, Dark Green, Forest Green, Swarthy, Dark-Skinned, Shining Black, Glossy Black
words.def: Rabash, Rabshes, Rabshe, Rabsheth (an.) Rabesh, Rabshas, Rabshae, Rabshath (inan.) 1. Of a color between purple and yellow, as of the sapphires or leaves. 2. Of a dark blue or dark green color. 3. (Of people, skin, or hair) Black or very dark brown in color. 4. (Poetic) Of a shining or glossy black color (as opposed to dul, dull black) or having a black color with bluish highlights, as of ravens or coal.
Rabzat, Rabzath, Rabzat, Rabzateth (an.) 1. Soft, sticky matter resulting from the mixing of earth and water.

Ram, Ramae, Ramath (inan.) 1. The usually green and flat organ that represents the most prominent feature of most vegetative plants. 2. The sharp, stiff, slender leaf of a conifer tree. 3. A sheet of a book.

words.loc: Right up to, As far as
words.def: 1. (LOC) Continues until it reaches a specific point or limit.
Rankar, Rankres, Ranker, Rankreth (an.) Ranker, Rankras, Rankrae, Rankrath (inan.) 1. Very cold in temperature.

words.loc: Rankaros, Arctic, Hell, Hades, Tartarus, Realm of Monsters
words.def: Rankaros, Rankarae, Rankarath (inan.) 1. The region around the North Pole. 2. An evil realm located in the far north where monsters are bred and from which the Mad One once ruled.
Rankrāsh, Rankrāshae, Rankrāshath (inan.) 1. Small white ice crystals formed on the ground or other surfaces when the temperature falls below freezing. 2. (Poetic) Especially cold or chilling water.
Rargither, Rargithra, Rargithrāth (inan.) 1. A hard, strong, gray or bluish-gray alloy of iron with carbon and usually other elements.
Relkhim, Relkhimae, Relkhimath (inan.) 1. A native or inhabitant of Relkor, which is the western region of Rhusagos.
Relkor, Relekrae, Relekrath (inan.) 1. A geographic region comprising the western parts of Rhusagos excluding Alensis.

words.loc: Lie, Recline, Be Situated, Rest, Sleep, Be Dead
words.def: Ārthen, Ārthahūs, Ārethne, Rethen, Rethno, Rethnaned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Intransitive) To rest in a horizontal position on a surface. 2. (Intransitive) To be placed or situated. 3. (Intransitive) To be dead.

words.loc: Law, Rule, Statute, Regulation, Act, Decree, Edict
words.def: Rethnal, Rethnalae, Rethnalath (inan.) 1. An individual rule as part of a system of law.
Rethnaus, Rethnaurae, Rethnaurath (inan.) 1. A prolonged military assault or a blockade of a city or fortress with the intent of conquering by force or attrition.
Rhosh, Rhoshae, Rhoshath (inan.) 1. A natural elevation of land of considerable size rising abruptly from the surrounding level.
Rhoshauk, Rhoshaukh, Rhoshauk, Rhoshauketh (an.) Rhoshauk, Rhoshaukas, Rhoshaukae, Rhoshaukath (inan.) 1. (Of a region) Having many mountains.
Rhoshkhon, Rhoshkhonae, Rhoshkhonath (inan.) 1. A low hill at the base of a mountain or mountain range. 2. An area of elevated land, typically hilly, at the base of a mountain or mountain range.

words.loc: Mountain, Mountainous, Mountaineer, Highlander, Outlaw, Brigand, Bandit, Robber
words.def: Rhoshīn, Rhoshīs, Rhoshīn, Rhoshīneth (an.) Rhoshīn, Rhoshīnas, Rhoshīnae, Rhoshīnath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to mountains. 2. Situated among or built in the mountains. 3. (Substantive Animate) A person who lives in a mountainous area, often with a connotation of being an outlaw or uncivilized. 4. (Substantive Animate) A fugitive from the law, especially one who has fled into the wilderness to evade capture. 5. (Substantive Animate) A member of a gang that typically operates in an isolated or lawless area.

words.loc: Tear, Rip, Shred, Rend, Lacerate, Gash, Slash, Maul, Mangle, Mutilate, Maim
words.def: Rerukh, Rerukhet, Rerukhūs, Rhukh, Rhukho, Rhukhened 1. To tear or cut into shreds. 2. (Of flesh or skin) To make deep cuts in. 3. To inflict a disfiguring injury on.

words.loc: Red, Dark Red, Crimson, Maroon, Blood Red, Orange, Pink, Gold, Yellow, White
words.def: Rhūs, Rhūsh, Rhūr, Rhūseth (an.) Rhūr, Rhūras, Rhūrae, Rhūrath (inan.) 1. Of a color at the end of the spectrum next to orange and opposite violet, as of blood, fire, or rubies. 2. Dark red (as opposed to zōrauk, bright red) of color. 3. Of a reddish-yellow color. 4. Of a color intermediate between red and white, as of carnations or salmon. 5. (Of gold) High-quality or pure (as opposed to iud, low-quality gold). 6. (Of the sun) Yellow or white in color.
Rhusagos, Rhusagorae, Rhusgorath (inan.) 1. The continental landmass lying directly to the north of Patrinor. (Literally meaning 'Red Land' or 'Bloody Land')

words.loc: Tavern, Taberna, Restaurant, Bar, Pub, Saloon, Store, Shop, Emporium, Inn, Hotel, Hostel
words.def: Ringodan, Ringodnae, Ringodnath (inan.) NOTE: When used in its sense as an inn, a ringodan is considered a smaller establishment. The word ghelgeglor "inn, caravansary" refers to a larger inn that might be used by wealthy travelers or large groups. 1. An establishment that sells food. 2. An establishment for the sale of alcoholic beverages. 3. An establishment that sells goods or services to the public. 4. An establishment providing housing, food, and drink, especially for travelers.
Ringor, Ringoras, Ringorae, Ringorath (an.) 1. A cephalopod mollusk with eight limbs, a soft body, strong beaklike jaws, and no internal shell.
Rodnūs, Rodnumesh, Rodnumer, Rodnūseth (an.) 1. A snake-like fish with a slender, elongated body and poorly developed fins.

words.loc: Snake, Serpent, Viper, Asp, Adder, Cobra
words.def: Rodon, Rodōs, Roden, Rodneth (an.) NOTE: See āpophos "snake" for a word that connotes non-venomous snakes. 1. Any type of venomous snake, especially if it has large, hinged fangs, a broad head, and a stout body.

Rog, Roges, Rog, Rogeth (an.) Rog, Rogas, Rogae, Rogath (inan.) 1. (Typically of a person) Old or aging. 2. Occuring before something else either in time or order. 3. (Of a tradition, belief, or convention) Having existed or continued for a long time.
Rāba, Rābās, Rāba, Rābāth (an.) 1. A tailless amphibian with a short, squat body, moist and smooth skin, and very long hind legs for leaping.
Rābando, Rābandae, Rābandōth (inan.) 1. Aquatic plants with large, round, floating leaves and large, typically cup-shaped, floating flowers.

Rālek, Rālekae, Rālekath (inan.) 1. The wind that originates in the west and blows in an eastward direction. 2. The direction toward the point of the horizon opposite to where the sun rises. 3. The western part of the world or of a specified country, region, or city.
Rālekīn, Rālekīs, Rālekīn, Rālekīneth (an.) Rālekīn, Rālekīnas, Rālekīnae, Rālekīnath (inan.) 1. Situated in the west or directed toward or facing the west. 2. Living in or originating from the west.
Rāpor, Rāpores, Rāpor, Rāporeth (an.) Rāpor, Rāporas, Rāporae, Rāporath (inan.) 1. Of, pertaining to, or related to the night. 2. Done, occurring, or active at night.

words.loc: Magic, Dark Magicks, Sorcery, Witchcraft, Dwimmer, Goety, Necromancy, Conjuration, Divination
words.def: Rās, Rāsh, Rār, Rāseth (an.) NOTE: This word specifically connotes magic of evil origins. See gathraes "thaumaturgy" for a word referring to benign or good magic. 1. Magic derived from the evil powers of That Which Is Not.

words.loc: Sorceror, Witch, Warlock, Occultist, Conjurer, Mage
words.def: Rātān, Rātānae, Rātānath (inan.) NOTE: This word specifically connotes a spellcaster who uses black magicks. See guthrūk "wizard" for a word referring to a spellcaster of good intentions. 1. Someone who uses or has skill with the manipulation of That Which Is Not, especially if they have evil intentions.
Rāzmaur, Rāzmaurae, Rāzmaurath (inan.) 1. A wicked and powerful spirit summoned from That Which Is Not. 2. The host of Liches as a whole.
Ierūber, Ierūbrit, Ierūbrikūs, Rūber, Rūbro, Rūbrined 1. To keep safe from harm or injury.
Rūbrāsh, Rūbrāshae, Rūbrāshath (inan.) 1. The quality of being friendly, generous, and considerate. 2. Friendly, helpful, or cooperative feelings or attitude.
Rūkar, Rūkres, Rūker, Rūkareth (an.) 1. A married person considered in relation to their spouse. 2. A married man considered in relation to his spouse.
Rūpesh, Rūpeshae, Rūpeshath (inan.) 1. An intense feeling of deep affection. 2. A great interest and pleasure in something.

words.loc: Kind, Nice, Tender, Caring, Gentle, Warm, Thick, Woolly
words.def: Rūpor, Rūbres, Rūber, Rūporeth (an.) Rūber, Rūbras, Rūbrae, Rūbrath (inan.) 1. Having or showing a friendly, generous, and considerate nature. 2. (Of clothes or coverings) Made of a material that helps the body to retain heat; suitable for cold weather.
Sam, Samae, Sameth (inan.) 1. A visible mass of water droplets suspended in the air. 2. Any mass of dust, steam, or smoke resembling a cloud.

words.loc: Cloudy, Clouded, Overcast, Misty, Foggy, Hazy, Murky
words.def: Samael, Samaeles, Samael, Samaeleth (an.) Samael, Samaelas, Samaelae, Samaelath (inan.) 1. Covered with or characteristic of clouds. 2. Full of or accompanied by mist or fog.
1. (Adverbial Allative) In a direction from lower to higher; toward a higher place; in a course toward the source or origin.

words.loc: Mist, Fog, Haze, Murk, Smog, Vapor, Steam, Fumes
words.def: Sambar, Sambrae, Sambrath (inan.) 1. A thick cloud suspended in the atmosphere at or near the ground which obscures or restricts visibility. 2. Cloudy, diffused matter suspended in the air.
Sar, Saras, Sarae, Sarath (an.) 1. A reptile that typically has a long body and tail, four legs, movable eyelids, and a rough, scaly, or spiny skin.
Sargi, Sargīs, Sargi, Sargīth (an.) 1. Large algae growing in the sea. 2. Long, trailing plants or climbing plants such as ivys and vines.
Sargim, Sargīs, Sargim, Sargimeth (an.) 1. A large, predatory, semiaquatic reptile with long jaws, long tail, short legs, and a horny textured skin.
Sebdil, Sebdilae, Sebdilath (inan.) 1. The alternate rising and falling of the sea. 2. The water as affected by the tide.

Sezbet, Sezbetkūs, Sezbete, Sebet, Sebdo, Sebdened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To move as a fluid from one position or another. 2. (Of liquids) To fall continuously and abundantly. 3. To cause to flow in a stream, either out of a container or into one. 4. (Of tidewater) To move away or recede from the land.

words.loc: Weak, Feeble, Frail, Puny, Soft, Pliant, Flexible, Unhealthy, Infirm, Sick, Ill
words.def: Sel, Seles, Sel, Seleth (an.) Sel, Selas, Selae, Selath (inan.) 1. Lacking the power to perform physically demanding tasks; lacking physical strength and energy. 2. Easy to mold, cut, compress, or fold. 3. Not having or showing good health.
Sem, Sēs, Sem, Semeth (an.) 1. A semiaquatic fish-eating mammal of the weasel family, with an elongated body, dense fur, and webbed feet.

Sereth, Sesthet, Serethkūs, Seth, Setho, Sethened NOTE: This verb is used chiefly when climbing steep slopes. Use lelot, lot "climb" instead when referring to gentle slopes. 1. To go or come up a high and steep surface, especially by means of one's feet and sometimes hands.
Setum, Setmae, Setmath (inan.) 1. A thing made or used for sitting on. 2. A seat fastened on the back of an animal for riding, typically made of leather and raised at the front and rear.

words.loc: Neck, Throat, Gullet, Strait, Channel, Sound, Narrows, Inlet
words.def: Shem, Shemae, Shemath (inan.) 1. The part of a person's or animal's body connecting the head to the rest of the body. 2. The passage which leads from the back of the mouth of a person or animal. 3. A narrow part of something, resembling a neck in shape or position. 4. A narrow passage of water connecting two seas or two other large areas of water.

words.loc: Bandage, Dressing, Band, Swathe, Strip, Ribbon
words.def: Shoruhar, Shoruhares, Shoruhar, Shoruhareth (an.) 1. A strip of cloth or similar material used to protect or support a wound or injury. 2. A strip of cloth or other material used for strengthening, coupling, or ornamenting.

words.loc: Do, Act, Behave, Make, Effect, Accomplish, Achieve, Perform, Move, Impel, Put into Motion, Drive, Conduct, Administer, Direct, Govern, Discuss, Debate, Last, Continue
words.def: Sazhā, Sazhkūs, Sazhke, Shā, Sho, Shaened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. NOTE: Sazhā, shā is atelic as opposed to pephek, phek "do, make", which is telic. 1. To perform an action, the precise nature of which is often unspecified. 2. To conduct oneself in a certain manner for an indefinite amount of time. 3. To create something that does not continue to exist after the maker stops. 4. To successfully bring about or reach a desired objective or result. 5. To do something in front of an audience, such as acting or music, often in order to entertain. 6. To take action or begin to act. 7. To incite to action or motion. 8. (Of livestock) To compel to move. 9. To organize and carry out. 10. To manage or supervise the conduct, performance, or execution of something. 11. (Of civil, political, and legal situations) To converse or argue over a particular topic. 12. (In the Passive; Of a process, activity, or state of things) To persist for a prolonged period of time.

words.loc: Expel, Evict, Drive Out, Cast Out, Exile, Banish, Eject, Ejaculate, Climax, Cum
words.def: Sazheg, Sazhget, Sazhāgūs, Shāg, Shāgo, Shāgened 1. To force someone or something to leave a place. 2. (Intransitive; Of a man or male animal) To eject semen from the body at the moment of sexual climax.

Shāgened, Shāgendis, Shāgendi, Shāgendith (an.) Shāgened, Shāgendas, Shāgendae, Shāgendath (inan.) 1. Sent away from a country or place as an official punishment. 2. (Substantive Animate) A person who is forced to live away from their country or land.

words.loc: Doer, Actor, Agent, Representative, Medium, Means, Instrument, Plaintiff, Prosecutor, Claimant
words.def: Shākar, Shākres, Shāker, Shākreth (an.) 1. Someone who does, performs, or executes. 2. A person who acts on behalf of another group. 3. A person or thing that takes an active role or produces a specified effect. 4. A person who initiates a case in a court of law.
Sazhkrum, Sazhkrumet, Sazhkruhūs, Shākrum, Shākrumo, Shākrumed 1. To move about or travel about, especially over a wide area or in search of adventure.

words.loc: Go Past, Go By, Pass By, Elapse, Wear On, Surpass, Exceed, Transcend, Outclass, Pass Away, Die
words.def: Sezhalat, Sezhāltet, Sezhālte, Shālat, Shālto, Shāltened 1. To travel past without stopping. 2. (Of time) To travel past or go by. 3. To go beyond or be greater than something. 4. (Figurative) To stop living.

words.loc: Pass Time, Spend Time, Live, Stay, Visit
words.def: Sazhimbar, Sazhimbro, Sazhimbarkūs, Shāmbar, Shāmbro, Shāmbrened 1. To use time doing something or being somewhere. 2. (Of a person) Live somewhere temporarily.

words.loc: Action, Act, Process, Procedure, Operation, Doing, Deed, Performance, Gesture, Gesticulation, Plot, Storyline, Lawsuit
words.def: Shāpesh, Shāpshae, Shāpshath (inan.) 1. A series of actions or steps taken in order to achieve a particular end. 2. The action or process of carrying out or accomplishing an action, task, or function. 3. An action of representing a character in a play, concert, or other form of entertainment. 4. A gesture, especially a dramatic one, used instead of speaking or to emphasize one's words. 5. The course of a story, comprising a series of incidents which are gradually unfolded. 6. A claim or dispute brought to a court of law for adjudication.

words.loc: Crook, Staff, Stick, Rod, Cane, Crutch
words.def: Shāphek, Shāphkae, Shāphkath (inan.) 1. The hooked staff of a shepherd. 2. A long stick used as support when walking or climbing.

words.loc: Active, Busy, Bustling, Energetic, Lively, Animated, Vigorous, Spirited
words.def: Shāpor, Shāpres, Shāper, Shāporeth (an.) Shāper, Shāpras, Shāprae, Shāprath (inan.) 1. Constantly engaged in action. 2. Having many things to do. 3. Full of energy.

words.loc: Almost, Nearly, About, More or Less, Not Quite, Practically, Virtually, Pretty Much
words.def: 1. (Used sentence-finally) Not quite; very nearly.

words.loc: Come On, C'mon, Let's Go, Nice, Hell Yeah
words.def: Interjection 1. A cheer or expression of support or encouragement.

words.loc: Drive Away, Flush, Frighten Away, Scare Off, Rustle, Steal
words.def: Sazhtel, Sazhtelet, Sazhtelgūs, Shātel, Shātlo, Shātlened 1. (Of animals) To compel to leave. 2. (Typically of animals) To cause to flee out of or away from. 3. (Of livestock) To round up and steal.
Shātelkar, Shātelkres, Shātelker, Shātelkreth (inan.) 1. A person who rounds up and steals livestock.

Shātlened, Shātlendis, Shātlendi, Shātlendith (an.) Shātlened, Shātlendas, Shātlendae, Shātlendath (inan.) 1. (Of livestock) Taken illegally without the owner's permission and without an intention of returning.

words.loc: Press, Squeeze, Wring, Compress, Stamp, Imprint, Impress
words.def: Sezhēr, Sezhēret, Sezhērgūs, Shēr, Shēro, Shērened 1. To exert weight or force against. 2. To apply pressure from two or more sides at once. 3. To twist or squeeze something tightly so that liquid is forced out. 4. To mark by pressing quickly and heavily. 5. To give an official marking to by impressing or imprinting a symbol.

words.loc: Stamp, Imprint, Mark, Footprint, Footstep, Print, Tracks, Trail, Traces, Spoor, Wax Seal, Signet, Symbol, Emblem, Insignia, Crest, Device
words.def: Shēs, Shēsh, Shēr, Shēseth (an.) 1. An indentation made by stamping. 2. The impression left by a foot or shoe on the ground or a surface. 3. (In the plural) A mark or line or marks left by a person, animal, or vehicle in passing. 4. A piece of wax, lead, or other material with an individual design stamped into it, attached to a document to show that it has come from the person who claims to have issued it. 5. (By extension) A heraldic or symbolic shape used to represent a nation, organization, person, or family.
Sermi, Sermīt, Sermīkūs, Simi, Simo, Simīned 1. (Intransitive) To come together as a group. 2. (Transitive) To collect or bring together. 3. To manage, care for, or guard a herd.

words.loc: Weasel, Ermine, Stoat, Marten, Polecat, Ferret, Mink
words.def: Simin, Simines, Simin, Simineth (an.) 1. Any weasel-like mammal, characterized by a slender body and a long tail.

Simūs, Simūsh, Simūr, Simūseth (an.) 1. The daily event occuring in the early afternoon wherein the Sun passes behind the Moon for approximately an hour. 2. An afternoon rest or nap, especially one taken during the daily eclipse.

words.loc: Heart, Feelings, Emotions, Intention, Intent, Purpose, Objective, Goal
words.def: Sin, Sinae, Sinath (inan.) 1. A hollow muscular organ that pumps the blood through the circulatory system by rhythmic contraction and dilation. 2. (Figurative) One's feelings and emotions, especially when considered as part of one's character. 3. The reason for which something is done or created or for which something exists
Seren, Sernet, Sernekūs, Sin, Sino, Sined 1. To beat or sound with a strong, regular rhythm.
Sinauk, Sinaukh, Sinauk, Sinauketh (an.) Sinauk, Sinaukas, Sinaukae, Sinaukath (inan.) 1. Ready to face and endure danger or pain.

words.loc: Spin, Turn Over, Turn Around, Rotate, Revolve, Gyrate, Stir, Mix, Whisk, Swirl, Churn, Blend
words.def: Ser, Serit, Seriskūs, Sir, Siro, Sīrined 1. (Intransitive) To move around an axis or center. 2. (Intransitive) To change one's direction of travel. 3. (Transitive) To change the direction or orientation of, especially by rotation. 4. To mix a substance using a continuous circular movement.

words.loc: Pivot, Fulcrum, Hinge, Axis, Bright Star, North Star, Polaris
words.def: Sīril, Sīrilae, Sīrilath (inan.) 1. A thing on which something turns. 2. An imaginary or fixed straight line around which an object spins. 3. The brightest star in the sky, being the very distant red dwarf star of the binary star system that Nīmlad is within.

words.loc: Wheel, Roller, Court, Courtroom, Curia, Senate House, Meeting Hall
words.def: Siskar, Siskres, Sisker, Siskreth (an.) 1. A circular device capable of rotating on its axis, facilitating movement or transportation or performing labour in machines. 2. A hall, chamber, or place where legal proceedings are administered. 3. A hall, chamber, or place where a legislative or governmental body (such as a senate) meets. 4. (Figurative) A closed, two-dimensional geometric shape where all points on a plane are at an equal distance from a fixed center.
Serkorum, Serkormet, Serkormekūs, Siskorum, Siskormo, Siskormed 1. To enclose an object completely in any flexible, thin material such as fabric or paper.

words.loc: Cycle, Sequence, Loop, Series, Progression, Circuit, Orbit, Circle, Rotation
words.def: Sispesh, Sispeshae, Sispeshath (inan.) 1. A process that returns to its beginning and then repeats itself in the same sequence. 2. A single complete movement all the way around a center or an axis. 3. The act of turning around a centre or an axis.
Serek, Serkit, Serkūs, Sok, Soko, Sokined 1. To make white.
