Soket, Soktas, Soktae, Soktath (an.) Soket, Soktās, Sokta, Soktāth (inan.) NOTE: Considered a shade of iud 'yellow'. 1. Of a light yellowish-brown or yellowish-white color, as of butter or light skin. 2. (Of people or skin) Light brown, tan, or olive in color.
Sokhūs, Sokhumesh, Sokhumer, Sokhūseth (an.) 1. A fungal growth that typically takes the form of a domed cap on a stalk, with gills on the underside of the cap, especially if inedible or poisonous.
1. Used to connect verbs that are to be taken jointly.
Sudus, Sudush, Suder, Suduseth (an.) Suder, Sudras, Sudrae, Sudrath (inan.) 1. Free from dirt, marks, or stains. 2. Not mixed or adulterated with any other substance or material.

Sulum, Sulūs, Sulem, Sulumeth (an.) 1. A tall vertical structure used either as a support for a building or as an ornament or monument. 2. A tall and slim post, usually tapering upwards, that is used to support sails on a ship. 3. (Vulgar slang) The penis; The male reproductive organ.
Suril, Surilas, Surilae, Surilath (an.) 1. The main bones of the head considered as a unit.

Surthā, Surthakae, Surthakath (inan.) 1. An organ of soft nervous tissue contained in the skull of vertebrates, functioning as the coordinating center of sensation and intellectual and nervous activity.
Sāsh, Sāshae, Sāshath (inan.) 1. A precious, shiny grayish-white metal. 2. A polished or smooth surface (as of glass) that reflects a clear image.
Sāshīn, Sāshīs, Sāshīn, Sāshīneth (an.) Sāshīn, Sāshīs, Sāshīnae, Sāshīnath (inan.) 1. Resembling silver in color. 2. Coated with silver. 3. Made of silver.
Sāshūk, Sāshūkh, Sāshūki, Sāshūketh (an.) 1. Someone or makes and repairs things made out of silver.
Sēgop, Sēgopes, Sēgope, Sēgopeth (an.) 1. A parent of one's father or mother. 2. (Colloquial) Used to address any old person.

words.loc: Shadow, Umbra, Shade, Shelter, Ghost, Phantom, Wraith, Spirit, Specter, Apparition
words.def: Sīmon, Sīmae, Sīmath (inan.) 1. A dark shape or area produced by a body coming between rays of light and a surface. 2. Comparative darkness and coolness caused by shelter from direct sunlight. 3. (Figurative) A soul of a dead person which is believed to appear or become manifest to the living, typically as a nebulous image.
Sō, Sokh, Sok, Soheth (an.) Sok, Sokas, Sokae, Soketh (inan.) 1. Of the color of milk or fresh snow; the opposite of black.
Sōmak, Sōmakh, Sōmāk, Sōmāketh (an.) 1. A small, pale, soft-bodied insect similar to an ant that lives eusocially in large colonies, typically within a mound of earth.
1. (LOC) In the time following (an event or another period of time).

Sūlith, Sūlthae, Sūlthath (inan.) NOTE: This word specifically refers to the hind part of an animal or person's body. See nadīr "rear surface" for a word referring to the backmost surface or part of an object. 1. (Anatomy) The rear of the body, especially the part between the neck and the end of the spine and opposite the chest and belly.
1. (Adverbial) The day after today.
Sūrīhurūk, Sūrīhurūkis, Sūrīhurūki, Sūrīhurūkith (an.) 1. The head of government of a city-state or province, especially if elected.

words.loc: First, Foremost, Earliest, Initial, Principal, Chief, Prime, Main, Preeminent, Head
words.def: Sūrīn, Sūrīs, Sūrīn, Sūrīneth (an.) Sūrīn, Sūrīnas, Sūrīnae, Sūrīnath (inan.) 1. Coming before all others in time or order. 2. Highest in position, rank, or importance. 3. Of, pertaining to, or related to the head.

words.loc: Head, Head of Hair, Top, Tip, Summit, Peak, Point, End, Front, Origin, Source, Spring, Chief, Leader, Capital City
words.def: Sūs, Sumesh, Sumer, Sūseth (an.) 1. The part of the body of a person or animal which contains the brain, mouth, and main sense organs. 2. The collection of fine, threadlike strands growing from the head of a person. 3. The topmost, foremost, or leading part of something. 4. (Of rivers) The source. 5. (Figurative) A person in charge of something. 6. (Figurative) A city designated as a legislative seat by the government or some other authority; the most important city within a country or subdivision of it.
Sūseph, Sūsphae, Sūsphath (inan.) 1. The skin covering the head, excluding the face 2. The back of a person's or animal's neck.
Sūzned, Sūznedae, Sūznedath (inan.) 1. The back part of the head.

words.loc: Verb-to-Verb, Negative, Opposite Action, Reverse Action, Un-
words.def: Productive Affix 1. Used to form a verb meaning the opposite or reverse action of the original verb.
Productive Affix 1. Expresses negation of the original word.

words.loc: Vampire, Shrike, Banshee, Race of Vampires
words.def: Taengaur, Taengaurae, Taengaurath (inan.) 1. A member of the race of vampires, which are accounted as Younger Children. 2. The race of vampires as a whole.
Taengos, Taengosh, Taengor, Taengoseth (an.) 1. A mainly nocturnal mammal capable of sustained flight, with membranous wings that extend between the fingers and connect the forelimbs to the body.

words.loc: Board, Plank, Beam, Table, Counter, Desk
words.def: Tag, Tagae, Tagath (inan.) 1. A long, thin, flat piece of wood or other hard material, used for various purposes. 2. A flat surface with markings for playing board games. 3. A piece of furniture with a flat top and one or more legs, providing a level surface on which objects may be placed.
Tedgi, Tedgīt, Tedgīkūs, Tagi, Tago, Tagīned 1. (Of people) To take food, especially prepared food, into the body.
Tagīl, Tagīlae, Tagīlath (inan.) 1. The main meal of the day, taken either around midday or in the evening.
Productive Affix 1. Used to form a verb meaning a rapid or brief and uncontrolled variant of the action described by the original verb.
Tamar, Tamres, Tamer, Tamareth (an.) 1. The purple-blue gas giant around which Nīmlad orbits, visible at all times of the day and night. 2. The light reflected from the Moon.

words.loc: Croak, Crow, Caw, Squawk, Screech, Rasp, Cry
words.def: Tamāre, Tamārēt, Tamārēkūs, Tamaure, Tamauro, Tamaurēned 1. (Of a frog or bird) Make a characteristic deep, hoarse sound, typically repeatedly.

words.loc: Gorge, Overeat, Glut, Binge, Stuff, Feast, Devour, Gobble
words.def: Tambebar, Tambebret, Tambebarkūs, Tambar, Tambro, Tambrened 1. To eat to the point of excess. 2. To eat large amounts of.
Tambarbesh, Tambarbeshae, Tambarbeshath (inan.) 1. Excess in eating.
Tambarkar, Tambarkres, Tambarker, Tambarkreth (an.) 1. An excessively greedy eater.

words.loc: Beg, Plead, Entreat, Implore, Urge, Exhort, Beseech, Solicit
words.def: Tamēpu, Tamēpūkūs, Tamēpe, Tambu, Tambo, Tambūned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To plead with someone for something.
Tambūk, Tambūkis, Tambūki, Tambūkith (an.) 1. A person who begs. 2. A person suffering from poverty.

Tamdrak, Tamdrakūs, Tamdrake, Tamdak, Tamdako, Tamdakened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To kill in a cruel or violent way, typically in large numbers.

words.loc: Massacre, Slaughter, Mass Murder, Butchery, Carnage, Bloodbath
words.def: Tamdakil, Tamdaklae, Tamdaklath (inan.) 1. An indiscrimate and brutal killing of a large number of people.

words.loc: Stumble, Trip, Slip, Meet, Encounter, Come Upon, Happen Upon, Run Into
words.def: Tamēg, Tamēgūs, Tamēge, Tameg, Tango, Tangened 1. To momentarily lose one's balance. 2. To meet or find someone or something by chance or unexpectedly.

Tamemkrum, Tamemkrumet, Tamemkruhūs, Tamkrum, Tamkrumo, Tamkrumened 1. (Of a group of people or animals) To seperate and quickly move away in different directions. 2. To run away from someone or something.

words.loc: Motion, Movement, Locomotion, Stirring, Turbulence, Agitation, Commotion
words.def: Tamzhaus, Tamzhaurae, Tamzhaurath (inan.) 1. The action or process of changing physical location. 2. The action of briskly stirring or disturbing something. 3. Violent or unsteady movement of air or liquid. 4. The action of exciting public feeling.

words.loc: Do, Make, Behave, Do Persistently, Continue, Persist, Carry On, Keep At, Drive, Hunt, Chase, Pursue, Row, Agitate, Disturb, Churn, Stir Up, Excite, Urge, Rouse, Awaken, Operate, Steer, Pilot
words.def: Tarzhā, Tarzhat, Tarzhakūs, Tamzhā, Tamzho, Tamzhaened 1. To persist with something. 2. (Of livestock) To compel to move in a specified direction. 3. (Of animals) To follow after in order to catch up with or kill. 4. To propel a vessel with oars. 5. (Of liquids or gases) To stir or disturb. 6. (Of action, passion, etc.) To arouse or excite. 7. To cause to stop sleeping. 8. (Of vehicles) To guide and control the movement of.
Tamzhākar, Tamzhākres, Tamzhāker, Tamzhākreth (an.) 1. A person who drives something. 2. A person who drives livestock over long distances. 3. A person who drives a chariot.
Tamzhīn, Tamzhīs, Tamzhīn, Tamzhīneth (an.) Tamzhīn, Tamzhīnas, Tamzhīnae, Tamzhīnath (inan.) 1. Capable of being moved.

words.loc: Flutter, Flit, Flitter, Hover, Dance, Fly About, Go To and Fro, Go Back and Forth
words.def: Timēgo, Timēgōt, Timēgōkūs, Tamēgo, Tamēgo, Tamegōned 1. (Of winged creatures) Fly unsteadily or hover by flapping the wings quickly and lightly. 3. (Figurative) To move between two points quickly and repeatedly.

words.loc: Withhold, Suppress, Repress, Control, Check, Curb, Inhibit, Hold Back, Keep Back
words.def: Thamū, Thamūt, Thamūkūs, Tamū, Tamo, Tamūned 1. To hold back an emotion, reaction, or process.

Tandebeg, Tandebgat, Tandebgūs, Tandebek, Tandebgo, Tandebgened 1. To change one's decision or opinion about something suddenly and/or without prior thought. (Implies fickleness and/or indecisiveness)
Tangil, Tangilae, Tangilath (inan.) 1. An unexpected coming together with someone or something.
Tangegin, Tangegnet, Tangegihūs, Tangin, Tangino, Tangined 1. To set down in writing a related series of events in a factual and detailed way.
Tanir, Tanenret, Tanenrekūs, Tankir, Tanegro, Tanegrened 1. To rapidly flood. (Typically due to heavy rain) 2. To flow out in a rapid and plentiful stream, often suddenly.

Tanrauk, Tanraukh, Tanrauk, Tanrauketh (an.) Tanrauk, Tanraukas, Tanraukae, Tanraukath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to the male gender. 2. Having the qualities stereotypically associated with men by the Patrin: brave, emotional, diligent.
Tanrīn, Tanrīs, Tanrīn, Tanrīneth (an.) Tanrīn, Tanrīnas, Tanrīnae, Tanrīnath (inan.) 1. Of, pertaining to, or resembling the Moon (that is, Tamar, the gas giant).
Pronoun 1. Used by a speaker to refer to the addressee.
Taran, Tarās, Taren, Taraneth (an.) Taren, Tarnas, Tarnae, Tarnath (inan.) 1. Giving or showing firm and constant support or allegiance to a person or institution.

words.loc: Harden, Solidify, Stiffen, Strengthen, Toughen, Reinforce, Fortify, Secure
words.def: Tharen, Tharnet, Tharangūs, Taren, Tarno, Tarnined 1. To make or become harder, stronger, or tougher. 2. To strengthen or support an object or substance. 3. To provide with defensive works as protection against attack.
Tarer, Tarerit, Tarerikūs, Tarir, Tarit, Tarined 1. To rotate, revolve, spin, or turn rapidly.

words.loc: Vortex, Gyre, Whirlpool, Maelstrom, Whirlwind, Tornado, Waterspout, Dust Devil, Fire Whirl
words.def: Tariskar, Tariskres, Tarisker, Tariskreth (an.) 1. Any swirling matter that moves in the form of a spiral or column.

words.loc: Trust, Believe In, Rely Upon, Depend On
words.def: Tharnash, Tharnashet, Tharnashkūs, Tarnash, Tarnasho, Tarnashened 1. To believe in the reliability, truth, ability, or strength of. 2. To depend on with full trust or confidence.
Tarnus, Tarnurae, Tarnurath (inan.) 1. The ability to do something that frightens one.

words.loc: Faith, Belief, Confidence, Trust, Loyalty, Fidelity
words.def: Tarnāsh, Tarnāshae, Tarnāshath (inan.) 1. Trust in the reality of something; often without empirical evidence, direct experience, or observation. 2. Complete trust or confidence in someone or something. 3. Faithfulness or devotion to some person, cause, or nation

words.loc: Lone, Alone, Solitary, Hermit, Recluse, Loner
words.def: Tauk, Taukh, Tauk, Tauketh (an.) Tauk, Taukas, Taukae, Taukath (inan.) 1. Having no one else present or no companions. 2. Done or existing alone. 3. (Substantive Animate) A person living in solitude.

words.loc: Infertile, Infecund, Sterile, Barren, Unproductive, Unprofitable, Fruitless, Futile
words.def: Taukūger, Taukūgros, Taukūger, Taukūgroth (an.) Taukūger, Taukūgras, Taukūgrae, Taukūgrath (inan.) 1. (Of a person, animal, or plant) Unable to reproduce. 2. (Of land) Unable to sustain crops or vegetation. 3. (Of a business or activity) Not yielding profit or financial gain. 4. Incapable of producing any useful result.
Tebeg, Tebgat, Tebebgūs, Tebek, Tebgo, Tebgened 1. To change one's decision or opinion about something.
Tebrīn, Tebrīs, Tebrīn, Tebrīneth (an.) Tebrīn, Tebrīnas, Tebrīnae, Tebrīnath (inan.) 1. Not fit or suitable for eating.

Tedrakos, Tedrakosh, Tedragor, Tedrakoseth (an.) Tedragor, Tedragras, Tedragrae, Tedragrath (inan.) 1. Incapable of causing harm or danger. 2. Not guilty of a crime or offense. 3. Having done no wrongdoing.
Tegbīn, Tegbīs, Tegbīn, Tegbīneth (an.) Tegbīn, Tegbīnas, Tegbīnae, Tegbīnath (inan.) 1. Not stable.
Tegbīnāsh, Tegbīnāshae, Tegbīnāshath (inan.) 1. The state of being unstable.

words.loc: Huge, Large, Vast, Enormous, Extraordinary, Unnatural, Great, Terrible, Mighty, Powerful
words.def: Tegendes, Tegendesh, Tegender, Tegendreth (an.) Tegendes, Tegendras, Tegendrae, Tegendrath (inan.) 1. Very large. 2. Very unusual or remarkable. 3. Contrary to the ordinary course of nature; abnormal. 4. (Figurative) Much larger or much stronger than what is typical. 5. Used as an epithet for Thobrauk and, more rarely, Mālgal.
Tegzhael, Tegzhaeles, Tegzhael, Tegzhaeleth (an.) Tegzhael, Tegzhaelas, Tegzhaelae, Tegzhaelath (inan.) 1. Having, bringing, or resulting from bad luck.
Tekhī, Tekhihet, Tekhihūs, Tekī, Tekihet, Tekihened 1. To undo the lock of something by using a key.
1. (ERG) Used to indicate removal or separation. 2. (ERG) Used to indicate a source or origin.

words.loc: Intangible, Untouchable, Incorporeal, Imaginary, Imagined, Fictional, Made-Up
words.def: Telnīn, Telnīs, Telnīn, Telnīneth (an.) Telnīn, Telnīnas, Telnīnae, Telnīnath (inan.) 1. Unable to be touched or grasped. 2. Existing only in the imagination.

Tembor, Tembres, Tember, Tembreth (an.) Tember, Tembras, Tembrae, Tembrath (inan.) 1. (Of a person or animal) Not showing or feeling nervousness, anger, or other strong emotions; a result of peacefulness or tranquility as opposed to control.

words.loc: Flock, Herd, Pack, Swarm, Group, Team, Clique, Company, Band, Fellowship, Troop, Troupe
words.def: Temel, Temles, Temel, Temelath (an.) 1. A group of (typically) smaller animals, namely: (Of birds, sheep, etc.) A flock. (Of cattle, etc.) A herd. (Of wolves, dogs, etc.) A pack. (Of insects, etc.) A swarm. 2. (Figurative) A small grouping of people, namely: (As a general term) A group, clique, crowd, company, band, or troop. (In sports) A team. (In the theater or circus) A troupe.

words.loc: Common, Ordinary, Usual, Commonplace, Simple, Plain
words.def: Temlauk, Temlaukh, Temlauk, Temlauketh (an.) Temlauk, Temlaukas, Temlaukae, Temlaukath (inan.) 1. Occuring, found, or done often. 2. Having no special or distinctive characteristics.
Temlūk, Temlūkh, Temlūki, Temlūkith (an.) 1. A person who tends and rears sheep. 2. The owner or keeper of a herd of domesticated animals.
Tenemeb, Tenembut, Tenembutkūs, Tenap, Tembo, Tembuned 1. To make tranquil and quiet.

words.loc: Crime, Offense, Felony, Misdemeanor, Misdeed, Wrongdoing
words.def: Tendu, Tendūs, Tendu, Tendūth (an.) 1. A breach of a law or rule.

words.loc: Undo, Unmake, Reverse, Retract, Rescind, Repeal
words.def: Temener, Temenrikūs, Temenre, Tener, Tenro, Tenrined 1. To cancel or reverse the making, effects or results of.

words.loc: Maimed, Crippled, Handicapped, Disfigured, Lame, Cripple
words.def: Tepen, Tebned, Teben, Tepeneth (an.) Teben, Tebnaed, Tebnae, Tebnath (inan.) 1. Partially or wholly deprived of some part of the body, usually by wounding or injury. 2. (Substantive Animate) A person who has impaired physical abilities because of deformation, injury, or amputation of a part or parts of the body.
Tepher, Tephret, Tephergūs, Teper, Tebro, Tebrened 1. To fail to remember. 2. To erase the memory of something or pretend there is no memory of something, especially as a result of coercion.

words.loc: Unwind, Unwrap, Untwist, Unravel, Untangle, Disentangle, Unbind
words.def: Tezher, Tezhret, Tezhrekūs, Ter, Tero, Terened 1. To separate something that is wound up. 2. To open or undo wrappings or bindings. 3. To free something from entanglement.

words.loc: Enter, Go Into, Set Foot In, Put On, Don, Clothe, Dress, Get In, Get On, Climb Aboard, Embark, Invade, Attack, Assail, Assault, Have Sex With, Penetrate, Fuck, Screw, Shag
words.def: Tethes, Tetheskūs, Tethere, Teres, Tezho, Tezhened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Intransitive) To come or go into. 2. (Instransitive) To put on an item of clothing. 3. (Intransitive) To enter or get on a ship or other vehicle. 4. To enter by force, usually in order to conquer. 5. (Intransitive) To insert the penis into the vagina or anus of a sexual partner.
Tergis, Tergish, Tergir, Tergireth (an.) 1. A stinging, flying insect that has a narrow waist, lives eusocially in hives made of paper, and raises its larvae on a diet of insects.
Tezhmi, Tezhmīt, Tezhmīkūs, Termi, Termo, Termined 1. To divide a thing into distinct elements or parts.

words.loc: Wall, House Wall, Room Wall, Partition, Screen, Divider
words.def: Termīkar, Termīkres, Termīker, Termīkreth (an.) NOTE: Termīkar "wall, partition" is used in reference to the walls of a house or room. See aegus "wall, fence" for a word meaning the walls of a city or fort. 1. A structure acting either as the exterior of or dividing part within a structure.
Tes, Tesh, Ter, Tereth (an.) 1. A stinging, flying insect that collects nectar and pollen, produces wax and honey, and lives eusocially in hives.

Tethobrīn, Tethobrīs, Tethobrīn, Tethobrīneth (an.) Tethobrīn, Tethobrīs, Tethobrīnae, Tethobrīnath (inan.) 1. (Of food) Not flavored with salt, pepper, herbs, or other spices. 2. (Of food or drink) Lacking flavor.

words.loc: Civilization, Society, Ecumene, Earth, World, Nīmlad
words.def: Tethros, Tethrosh, Tethror, Tethrorath (an.) 1. (Substantive Animate) The regions of Nīmlad inhabited by the Elder Children, as opposed to the wild.
Tezban, Tezbanae, Tezbanath (inan.) 1. (Typically of wild bees) A structure wherein bees are housed.

words.loc: Not One or the Other, Neither Fish nor Fowl, Ambiguous
words.def: 1. (Idiomatic; lit. a person of no colors) Not easily categorized or not fitting well in a given group or situation.
Tezbilka, Tezbilkae, Tezbilkāth (inan.) 1. Any plant with leaves, seeds, or flowers used for flavoring or food. 2. A plant with white flowers and aromatic leaves that is used as a culinary herb.

words.loc: Disprove, Refute, Rebut, Challenge, Oppose, Contradict
words.def: Tezhēr, Tezhērit, Tezhērikūs, Tezhner, Tezhnero, Tezhnerined 1. To prove that something is false. 2. To dispute the truth or validity of. 3. To deny the truth of something by asserting the opposite.
Thalīn, Thalīna, Thalīnāth (inan.) 1. A crustacean with a cylindrical body and five pairs of legs, the first pair of which are modified as pincers.

words.loc: Winter, Flood Season, Late Autumn, Late Fall
words.def: Thar, Tharae, Tharath (inan.) 1. The coldest season of the year, defined by the period of time when the Lash River is flooding; it spans from the middle of September to the middle of January.
Tethar, Tethrit, Tetharkūs, Thar, Tharo, Tharined 1. To spend the winter in a particular place.

words.loc: Wintry, Hibernal, Brumal, Cold, Bleak, Ready for Winter, Suitable for Winter
words.def: Tharīn, Tharīs, Tharīn, Tharīneth (an.) Tharīn, Tharīnas, Tharīnae, Tharīnath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to winter. 2. Characteristic of winter, especially in feeling or looking very cold and bleak. 3. (Of places, especially military camps) Suitable for getting through the winter.
Thekhomban, Thekhombanae, Thekhombanath (inan.) 1. A collection or space for a collection of scrolls, books, or other forms of stored information.
Thekhon, Thekhonae, Thekhonath (inan.) 1. A collection of pages bound together to hinge at one edge, containing written material, pictures, etc.

words.loc: Long, Lengthy, Vast, Spacious, Broad, Wide, Boundless
words.def: Thel, Thelis, Theli, Thelith (an.) Thel, Thelas, Thelae, Thelath (inan.) 1. Measuring a great distance from end to end. 2. (Of regions or lands) Of very great extent.
Thelmeger, Thelmegris, Themegri, Thelmegrith (an.) 1. A terrestrial arachnid with pincers similar to those of a lobster and a venomous stinger at the end of its jointed tail.
Thelpīn, Thelpīnes, Thelpīn, Thelpīneth (an.) 1. An upper limb of the body extending from the shoulder to the wrist. 2. (Of cephalopods) An elongated, boneless, flexible limb of some animals.
Thelāsh, Thelāshae, Thelāshath (inan.) 1. The measurement or extent of something from end to end; the greater of two or the greatest of three dimensions of a body.
Thengor, Thengorae, Thengorath (inan.) 1. The bottom part of a tree left projecting from the ground after most of the trunk has fallen or been cut down. 2. (Figurative; Derogatory) A stupid person.

words.loc: Fell, Hew, Cut Down, Knock Down
words.def: Ūthengor, Ūthengorit, Ūthengorgūs, Thengor, Thengoro, Thengorined 1. To cut or knock down a tree.
Therpor, Therpres, Therper, Therporeth (an.) Therper, Therpras, Therprae, Therprath (inan.) 1. Kind and pleasant.

words.loc: Like, Love, Admire, Be Fond Of, Appreciate, Respect, Esteem, Prize, Value
words.def: Thesu, Thesūt, Thesūkūs, Theru, Thero, Therūned 1. To find agreeable, enjoyable, or satisfactory. 2. To regard with admiration and respect.

words.loc: Salt, Brine, Saltwater, Seawater, Sailor, Wit
words.def: Thobos, Thobrae, Thobrath (inan.) 1. A white crystalline substance that gives seawater its characteristic taste and is used for seasoning or preserving food. 2. (Poetic) Water strongly consisting of salt. 3. (Poetic) Water hailing from the sea. 4. (Slang) An old sailor. 5. (Figurative) Humor, especially when clever or quick.
Thobriger, Thobrigros, Thobriger, Thobrigroth (an.) Thobriger, Thobrigras, Thobrigrae, Thobrigrath (inan.) 1. (Of food) Having had salt, pepper, herbs, or spices added.

Thobrīn, Thobrīs, Thobrīn, Thobrīneth (an.) Thobrīn, Thobrīs, Thobrīnae, Thobrīnath (inan.) 1. Tasting of, containing, or preserved with salt. 2. Of salty water or seawater. 3. Showing or characterized by wit.

words.loc: Curse, Hex, Jinx, Malediction, Evil Eye
words.def: Tholōkil, Tholōkilae, Tholōkilath (inan.) 1. A solemn utterance intended to invoke a supernatural power to inflict harm or punishment on someone or something.

words.loc: Strong, Powerful, Mighty, Hard, Firm, Solid, Dense, Rigid, Stiff
words.def: Thon, Thones, Thone, Thoneth (an.) Thon, Thonas, Thonae, Thonath (inan.) 1. Having the power to move heavy weights or perform other physically demanding tasks. 2. Solid and firm; not easily broken, bent, or pierced.
Thundo, Thundae, Thundōth (inan.) 1. A small stone, especially one rounded by the action of water.

Thundokh, Thundokis, Thundokthen (an.) NOTE: This noun is pluralia tantum, meaning it is only ever declined in the plural. 1. A loose aggregation of small, fragmented rocks used for laying on the beds of roads. 2. Sand that has larger grains and a rough texture.
Thurīn, Thurīs, Thurīn, Thurīneth (an.) Thurīn, Thurīnas, Thurīnae, Thurīnath (inan.) 1. Suitable or good enough to live in.
Tethus, Tethret, Tethuskūs, Thus, Thuro, Thurined 1. (Intransitive) To live in or at a specified place.
Thuskar, Thuskres, Thusker, Thuskreth (an.) 1. A person or animal that lives in or is present at a place. 2. A person that lives in a particular area.
Thā, Thakae, Thakath (inan.) 1. A soft fatty substance in the cavities of bones, in which blood cells are produced. 2. The dense inner part of a tree trunk, yielding the hardest timber.
Thānus, Thānrae, Thanrath (inan.) 1. A part or division of a building enclosed by walls, floor, and ceiling.
Thār, Thāres, Thār, Thāreth (an.) 1. A woody plant which is smaller than a tree and has several main stems arising at or near the ground.
Thārduk, Thārdukae, Thārdukath (inan.) 1. The plants in a woodland whoch only reach a relatively low height. 2. A dense, but generally small, growth of shrubs, bushes, and small trees.
Tothōr, Tothōrit, Tothōrgūs, Thōr, Thōro, Thōrined 1. To speak very softly using one's breath without one's vocal cords, especially for the sake of privacy.
Thōs, Thomesh, Thomer, Thōseth (an.) 1. A light or gentle wind.

words.loc: Strength, Might, Force, Energy, Vigor, Power, Authority
words.def: Thōs, Thonrae, Thonrath (inan.) 1. The quality of being strong. 2. Strength or energy as an attribute of physical action or movement. 3. The ability to do or undergo something. 4. The ability to coerce, influence or control.

words.loc: Take Off, Remove, Doff, Disrobe, Undress
words.def: Tōbos, Tōbzet, Tōboskūs, Tobos, Tobzo, Tobzened 1. To remove clothing from one's or another's body.
Tōgon, Tōgnet, Tōgohūs, Togon, Togno, Togoned 1. To refuse to take notice of or acknowledge.

words.loc: Poetic, Flowery, Aesthetic, Moving, Epic, Saga, Legend, Romance, Narrative
words.def: Tolkīn, Tolkīs, Tolkīn, Tolkīneth (an.) Tolkīn, Tolkīs, Tolkīnae, Tolkīnath (inan.) 1. Related to or used in poetry. 2. Having an imaginative or sensitively emotional style of expression. 3. (Substantive Inanimate) A long poem, typically derived from ancient oral tradition, narrating the deeds of heroic or legendary figures from the past.

words.loc: Historian, Chronicler, Annalist, Reporter, Recorder, Chronologer
words.def: Tolok, Tolokh, Tolek, Toloketh (an.) 1. A person who writes accounts of important or historical events.

words.loc: Now, By Now, Already, Previously, Anymore, Again, Moreover
words.def: NOTE: To refer to the literal, present moment, mūznūs "right now" should be used. 1. Prior to some specified time, either past, present, or future. 2. So soon. 3. Differently from the immediate past, from a more remote past or possible future, or differently from all other times. 4. (In negative constructions) From a given time onwards. 5. (In transitions) In addition to what has been said.
1. (Adverbial; With an ordinal number) Days before the present time.

Tongōkar, Tongōkres, Tongōker, Tongōkreth (an.) NOTE: To distinguish butterflies from moths, one must use the phrase 'hanīn tongōkar' (day butterfly) for butterflies and 'rāpor tongōkar' (night butterfly) for moths. 1. A nectar-feeding, flying insect with two pairs of large, typically brightly colored wings. 2. A chiefly nocturnal flying insect related to butterflies.
Tonshar, Tonsharas, Tonsharae, Tonsharath (an.) 1. A monster resembling a giant reptile, having wings and the ability to breathe fire.

words.loc: Swamp, Marsh, Bog, Fen, Wetland, Morass, Mire, Quagmire
words.def: Torban, Torbanae, Torbanath (an.) 1. An area of low-lying, uncultivated ground where water collects.
Tuglelōk, Tuglelōket, Tuglelōkūs, Tuglōk, Tuglōko, Tuglōkened 1. To talk with excessive pride and self-satisfaction about one's achievements, possessions, or abilities.

Tuglōken, Tuglōkenos, Tuglōken, Tuglōkenoth (an.) Tuglōken, Tuglōkenas, Tuglōkenae, Tuglōkenath (inan.) 1. Showing excessive pride and self-satisfaction in one's achievements, possessions, or abilities. 2. (Substantive Animate) A person who boasts about achievements or possessions.

words.loc: Big, Large, Sizeable, Great, Grand, Important, Prominent
words.def: Tuk, Tukes, Tuke, Tuketh (an.) Tuk, Tukas, Tukae, Tukath (inan.) 1. Of considerable size, extent, or intensity. 2. Of considerable ability, quality, or eminence.

words.loc: Grow, Increase, Expand, Enlarge, Multiply, Mature, Develop, Sprout, Germinate
words.def: Tetek, Tetkit, Tetkūs, Tuk, Tuko, Tukined 1. (Intransitive) To become larger. 2. (Intransitive; Of a living thing) To undergo natural development by increasing in size and changing physically. 3. (Intransitive; Of plants and fungi) To germinate and develop.

words.loc: Fat, Blubber, Lard, Grease, Tallow, Suet
words.def: Tukh, Tukhae, Tukhath (inan.) 1. A natural oily or greasy substance occurring in animal bodies, especially when deposited as a layer under the skin or around certain organs. 2. Any fatty substance made from rendered animal fat.
Tukshael, Tukshaelae, Tukshaelath (inan.) 1. A cylinder or block of wax or tallow with a central wick that is lit to produce light as it burns.
Tukāsh, Tukāshae, Tukāshath (inan.) 1. The relative physcial extent of something.
Tulsutor, Tulsutres, Tulsuter, Tulsutoreth (an.) 1. A large, boar-like mammal with black, bristly fur, a long snout, and an omnivorous diet.
Tulus, Tulush, Tules, Tuluseth (an.) 1. A large, generally omnivorous mammal with shaggy hair, a very small tail, and flat feet.

words.loc: Not Want, Not Wish, Be Unwilling, Refuse, Do Not, Prohibitive Mood
words.def: Tepāgo, Tepāgōkūs, Tepāge, Tāgo, Tāgo, Tāgōned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To be not willing to have or do something. 2. To indicate that one is not willing to have or do something. 3. (Imperative; Auxiliary) A polite way of expressing that the lexical verb is not to be done.

Thān, Thahūs, Thāne, Tān, Tāno, Tāned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To construct a physical structure or framework by putting parts or material together.
Tānus, Tānrae, Tānrath (inan.) 1. A structure with a roof and walls.
Tās, Tanesh, Taner, Tāseth (an.) 1. An adult male human. 2. (Of animals) Male.
Tēz, Tamēzut, Tamēzgūs, Tāz, Tāzo, Tāzuned 1. (Intransitive) Speak in order to give information or express ideas or feelings.
Tāzbor, Tāzbres, Tāzber, Tāzboreth (an.) Tāzber, Tāzbras, Tāzbrae, Tāzbrath (inan.) 1. Tending to talk a lot.
1. In a friendly, informal manner.
Tāzul, Tāzulae, Tāzulath (inan.) 1. Casual or unconstrained conversation or reports about other people, typically involving details that are not confirmed as being true.
Totōr, Totōrit, Totōrgūs, Tōr, Tōro, Tōrined 1. (Of wind) To move creating an air current. 2. To expel air through pursed lips.
Tōrauk, Tōraukh, Tōrauk, Tōrauketh (an.) Tōrauk, Tōrauaks, Tōraukae, Tōraukath (inan.) 1. (Of weather, a period of time, or a place) Marked by or exposed to strong winds.
Tōs, Tomesh, Tomer, Tōseth (an.) 1. The perceptible natural movement of the air, especially in the form of a current of air blowing from a particular direction.
Tōzbar, Tōzbrae, Tōzbrath (inan.) 1. the state of the atmosphere at a place and time in regards to heat, dryness, sunshine, wind, rain, etc.
Tūrsulaes, Tūrsulaesh, Tūrsulaer, Tūrsulaeseth (an.) Tūrsulaer, Tūrsulaeras, Tūrsulaerae, Tūrsulaerath (inan.) 1. Liable to forget. 2. Careless and unaware.
Uder, Udrae, Udrath (inan.) 1. A sequence of rhythmic movements usually performed to music.
Ūdri, Ūdrīt, Ūdre, Udri, Udro, Udrīned 1. To move with rhythmic movements, especially in time to music.
Udrikar, Drīkres, Drīker, Drīkreth (an.) 1. A person who dances or whose profession is dancer.
Ugdāshbu, Ugdāshbūs, Ugdāshbu, Ugdāshbūth (an.) 1. A droplet of water formed as dew.

words.loc: Against, Opposite, Facing, Opposite to, Contrary to
words.def: 1. (LOC) Positioned so as to face something. 2. (COM) Conflicting with.
Umak, Uhae, Uhath (inan.) 1. A prickly, scrambling vine or shrub, especially one that produces blackberries or raspberries.

words.loc: Feel, Experience, Undergo, Think, Believe, Suppose, Vote, Elect
words.def: Ūneba, Ūmbākūs, Ūnebae, Umba, Umbo, Umbāned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Copulative) To experience an emotion or other mental or internal state. 2. To have a particular opinion, belief, or idea about someone or something. 3. Give or register a vote. 4. (In the Passive) To have been chosen to hold public office or some other position by voting.

words.loc: Feeling, Emotion, Sentiment, Opinion, View, Way of Thinking, Perspective, Outlook, Vote
words.def: Umbās, Umbārae, Umbārath (inan.) 1. A natural instinctive state of mind deriving from one's circumstances, mood, or relationships with others. 2. A belief, judgement, or perspective that someone has formed. 3. A person's general attitude to life or a particular topic. 4. (Of politics) A formal indication of a choice between candidates or courses of action.
Undauk, Undaukh, Undauk, Undauketh (an.) Undauk, Undaukas, Undaukae, Undaukath (inan.) 1. Containing or made up of stones or rocks.

Undo, Undae, Undōth (inan.) 1. A hard earthen substance that can form large rocks. 2. (Poetic) A precious or semiprecious stone, especially when cut or polished or engraved. 3. (Philosophy, Alchemy) One of the six elements by which the Patrin categorize the world's substances. 4. (Slang) Either of the two organs that produce sperm in men and other male animals.

Undoger, Undogros, Undoger, Undogeroth (an.) Undoger, Undogras, Undograe, Undograth (inan.) 1. Covered or encrusted with barnacles. 2. Having or showing experience, knowledge, and good judgement, especially as a result of old age or worldliness.
Undonūs, Undonumesh, Undonumer, Undonūseth (an.) 1. A marine crustacean with an external shell, which attaches itself permanently to a variety of surfaces.
Undīn Karko, Undīnae Karkae, Undīnath Karkōth (inan.) 1. A plantlike organism that typically forms a low crusty, leaflike, or branching growth on rocks, walls, and trees.
1. (Idiomatic; lit. stone's salt) A layer of sea salt or of a salt-like substance encrusting something.
Undōphek, Undōphekae, Undōphekath (inan.) 1. A person who cuts stone from a quarry or who shapes and carves it for use.

words.loc: Swell, Swell Up, Bloat, Distend, Inflate, Balloon
words.def: Ūngol, Ūngolet, Ūngolgūs, Ungol, Ungolo, Ungolned 1. (Intransitive) To become larger or rounder in size.

words.loc: Swelling, Pimple, Tumor, Bump, Lump, Boil, Welt, Blister
words.def: Ungolbesh, Ungolbeshae, Ungolbeshath (inan.) 1. (Of the body) An abnormally enlarged or inflamed region or spot.

words.loc: Swollen, Distended, Puffy, Inflated, Bulging, Tumid, Hard, Erect, Tumescent
words.def: Ungolned, Ungolendis, Ungolendi, Ungolendith (an.) Ungolned, Ungolendas, Ungolendae, Ungolendath (inan.) 1. Having swelled up. 2. (Of the penis) Enlarged and rigid, especially as a result of sexual excitement.
Unkhon, Makhnae, Makhneth (inan.) 1. An edible soft fruit from a bramblebush that consists of a cluster of drupelets.
Ursul, Ursula, Ursulāth (inan.) 1. Something remembered from the past. 2. The act of remembering. 3. The ability of the brain to record information with the facility to recall them later at will.
Ursulaes, Ursulaesh, Ursulaer, Ursulaeseth (an.) Ursulaer, Ursulaeras, Ursulaerae, Ursulaerath (inan.) 1. Bringing strong ideas, memories, or feelings of.

Ursulban, Ursulbanae, Ursulbanath (inan.) 1. A monument by which someone or something is remembered. 2. A monument erected to commemorate a victory over one's foes, typically displayed prominantly in a city-state and designed to further the political career of the winning general.

words.loc: Serve, Attend, Wait Upon, Subject To, At the Orders of
words.def: Ūshel, Ūshlut, Ūshlikus, Ushel, Ushlo, Ushlined 1. To provide a service or product. 2. To care for someone requiring attention. 3. To wait on someone or their instructions as an attendent. 4. To be under the command of someone or something, typically a king or other leader.
Ūtel, Ūtelet, Ūtelgūs, Utel, Utelo, Utelened 1. To state that one refuses to admit the truth or existence of.
1. (LOC) Situating beyond the boundaries or confines of.
Unproductive Affix 1. Prefix that weakens/diminishes the idea of the original word.
Uthbebar, Uthbebret, Uthbebarkūs, Uthbar, Uthbro, Uthbrened 1. To take small bites out of.

words.loc: Best Friend, Dear Friend, Close Friend
words.def: Uther, Uthrus, Uther, Uthruth (an.) 1. A person's closest or very close friend.
Uthgāz, Uthgāza, Uthgāzāth (inan.) 1. A young tree, especially one with a slender trunk.
Uthkash, Uthkashae, Uthkashath (inan.) 1. A stabbing weapon, similar to a sword but with a short, double-edged blade.
Uthkhon, Uthkhōs, Uthkhen, Uthkhoneth (an.) 1. An enslaved child.

words.loc: Give Reluctantly, Give Stingily, Give Unwillingly
words.def: Thēman, Thēmnet, Thēmahūs, Uthman, Themno, Themaned 1. To give something with reluctance or unwillingness.

Uthmīs, Uthmīnrae, Uthmīnrath (inan.) 1. A small circular band, typically made of precious metals and with gemstones, worn on the finger as an ornament. 2. A piece of jewelry worn on the ear. 3. An ornamental band, hoop, or chain worn on the wrist or arm.
Uthnen, Uthnenae, Uthnenath (inan.) 1. A small field or enclosure where livestock is kept.
Uthphel, Uthphelae, Uthphelath (inan.) 1. A small, narrow river.

Uthrāba, Uthrābās, Uthrāba, Uthrābāth (an.) 1. The tailed aquatic larva of an amphibian (frog, toad, newt, or salamander), breathing through gills and lacking legs until the later stages of its development.
Utor, Utres, Uter, Utoreth (an.) 1. An omnivorous, domesticated mammal with sparse, bristly hair, hooves, and a flat snout for rooting in the soil.

words.loc: Domestic, Family, Household, Familial, Familiar, Customary
words.def: Uzhlīn, Uzhlīs, Uzhlīn, Uzhlīneth (an.) Uzhlīn, Uzhlīs, Uzhlīnae, Uzhlīnath (inan.) 1. Of or pertaining to a household or family. 2. Well-known from close or long association. 3. According to the customs or usual practices of a particular society, community, or person.
Ushlūk, Uzhlūkh, Uzhlūki, Uzhlūkith (an.) 1. An individual hired to perform regular household or other duties. 2. A high-ranking slave.

Uzhul, Uzhlae, Uzhlath (inan.) 1. All of the persons living within a given house; a family including attendants, servants, and slaves. 2. A group of attendants or servants, especially of someone considered important. 3. A group of warriors or nobles accompanying a king or other leader.

Sezag, Sezagūs, Sezage, Zag, Zago, Zagened NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. (Intransitive) Move at a speed faster than a walk, never having both or all the feet on the ground at the same time. 2. (Transitive) Compete with another or others to see who is fastest at covering a set course or achieving an objective.
Zagar, Zagres, Zager, Zagreth (an.) 1. A two-wheeled horse-drawn vehicle used in warfare and racing.
Zagbesh, Zagbeshae, Zagbeshath (inan.) 1. A competition between runners, horses, vehicles, boats, etc., to see which is the fastest in covering a set course.
Zagūk, Zagūkis, Zagūki, Zagūkith (an.) 1. A person who runs. 2. A person who competes in races.
Zarmokros, Zarmokrosh, Zarmokror, Zarmokroseth (an.) 1. A free-swimming aquatic animal with a gelatinous body that is typically transparent and has stinging tentacles around the edge.
Zarūtor, Zarūtres, Zarūter, Zarūtoreth (an.) 1. A carnivorous, aquatic mammal that is typically gregarious and has both a beaklike snout and a curved fin on the back.

words.loc: Sigh, Groan, Moan, Mourn, Grieve, Lament, Sorrow, Bemoan, Bewail
words.def: Zhaush, Zhaushit, Zhautkūs, Zaush, Zausho, Zaushined 1. (Intransitive) Emit a long, deep, audible breath expressing sadness, relief, tiredness, or a similar feeling. 2. (Transitive) Feel or show deep sorrow or regret for.
Zaushil, Zaushilae, Zaushilath (inan.) 1. Deep sorrow, especially that caused by someone's death.

words.loc: Sadness, Unhappiness, Sorrow, Misery, Sad, Unhappy, Sorrowful
words.def: Zaut, Zautas, Zautae, Zautath (inan.) 1. A feeling of sorrow or unhappiness.
Zauthānus, Zauthānrae, Zauthānrath (inan.) 1. A place for the confinement of people accused or convicted of a crime.
1. (Adverbial) On or in the course of the present day.
Zhohar, Zhohares, Zhohar, Zhohareth (an.) 1. A person or animal that fights. 2. A person who serves in an army, especially if said army is a militia.

words.loc: Laugh, Chuckle, Giggle, Ridicule, Mock, Tease, Jeer, Scorn, Deride
words.def: Sozhol, Sozholet, Sozholgūs, Zhol, Zholo, Zholened 1. To make the spontaneous sounds and movements of the face and body that are the instinctive expressions of lively amusement and sometimes also of contempt or derision. 2. To subject (someone or something) to contemptuous and dismissive language or behavior.

words.loc: Funny, Amusing, Humorous, Hilarious, Comical, Joke, Jest, Quip, Pun, Gag, Bit
words.def: Zholbor, Zholbres, Zholber, Zholboreth (an.) Zholber, Zholbras, Zholbrae, Zholbrath (inan.) 1. Causing laughter or amusement. 2. (Substantive Inanimate) A thing that someone says to cause amusement or laughter.

words.loc: Laughter, Mockery, Teasing, Smile, Grin, Smirk
words.def: Zholil, Zholilae, Zholilath (inan.) 1. The action or sound of laughing. 2. Teasing and contemptuous language or behavior directed at a particular person or thing. 3. A pleased, kind, or amused facial expression, typically with the corners of the mouth turned up and the front teeth exposed.

Sozhlur, Sozhluret, Sozhlurgūs, Zholur, Zholuro, Zholurened 1. To form one's features into a pleased, kind, or amused expression, typically with the corners of the mouth turned up and the front teeth exposed.
Zholus, Zholurae, Zholurath (inan.) 1. The quality of being amusing or comic, especially as expressed in literature or speech.
Zhomban, Zhombanae, Zhombanath (inan.) 1. The piece of ground on which a battle is or was fought.

words.loc: Army, Levy, Troops, Host, Militia, Military
words.def: Zhombar, Zhombrae, Zhombrath (inan.) 1. An organized military force equipped for fighting on land.

words.loc: Anger, Ire, Wrath, Rage, Fury, Angry, Mad, Irate, Enraged, Furious
words.def: Zhombor, Zhombras, Zhombrae, Zhombrath (inan.) 1. (Substantive Inanimate) A strong and unpleasant feeling of displeasure, hostility or antagonism towards someone or something.
Zhomphek, Zhomphekae, Zhomphekath (inan.) 1. Metal coverings worn by soldiers or warriors to protect the body in battle.

words.loc: Handle, Manage, Tackle, Address, Deal With, See To, Attend To, Work On, Sort Out
words.def: Sozhon, Sozhohūs, Sozhone, Zhon, Zhono, Zhoned NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To make determined efforts to resolve a situation, problem, or task.

words.loc: Aggressive, Hostile, Antagonistic, Combative, Belligerent, Bellicose, Confrontational, Pugnacious
words.def: Zhon, Zhonos, Zhon, Zhonoth (an.) Zhon, Zhonas, Zhonae, Zhonath (inan.) 1. Ready or likely to attack or confront.

words.loc: Beard, Moustache, Beard Hair, Facial Hair
words.def: Zhāruk, Zhādgae, Zhādgath (inan.) 1. A growth of hair around the lips, on the chin, and on the lower cheeks of a man's face.
Zhē, Zhekh, Zhek, Zheheth (an.) Men, Menas, Menae, Menath (inan.) 1. The entity or entities indicated or understood from context, especially if less remote in some way than one designated "that".

words.loc: Deceive, Lie, Swindle, Cheat, Trick, Delude, Fool
words.def: Zezhner, Zezhnerit, Zezhnerikūs, Zhēr, Zhēro, Zhērined 1. To cause someone or something to believe something that is not true, typically in order to gain some personal advantage.
Zhēreg, Zhērgae, Zhērgath (inan.) 1. A frightening or unpleasant dream.
Zhērgar, Zhērgares, Zhērgar, Zhērgareth (an.) 1. A person who tells lies or misleads others.

words.loc: Dream, Daydream, Vision, Fantasy, Vision, Hallucination
words.def: Zhēs, Zhērae, Zhērath (inan.) 1. A series of thoughts, images, and sensations occurring in a person's mind during sleep.

words.loc: False, Untrue, Incorrect, Wrong, Lie, Untruth, Falsehood, Fib, Deceit, Deception
words.def: Zhēzmūs, Zhēzmūsh, Zhēzmūr, Zhēzmūseth (an.) Zhēzmūr, Zhēzmūras, Zhēzmūrae, Zhēzmūrath (inan.) 1. Not in accordance with fact or reality. 2. (Substantive Inanimate) An intentionally false statement.

words.loc: More, Additional, Most, Almost All, Nearly All
words.def: Zhīr, Zhīris, Zhīri, Zhīrith (an.) Zhīr, Zhīras, Zhīrae, Zhīrath (inan.) NOTE: The stem "zhī-" is used for the comparative and superlative grades of azor 'good'. 1. A greater or additional amount, degree, or proportion of. 2. Greatest in amount, degree, or proportion.

words.loc: Battle, Combat, Conflict, Engagement, Action, Campaign, Offensive, Operation, Expedition, Electioneering
words.def: Zhōs, Zhonish, Zhoner, Zhōseth (an.) 1. An encounter in which all the divisions of an army are or might be engaged. 2. (In the Plural) A series of operations undertaken to achieve a set goal, especially if those are military or political goals.
Zhōz, Zhōzas, Zhōzae, Zhōzath (an.) 1. A brief battle between small groups, usually as part of a longer or larger battle or war.

Zhōzūk, Zhōzūkis, Zhōzūki, Zhōzūkith (an.) 1. A person sent out to gather and bring back information; especially, one employed in war to gain information about the enemy and ground. 2. An attendant, scout, or guard who travels ahead of or beside a moving group, vehicle, or procession.
1. Without difficulty or effort.

words.loc: Easy, Simple, Trivial, Calm, Peaceful, Safe, Light, Lightweight
words.def: Zhūlud, Zhūldes, Zhūled, Zhūldeth (an.) Zhūled, Zhūldas, Zhūldae, Zhūldath (inan.) 1. Requiring little skill or effort. 2. (Of a period of time or way of life) Free from troubles or problems. 3. Of little weight.
Zimūk, Zimūkh, Zimūki, Zimūketh (an.) 1. A person who is able, through supernatural insight, to see the future.
Zonāk, Zonākae, Zonākath (inan.) 1. An arranged, formal combat or contest between two private persons, often over a matter of honor.
Zīr, Zīros, Zīr, Zīreth (an.) 1. The organ within the mouth used for tasting, licking, swallowing, and for the articulation of speech. 2. (Figurative) Tongue-shaped things.

words.loc: Fire, Soul, Spirit, Life Force, Firewood
words.def: Zōr, Zōres, Zōr, Zōreth (an.) 1. The state of combustion or burning that produces flames. 2. Wood burned as fuel. 3. (Philosophy, Alchemy) One of the six elements by which the Patrin categorize the world's substances. 4. (Figurative) The spiritual or immaterial part of a living being.

words.loc: Red, Bright Red, Scarlet, Cherry, Orange, Vermillion, Fiery, Ruddy
words.def: Zōrauk, Zōraukh, Zōrauk, Zōrauketh (an.) Zōrauk, Zōraukas, Zōraukae, Zōraukath (inan.) NOTE: Considered a shade of rhūs 'red'. 1. Bright red of color, as of fire or cardinals. 2. Reddish yellow of color. 3. Reddish in color, especially of the face, fire, or sky at sunset.
Zōrbu, Zōrbūs, Zōrbu, Zōrbūth (an.) 1. A stream of hot, burning gas from something on fire.

words.loc: Agreement, Arrangement, Contract, Bargain, Deal, Pact
words.def: Āgbus, Āgburae, Āgburath (inan.) 1. A negotiated and sometimes legally binding arrangement between two parties, especially if related to business or financial matters in some way.

words.loc: Agree, Concur, Accord, Match, Fit, Correspond, Suit, Go With, Complement, Harmonize, Be Certain, Be Decided
words.def: Āgkhab, Āgkhabet, Āgkhabgūs, Āgeb, Āgbo, Āgbened 1. To have the same opinion about something. 2. To have a close similarity or match almost exactly. 3. To add to or match with something in a way that enhances or improves it.

words.loc: Garment, Clothes, Clothing, Attire, Garb, Dress, Outfit
words.def: Ānshor, Ānshoras, Ānshorae, Ānshorath (inan.) 1. (Substantive Inanimate) An item worn to cover the body.

Āpophos, Āpophosh, Āpophor, Āpophoseth (an.) NOTE: This word often connotes snakes that lack venomous bites. See rodon "snake, viper" for a word referring to venomous snakes. 1. (Substantive Animate) A long, limbless reptile which has no eyelids, a short tail, and jaws that are capable of considerable extension, especially if it lacks venom.

Arthambraes, Arthambraesh, Arthambraer, Arthambraeseth (an.) Arthambraer, Arthambraeras, Arthambraerae, Arthambraerath (inan.) 1. Next to something else. 2. (Substantive Inanimate; Of countries or areas) A region, country, or land bordering another.
Ē, Ekae, Ekath (inan.) 1. A point or part that is equally distant from all sides, ends, or surfaces of something.
Ēbri, Ēbrīs, Ēbri, Ēbrīth (an.) 1. A domesticated carnivorous mammal that typically has a long snout, an acute sense of smell, nonretractable claws, and a barking, howling, or whining noise.

words.loc: Cub, Puppy, Kitten, Whelp, Baby, Young, Offspring
words.def: Ēbrīla, Ēbrīlās, Ēbrīla, Ēbrīlath (an.) 1. (Of canines, felines, bears, etc.) A young offspring of a carnivorous mamal, especially of dogs.

words.loc: Litter, Brood, Young, Hatch, Nest, Family
words.def: Ēbrīlbar, Ēbrīlbarae, Ēbrīlbarath (inan.) 1. A family of young animals, produced at one hatching or birth.
Ēbrīn, Ēbrīs, Ēbrīn, Ēbrīneth (an.) Ēbrīn, Ēbrīnas, Ēbrīnae, Ēbrīnath (inan.) 1. Relating to or resembling a dog or dogs.

words.loc: Good, Fine, Acceptable, Suitable, Appropriate, Virtuous, Moral, Ethical, Enjoyable, Pleasant, Agreeable, Desirable
words.def: Ēgor, Ēgres, Ēger, Ēgoreth (an.) Ēger, Ēgras, Ēgrae, Ēgoreth (inan.) NOTE: See 'os' for the comparative and superlative grades. 1. Being desired or approved of. 2. Having the qualities required for a particular role. 3. Appropriate to a particular purpose. 4. Possessing or displaying moral virtue. 5. Giving pleasure.
1. In a good or satisfactory way.
Ēpros, Ēprosh, Ēpror, Ēproseth (an.) 1. (Substantive Animate) A terrifying and dangerous creature, especially if rare, mythical, or imaginary.

Ēs, Ēsh, Ēr, Ēseth (an.) 1. A small rodent that typically has a pointed snout, relatively large ears and eyes, and a long tail. 2. A rodent that resembles a large mouse, typically having a pointed snout and a long, sparsely-haired tail.
Ēstel, Mestelae, Mestelath (inan.) 1. A piece of land surrounded by water.

words.loc: Burrow, Warren, Tunnel, Hole, Lair, Den, Foxhole, Mousehole, Pantry, Larder, Storeroom
words.def: Ēzban, Ēzbanae, Ēzbanath (inan.) 1. A hole or tunnel dug by a small animal as a dwelling. 2. A room or large cupboard for storing food.
Ēzberi, Ēzberīs, Ēzberi, Ēzberīth (an.) 1. A carnivorous mammal with a pointed muzzle, bushy tail, and red or silver fur.

Ēnozdin, Ēnozdihūs, Ēnozdine, Ēzdin, Nozdino, Nozdined NOTE: This verb has no imperfect or preterite tense conjugations. Instead, the past habitual tense fulfills the role of the imperfect while the narrative tense fulfills the role of the preterite. 1. To decay by the action of bacteria and fungi.

Ī, Ae, Ath (inan.) NOTE: Can be used pronominally or as a determiner 1. (Interrogative) Asking for information specifying something, especially if semantically inanimate. 2. (Interrogative) Asking for information specifying one or more things from a set, especially if semantically inanimate.

words.loc: How Are You Doing, How's It Going, How Are Things, What's Up
words.def: Phatic Expression 1. An informal greeting or conversation opener that does not require a literal response.
1. (Postpositive) Used to connect nouns that are to be taken jointly.

words.loc: Sorry, I'm Sorry, Apologies, I Apologize
words.def: Phatic Expression NOTE: This is a contracted form of the phrase "nar in īndūgud." 1. (Literally) I cry for you. 2. (Apologetic Force) Used to express that someone feels bad for or sympathy for another entity. 3. (Apologetic Force) Used to express a regretful acknowlogment of an offense or failure.
Pronoun 1. Used by a speaker to refer to more than one addressee.

Ītor, Ītores, Ītor, Ītoreth (an.) 1. A place where ships load or unload and are moored, especially one where customs officers are stationed. 2. A place on the coast where vessels may find shelter, especially one protected from rough water by piers, jetties, and other artificial structures.

words.loc: Bad Death, Bad End, Violent Death, Killing, Doom, Annihilation, Oblivion, Ruin, Destruction, Overthrow
words.def: Īzil, Īzlae, Īzlath (inan.) 1. A particularly unpleasant, gruesome, or undesirable death. 2. The state of being completely destroyed. 3. A change that destroys or defeats something.

Īzlauk, Īzlaukh, Īzlauk, Īzlauketh (an.) Īzlauk, Īzlaukas, Īzlaukae, Īzlaukath (inan.) 1. Disturbing or horrifying due to an involvement or association with death and injury. 2. Causing replusion or horror, especially as a result of gore or death. 3. Involving or showing violence and bloodshed.
Īzlāsh, Īzlāshae, Īzlāshath (inan.) 1. A disastrous event, especially one involving great loss of life or injury. 2. An extremely cruel or horrific act.
1. In a way that involves extreme physical force, especially when it causes pain and suffering; in a violent manner.

words.loc: Name, Dub, Designate, Label, Call, Summon, Beckon, Shout, Cry
words.def: Sōr, Sōret, Sōrgūs, Ōr, Ōro, Ōrined 1. To give a name to. 2. To reach out with one's voice.
Ōril, Ōrilae, Ōrilath (inan.) 1. A word or set of words by which someone or something is known, addressed, or referred to.
Ōs, Ōrae, Ōrath (inan.) 1. The sound produced in a person's larynx and uttered through the mouth as speech or song.
Ōt, Ōtae, Ōtath (inan.) 1. A projectile consisting of a shaft, a point and a tail with stabilizing fins that is shot from a bow.
Ōzbar, Ōzbrae, Ōzbrath (inan.) 1. An organized group of people who sing together.
Ūgdos, Ugdorae, Ugdorath (inan.) 1. An area of low-lying land which is regularly flooded and typically remains waterlogged at all times.

Ūgdāsh, Ugdāshae, Ugdāshath (inan.) 1. Water or another liquid diffused in a small quantity as vapor, within a solid, or condensed on a surface. 2. Water vapor or another liquid in the air that settles upon surfaces, resulting in drops. (Especially used in reference to condensation on plants)

words.loc: Wet, Damp, Moist, Humid, Muggy, Raw, Common Cold, Influenza
words.def: Ūgud, Ūgdes, Ūged, Ūgudeth (an.) Ūged, Ūgdas, Ūgdae, Ūgudath (inan.) 1. Covered or saturated with water or another liquid. 2. (Of the air) Having a relatively high degree of water vapor. 3. (Of food) Uncooked. 4. (Substantive Inanimate) The common cold; An illness of the nose and mouth characterized by a running nose, sneezing, sniffling, coughing, a sore throat, and headaches. 5. (Substantive Inanimate) Influenza; A disease of the lungs, nose and throat characterized by a fever, chills, muscle aches, a running nose, coughing, a sore throat, headaches, and fatigue.
Ūk, Ūkis, Ūki, Ūkith (an.) 1. A man, woman, or child of the species Homo Sapiens. 2. A sentient being. (Typically, more specific terminology is used for non-humans, however)
